description: Course Description: Two five-week laboratory assignments in the research laboratories of Biophysics Graduate Group faculty members. Individual research problems with emphasis on methodological/procedural experience and experimental design.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): BPH 200 (can be concurrent).

title: Biological Nuclear Magnetic Resonance
description: Course Description: Principles and applications of magnetic resonance in biomedicine. Fundamental concepts and the biophysical basis for magnetic resonance applications in areas of tissue characterization/imaging, metabolic regulation, and cellular bioenergetics.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): MCB 221A; or consent of instructor, or the equivalent.

title: Membrane Biology
description: Course Description: Advanced topics on membrane biochemistry and biophysics. Relationship of the unique properties of biomembranes to their roles in cell biology and physiology.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): BIS 102; BIS 103; BIS 104; or consent of instructor.

title: Nanoscale Imaging for Molecular Medicine
description: Course Description: Current and emerging technologies to visualize biological structures and processes at size scales = 100 nanometers – and their application towards the advancement of molecular medicine. Technologies include superresolution optical microscopy electron microscopy and tomography. Emphasis on quantitative imaging.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): BIM 202 highly recommended; graduate standing.

title: Optical Methods in Biophysics
description: Course Description: Principal optical techniques used to study biological structures and their related functions. Specific optical techniques useful in the studies of protein-nucleic acid, protein-membrane and protein-protein interactions. Biomedical applications of optical techniques.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): BIS 102; EAD 108B; CHE 110A; or equivalents.

title: Living Matter: Physical Biology of the Cell
description: Course Description: Introduction to the origin, maintenance, and regulation of the dynamic architecture of the cell, including cellular modes of organization, dynamics and energy dissipation, molecular transport, motility, regulation, and adaptability.

title: Biophysics Seminar
description: Course Description: Presentation of current research by experts in biophysics.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing or consent of instructor.

title: Research Conference in Biophysics
description: Course Description: Presentation and discussion of faculty and graduate-student research in biophysics.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): BPH 299 (can be concurrent); graduate standing in Biophysics and/or consent of instructor.

title: Introduction to Research Topics
description: Course Description: Presentation of current research activities of the Biophysics Graduate Group faculty. Facilitation of students in developing their research interest, and promoting collegial interactions.

title: Group Study
description: Course Description: Group study.

title: Research
description: Course Description: Research.

title: Current Topics in Biophotonics & Bioimaging Research
description: Course Description: Designed to help graduate students develop and maintain familiarity with the current and past literature in the field of Biophotonics and Bioimaging research and related areas.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Biophotonics Seminar
description: Course Description: Presentation of current research in the area of biophotonics by experts in the field, followed by group discussions.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing or consent of instructor.

title: Biostatistics: Survival Analysis
description: Course Description: Incomplete data; life tables; nonparametric methods; parametric methods; accelerated failure time models; proportional hazards models; partial likelihood; advanced topics.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): STA 131C.

title: Biostatistics: Generalized Linear Models
description: Course Description: Likelihood and linear regression; generalized linear model; Binomial regression; case-control studies; dose-response and bioassay; Poisson regression; Gamma regression; quasi-likelihood models; estimating equations; multivariate GLMs.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): STA 131C.

title: Analysis of Longitudinal Data
description: Course Description: Standard and advanced methodology, theory, algorithms, and applications relevant for analysis of repeated measurements and longitudinal data in biostatistical and statistical settings.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (BST 222 or STA 222); (BST 223 or STA 223); STA 232B; or consent of instructor.

title: Clinical Trials
description: Course Description: Basic statistical principles of clinical designs, including bias, randomization, blocking, and masking. Practical applications of widely-used designs, including dose-finding, comparative and cluster randomization designs. Advanced statistical procedures for analysis of data collected in clinical trials.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): BST 223 or STA 223; or consent of instructor.

title: Statistical Methods for Bioinformatics
description: Course Description: Standard and advanced statistical methodology, theory, algorithms, and applications relevant to the analysis of -omics data.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): BST 131C or consent of instructor; data analysis experience recommended.

title: Machine Learning in Genomics
description: Course Description: Emerging problems in molecular biology and current machine learning-based solutions to those problem. How deep learning, kernel methods, graphical models, feature selection, non-parametric models and other techniques can be applied to application areas such as gene editing, gene network inference and analysis, chromatin state inference, cancer genomics and single cell genomics.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): STA 208 or ECS 171; or consent of instructor.

title: Advanced Topics in Biostatistics
description: Course Description: Biostatistical methods and models selected from the following: genetics, bioinformatics and genomics;longitudinal or functional data; clinical trials and experimental design; analysis of environmental data; dose-response, nutrition and toxicology; survival analysis; observational studies and epidemiology; computer-intensive or Bayesian methods in biostatistics.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): BST 222; BST 223.

title: Seminar in Biostatistics
description: Course Description: Seminar on advanced topics in the field of biostatistics. Presented by members of the Biostatistics Graduate Group and other guest speakers.

title: Directed Group Study
description: Course Description: Special topics in Biostatistics appropriate for group study at the graduate level.

title: Special Study for Biostat Graduate Students
description: Course Description: Special topics in Biostatistics appropriate for directed individual study on advanced topics not otherwise covered in the Biostatistics curriculum.

title: Dissertation Research
description: Course Description: Research in Biostatistics under the supervision of major professor.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor; advancement to Candidacy for Ph.D.

title: Internship in Cardiology
description: Course Description: Supervised work experience in cardiology.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Upper division standing; approval of project by preceptor prior to internship.

title: Cardiology Research
description: Course Description: Special study by individual arrangement in cardiovascular medicine. Work will include directed readings, laboratory and discussions.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Basic Science in Cardiology
description: Course Description: Fundamentals underlying cardiovascular medicine. Including hemodynamics, neural control of the circulation, biochemistry and some experimental design and statistics. Experts in each of these fields will give current information in their areas.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate or medical student status.

title: Cardiology Research
description: Course Description: Research or special studies.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Clinical Cardiology Clerkship: Kaiser
description: Course Description: Emphasis placed on history taking and physical examination of pediatric and adult patients with congenital and acquired cardiovascular disease. Hospital rounds in CCU and elsewhere. Roles of ECG, PCG, and cardiac fluoroscopy, etc., in office cardiology will be evaluated.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Third- and fourth-year medical students with advance approval by Division of Cardiology.

title: Cardiology Clinical Clerkship
description: Course Description: Participation with members of subspecialty consultation service in initial clinical evaluation, work-up, management, and follow-up of patients with cardiologic disorders. Two outpatient clinics per week.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): IMD 430; third- and fourth-year medical students in good academic standing with consent of instructor.

title: Management of Coronary Artery Disease: Coronary Care Unit
description: Course Description: Research in laboratory and exercise testing to be determined by instructor. Current methods of clinical research involving certain aspects of diagnosis and treatment.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Completion of second-year of medical school and advance approval by Division of Cardiology.

title: Management of Coronary Artery Disease (CICU)
description: Course Description: Research in laboratory and exercise testing to be determined by instructor. Current methods of clinical research involving certain aspects of diagnosis and treatment.

title: Preventive Cardiology
description: Course Description: Clinical experience, weekly seminar and reading on primary and secondary prevention of cardiovascular disease. Will be carried out in Lipid and Hypertension Clinics, Exercise Laboratory, Cardiac Care Unit, Cardiac Catheterization, and Cardiac Surgery services.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor; completion of third-year medical school.

title: Insights in Cardiology
description: Course Description: Students attend one or more cardiovascular medicine clinics: general, hypertension, arrhythmia. Introduction to the diagnosis/treatment of common cardiovascular problems.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Medical student in good academic standing and approval by Division of Cardiology.

title: Gender Specific Medicine SSM
description: Course Description: Special Studies Module, a four-week course on the topic Basic Science Principles Relating to Gender Specific Medicine.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Special Group Study: EKG Unit
description: Course Description: Special group study in cardiology for medical students in EKG unit. May include lectures, directed reading, and/or discussion groups.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Medical student with advance approval by monthly attending faculty.

title: Research
description: Course Description: Research.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor; approval by Division of Cardiology.

title: Introduction to Film Studies
description: Course Description: Analysis of film form and narrative, including cinematography, editing, and sound. Issues in film studies including authorship, stardom, race, gender, class, and cultural identity. Introduction to selected cinematic movements and national film traditions.

title: Introduction to Technocultural Studies
description: Course Description: Contemporary developments in the fine and performing arts, media arts, digital arts, and literature as they relate to technological and scientific practices.

title: Media Archaeology
description: Course Description: Evolution of media technologies and practices beginning in the 19th century as they relate to contemporary digital arts practices. Special focus on the reconstruction of the social and artistic possibilities of lost and obsolete media technologies.

title: Introduction to Media Computation
description: Course Description: Introduction to key computational ideas necessary to understand and produce digital media. Fundamentals of programming and analysis of how media are represented and transmitted in digital form. Aimed primarily at non-computer science students.

title: Filmmaking Foundations
description: Course Description: Introduction to filmmaking concepts, principles, and methods. Emphasis on form, content and historical dialectic between classical narrative filmmaking conventions and artists' challenges to these conventions.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CDM 001 and/or CDM 003 recommended.

title: Filmmaking Foundations
description: Course Description: Introduction to filmmaking concepts, principles, and methods. Emphasis on form, content and historical dialectic between classical narrative filmmaking conventions and artists' challenges to these conventions.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CDM 001 and/or CDM 003 recommended.

title: Media History 1: Gutenberg to Oppenheimer
description: Course Description: History of media to 1945, with particular focus on mechanically reproduced mass media technologies including the printing press, the newspaper, photography, cinema, radio and early computing technology. Analysis of interrelated cultural and political topics.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CDM 002 or CDM 003 recommended.

title: Media History 2: 1945-Present
description: Course Description: History of media from 1945 to present, with particular focus on the development of the computer, digital network and internet technologies in the context of other media infrastructures like radio, television and satellite networks.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CDM 002 or CDM 003 recommended.

title: History of Cinema from 1895-1945
description: Course Description: Examination of the cultural context of the emergence of cinema. Discussion of cinema as a product of the age of industrialization and conquest, as well as an element of urban culture, and mass transportation.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CDM 001 recommended.

title: History of Cinema from 1945-Present
description: Course Description: Examines cinema from 1945 through the present, with focus on neo-realist, new wave and third-world movements. Examines social critique in cinema from studio system to New Wave, Cine Novo, postcolonial cinema and contemporary independent cinema.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CDM 001 recommended.

title: History of Cinema from 1945-Present
description: Course Description: Examines cinema from 1945 through the present, with focus on neo-realist, new wave and third-world movements. Examines social critique in cinema from studio system to New Wave, Cine Novo, postcolonial cinema and contemporary independent cinema.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CDM 001 recommended.

title: Introduction to Games
description: Course Description: Introduction to the history, theory, and practice of play. Survey of both analog and digital games. Overview of gaming cultures, aesthetics, industries, and technologies.

title: Internship
description: Course Description: Supervised internship, on or off campus, in the area of cinema and digital media.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Directed Group Study
description: Course Description: Directed group study in cinema and digital media.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Special Study for Undergraduates
description: Course Description: Special study for undergraduates in cinema and digital media.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Experimental Digital Cinema I
description: Course Description: Experimental approaches to the making of film and video in the age of digital technologies. Builds upon the foundation provided by CDM 020. Instruction in technical, conceptual, creative, and critical skills for taking a project from idea to fruition.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CDM 020 or ART 012; or consent of instructor.

title: Experimental Digital Cinema II
description: Course Description: Continuation of CDM 100 with further exploration of digital cinema creation. Additional topics include new modes of distribution, streaming, installation and exhibition.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CDM 100; consent of instructor.

title: Interactivity & Animation
description: Course Description: Fundamentals of creating interactive screen-based work. Theories of interactivity, linear versus non-linear structures & audience involvement and participation. Use of digital production tools to produce class projects.

title: Documentary Production
description: Course Description: Traditional and new forms of documentary, with focus on technocultural issues. Skills and strategies for producing work in various media. Progression through all stages of production, from conception through post-production to critique.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CDM 020; CDM 155; TCS 155; or consent of instructor.

title: Feminist Media Production
description: Course Description: Media production as a mode of cultural criticism, furthering feminist and social justice goals. Fundamentals of camera, editing and distribution via a social engagement model. Study and hands-on response to key historic and contemporary feminist and social justice media discourses.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (CTS 020 or CDM 020); or two WMS courses.

title: Acting for Camera
description: Course Description: Analysis and practice of acting skills required for camera work and digital media.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Community Media Production
description: Course Description: Use of video and new media tools to address social issues among neighborhood and community groups. Use basic video, sound, and lighting techniques while working with local groups in a group video project.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CDM 020 recommended.

title: Community Networks & Social Media
description: Course Description: Impact and implications of computer-based networks in community, civic, and social life. Subjects may include community-access computer sites, neighborhood wireless networks, the digital divide, open-source software, and citizen action.

title: Introduction to Sonic Arts
description: Course Description: Introduction to the use of sound within the arts. Techniques and aesthetics of experimental contemporary practices. Creation of original sound works.

title: Introduction to Sonic Arts
description: Course Description: Introduction to use of sound within the arts. Techniques and aesthetics of experimental contemporary practices. Creation of original sound works.

title: Intermediate Sonic Arts
description: Course Description: Techniques of recording, editing, mixing, and synthesis to combine voice, field recordings, and electronic signals. Incorporating live, recorded, found sounds to create multidimensional stories. Presentation of live performances, etc.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CDM 121 or CDM 121V; or consent of instructor.

title: Intermediate Sonic Arts
description: Course Description: Techniques of recording, editing, mixing, and synthesis to combine voice, field recordings, and electronic signals. Incorporating live, recorded, found sounds to create multidimensional stories. Presentation of live performances, etc.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CDM 121 or CDM 121V; or consent of instructor.

title: Sight & Soundtrack
description: Course Description: Use of sound to articulate, lend mood or subconsciously underscore visual, environmental, or performative situations, combining music, voice, sound effects and other noises to create sound designs that enhance, alter or support action and movement.

title: Advanced Sound: Performance & Improvisation
description: Course Description: Culmination of CDM sound courses. Focuses on performance and improvisation, culminating in a final public performance. Expectation of extensive reading and rehearsal outside of class time.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (CDM 121 or CDM 121V); (CDM 122 or CDM 122V); or consent of instructor.

title: Fundamentals of Computer Graphics
description: Course Description: Foundation course teaches the theory of three-dimensional computer graphics, including modeling, rendering and animation. Development of practical skills through the use of professional software to create computer graphics.

title: Character Animation
description: Course Description: The art of character animation in three dimensional computer animation. Movement theory, principles of animation, animation timing. Development of technical and practical skills.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CDM 130 or TCS 130.

title: Object-Oriented Programming for Artists
description: Course Description: Introduction to object-oriented programming for artists. Focus on understanding the metaphors and potential of object-oriented programming for sound, video, performance, and interactive installations.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CDM 002 recommended.

title: Electronics for Artists
description: Course Description: Creative application of electronic technology relevant to media and fine arts involving both electronic principles and hands-on application.

title: Topics in Virtuality
description: Course Description: Social, political, economic, and aesthetic factors in virtual reality. Artificial environments, telepresence, and simulated experience. Focus on contemporary artists' work and writing.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CDM 002 recommended.

title: Histories of Animation
description: Course Description: Introductory survey of animation history. Reading, writing, and discussion about animation.

title: Where Do Films Go: Film Distribution
description: Course Description: Hands-on, experiential introduction to independent film distribution. Post-production stages. Current and evolving routes filmmakers use to reach audience.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CDM 020 or CDM 020V or ART 012; or consent of instructor.

title: Media Theory
description: Course Description: Critical and theoretical approaches to the emergence of new technologies since the invention of photography. Examine various approaches to media; formalist, semiotic, structuralist, Frankfurt School, cybernetics, visual and gamer theory.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CDM 002 or CDM 003 recommended.

title: Epic Television: The Golden Age of TV? Sopranos, Wire, Girls, Walking Dead
description: Course Description: Critically celebrated scripted television since the mid-1990s. Key themes including class, ethnicity, race, violence, and US politics. Major developments in the medium’s history as context for recent wave of epic television.

title: Technology & the Modern American Body
description: Course Description: History and analysis of relationships between human bodies and technologies in modern society. Dominant and eccentric examples of how human bodies and technologies influence one another and reveal underlying cultural assumptions.

title: Surveillance Media
description: Course Description: Surveillance technologies and media surveillance in daily life. Topics include CCTV, facial recognition, tracking systems, biosensors, data mining, encryption, and AI. Analysis of laws, security measures, and counter-surveillance practices.

title: Art & Cinema: Between the White Cube & the Black Box
description: Course Description: Current debates between cinema studies and contemporary art. Issues covered include, experimental modes of filming, montaging, installing, screening, and displaying images between the White Cube (gallery/museum) and the Black Box (cinema).

title: Art & Cinema: Between the White Cube & the Black Box
description: Course Description: Current debates between cinema studies and contemporary art. Experimental modes of filming, montaging, installing, screening, and displaying images between the White Cube (gallery/museum) and the Black Box (cinema).

title: Nazi & Fascist Cinema: Film & other Visual Media
description: Course Description: Analysis of nefarious and noxious cultural products in history: films made under the Nazis and other fascists, 1933-1945. Questions at heart of humanistic studies: relationship of culture to propaganda, politics, and even unfathomable crime.

title: Human Rights in Film & Television
description: Course Description: Human rights depictions in film and on television, including topics: genocide (Rwanda, Armenia, Indonesia), political persecution (Guatemala), carceral abuses (U.S.), human trafficking (Europe), persecution for sexual preference (Cuba). Documenting abuse as well as remedying it.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Topics in U.S. Film History
description: Course Description: Study of an aspect of American film history (such as the silent era; the studio system; U.S. avant-garde cinema), including the influences of technological, economic, regulatory, cultural, and artistic forces.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CDM 001; or consent of instructor.

title: Topics in Film Genres
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Study of one or more of the film genres (such as musicals, film noir, screwball comedy, or westerns), including genre theory and the relationship of the genre(s) to culture, history, and film industry practices.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CDM 001; or consent of instructor.

title: Narrative Game Design
description: Course Description: Narrative game design. Character, dialogue, environmental storytelling, procedurally-generated text, play as narrative. Workshop, critique, and publish narrative and text-based games.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 072 or CDM 072 or ENL 172 or CDM 172 or STS 172; or consent of instructor.

title: Game Studies Seminar
description: Course Description: Theory and methods for researching games, play, media, and culture. Reading, writing, and discussion about playable media.

title: Video Games & Culture
description: Course Description: Critical approaches to the study of video games, focusing on formal, historical, and cultural modes of analysis. History of software and hardware in North American and global contexts. Relations of games to society, politics, economics, media, etc.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CDM 072 or ENL 072 recommended.

title: Introduction to Analog Game Design
description: Course Description: Fundamental theories and practices of analog game design. Pen & paper, dice & cards, checkers & chess to riddles & stories, track & field, and other forms of play. Prototype, playtest, and presentation of original analog games.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CDM 072 or CDM 172 or CTS 172; or consent of instructor.

title: Special Topics in Analog Game Design
description: Course Description: Special topics in analog game design for upper division students.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CDM 072 or CDM 172 or CTS 172; or consent of instructor; Recommended: CDM 173, CDM 175 or CDM 177.

title: Introduction to Digital Game Development
description: Course Description: Digital game development. Theories of control, interface, game feel, glitch, mechanics. Design, demo, and distribution of new digital games.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CDM 072 or CDM 172 or CTS 172; consent of instructor.

title: Special Topics in Digital Game Development
description: Course Description: Special topics in digital game development for upper division students.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CDM 072 or CDM 172 or CTS 172; or consent of instructor. Recommended: CDM 173, CDM 175 or CDM 177.

title: Introduction to Game Programming
description: Course Description: Introduction to computer science concepts and programming practices for video games.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CDM 072 or CDM 172 or CTS 172; or consent of instructor.

title: Special Topics in Game Programming
description: Course Description: Special topics in game programming for upper division students.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CDM 072 or CDM 172 or CTS 172; or consent of instructor. Recommended: CDM 173, CDM 175 or CDM 177.

title: Special Topics in Cinema & Digital Media
description: Course Description: Special topics in cinema & digital media.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CDM 001 or CDM 002 recommended.

title: Research Methods in Cinema & Digital Media
description: Course Description: Introduction to basic research methods for Cinema and Digital Media; e.g., electronic & archived images, sounds & data, etc.

title: Honors Project in Cinema & Digital Media
description: Course Description: Directed reading, research and writing culminating in the completion of a senior honors thesis or significant creative project under direction of faculty sponsor.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor; GPA of at least 3.500; senior standing.

title: Honors Project in Cinema & Digital Media
description: Course Description: Directed reading, research and writing culminating in the completion of a senior honors thesis or significant creative project under direction of faculty sponsor.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor; GPA of at least 3.500; senior standing.

title: Tutoring in Cinema & Digital Media
description: Course Description: Leading small voluntary discussion groups affiliated with departmental courses under the supervision of the course instructor.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor. Consent of Department Chair.

title: Directed Group Study
description: Course Description: Directed group study in cinema and digital media. For students with upper division standing. May be taught abroad.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Special Study for Advanced Undergraduates
description: Course Description: Special study in cinema and digital media. For advanced undergraduates with upper division standing. May be taught abroad.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Teaching Assistant Training Practicum
description: Course Description: Teaching assistant training practicum.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor; graduate standing.

title: Introduction to Cognitive Science
description: Course Description: Introduction to the interdisciplinary cognitive scientific approach to the study of mind, drawing concepts and methods from psychology, philosophy, linguistics, artificial intelligence, and other disciplines.

title: Directed Group Study
description: Course Description: Directed group study in cognitive science.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Special Study for Lower Division Students
description: Course Description: Special study for lower division students.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Neuroeconomics/Reinforcement Learning & Decision Making
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Theoretical and empirical approaches to neuroeconomics (neuroscience of decision making) from psychology, neuroscience, economics, and computer science. Neuroscience of judgment and decision making, behavioral economics, and reinforcement learning.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 100 or PSC 100Y or PSC 135 or ECN 100A or ECN 100AV or ARE 100A or NPB 162 or NPB 163); (STA 013 or STA 013Y or STA 100 or PSC 103A); or consent of instructor.

title: Computational Cognitive Neuroscience
description: Course Description: Explorations of how brain secretes mind, via computer simulations that are manipulated and probed to investigate how neurons produce perception, attention, memory, language, and cognitive control.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V); PSC 100 or PSC 101 or PSC 135 recommended.

title: Consciousness & Cognition
description: Course Description: Current theoretical and empirical evidence in the study of cognition and consciousness. Theories of consciousness, psychological and neural basis of conscious and unconscious processes such as attention, intentionality, and dreams.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V); (PSC 041 or PSC 041V); (PSC 100 or PSC 100Y or PSC 135).

title: Seminar in Cognitive Science
description: Course Description: Intensive treatment of a special topic or problem related to cognitive science.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Fieldwork in Cognitive Science
description: Course Description: Supervised internship off and on campus, in community and institutional settings.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Special Study for Honors Students
description: Course Description: Directed research. Supervised reading, research and writing leading to submission of a Senior Honors thesis under the direction of faculty sponsor.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor. Senior standing in Cognitive Science; qualifications for admission into college honors program.

title: Special Study for Honors Students
description: Course Description: Directed research. Supervised reading, research and writing leading to submission of a Senior Honors thesis under the direction of faculty sponsor.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor. Senior standing in Cognitive Science; qualifications for admission into college honors program.

title: Directed Group Study
description: Course Description: Directed group study in cognitive science.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Special Study for Advanced Undergraduates
description: Course Description: Special study for advanced undergraduates.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Human Gross Anatomy
description: Course Description: Detailed study of the gross anatomical structure of the human body, with emphasis on function and clinical relevance to students entering health care professions.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): BIS 002A; concurrent enrollment in EXB 106L or CHA 101L strongly recommended.

title: Human Gross Anatomy Laboratory
description: Course Description: Detailed study of prosected human cadavers in small group format with extensive hands-on experience.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): BIS 002A; (EXB 106 (can be concurrent) or CHA 101 (can be concurrent)); if EXB 106 or CHA 101 is not taken concurrently, it must have been already completed.

title: Human Microscopic Anatomy: Structure & Function of Human Tissues & Organ Systems
description: Course Description: Complements Gross Anatomy by extending the study of structure to the microscopic level. Shows how cells are assembled into tissues, and tissues into organs, with an emphasis on demonstrating how microscopic structure explains function.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): BIS 104.

title: Human Clinical Neuroanatomy
description: Course Description: Clinically relevant anatomy of the normal human nervous system, including external and internal anatomy of the brain, spinal cord, and cranial nerves. Blood supply to the brain and spinal cord. Functional neuroanatomy of motor, sensory, and cognitive systems. Application of neuroanatomical principles relevant to clinical problem solving for students entering health care professions.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHA 101; or consent of instructor.

title: Advanced Human Gross Anatomy
description: Course Description: Clinically relevant advanced human gross anatomy and anatomical variation. Detailed human cadaver dissection, close reading and analysis of anatomical variation research literature, and intensive study of anatomical structures and relationships.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (CHA 101 C- or better or EXB 106 C- or better); (CHA 101L C- or better or EXB 106L C- or better); consent of instructor.

title: Internship in Morphology
description: Course Description: Experience of supervised internship in research laboratories of members of the department.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Upper division standing; laboratory science experience including some chemistry; approval of project by preceptor prior to period of internship.

title: Tutoring in Cell Biology & Human Anatomy
description: Course Description: Provides laboratory instruction in gross and microscopic human anatomy, with small groups of undergraduates under the supervision of the instructor.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHA 101 B or better; and consent of instructor.

title: Directed Group Study
description: Course Description: Directed reading, discussion, and/or laboratory experience on selected topics.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Special Study for Advanced Undergraduates
description: Course Description: Special study for advanced undergraduates.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Microscopic Anatomy for Researchers
description: Course Description: The growing importance of the use of gene knock-out studies and imaging technology requires significant understanding of basic anatomy. Designed to familiarize students in diverse fields with anatomical, cellular and tissue organization of typical animal models.

title: Neurobiology
description: Course Description: Physiology and anatomy of the normal human nervous system in an integrated format.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor. Two upper division or one graduate course in Neurobiology.

title: Seminar
description: Course Description: Seminar.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Research Group Conference
description: Course Description: Discussion of problems, progress and literature groups relevant to current research undertaken by laboratory groups Human Anatomy.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor. Graduate student with research experience (may be taken concurrently).

title: Advanced Group Study
description: Course Description: Advanced group study.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Research
description: Course Description: Research.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Medical Neuroanatomy
description: Course Description: Anatomy of the normal human nervous system, to include gross external and internal morphology of brain and spinal cord, and function neuroanatomy of motor, sensory and cognitive systems. Incorporates application of neuroanatomy to clinical problem solving.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHA 400; Block 1.

title: Anatomy Medical Education Special Study Module
description: Course Description: Attend all of the lectures and laboratory sessions for CHA 400 and CHA 402 during the four-week section; approximately seven anatomy labs and three-four histology labs. Tutor first-year students during the laboratory sessions. Prepare and present a clinical correlate session.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor. UC Davis School of Medicine students only.

title: Tutoring in Human Anatomy
description: Course Description: Assist instructor by tutoring medical students in preparation for one of the departmental courses that is a component of the required curriculum for the School of Medicine.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Advanced standing or consent of instructor.

title: Advanced Group Study
description: Course Description: Directed reading and group discussions and/or laboratory experience on selected topics.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Medical students, interns, and residents with consent of instructor.

title: Research
description: Course Description: Research.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Preparation for General Chemistry
description: Course Description: Preparation for the general chemistry series (CHE 002ABC & CHE 004ABC); unit conversion, dimensional analysis, periodic table, chemical equations, stoichiometry, and gases.

title: Preparation for General Chemistry
description: Course Description: Preparation for the general chemistry series (CHE 002ABC & CHE 004ABC); unit conversion, dimensional analysis, periodic table, chemical equations, stoichiometry, and gases.

title: General Chemistry
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Periodic table, stoichiometry, chemical equations, physical properties and kinetic theory of gases, chemical equilibrium, acids and bases. Laboratory experiments in stoichiometric relations, properties and collection of gases, atomic spectroscopy, introductory quantitative analysis and acids and bases.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): High school chemistry and physics, and concurrent enrollment in mathematics at or above the level of MAT 012 strongly recommended; must earn a qualifying score of 24 or higher on the Chemistry Placement Exam or complete CHE 001 or CHE 001V with a C- or higher; more information about the Chemistry Placement Requirements can be found at https://chemistry.ucdavis.edu/undergraduate/general-chemistry-series/chemistry-placement-requirements/0000.

title: Honors General Chemistry
description: Course Description: Limited enrollment course with a more rigorous treatment of material covered in CHE 002A. Students completing CHE 002AH can continue with CHE 002BH or CHE 002B.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor; high school chemistry and physics, and concurrent enrollment in mathematics at or above the level of MAT 012 strongly recommended; must earn a qualifying score of 33 or better on the Chemistry Placement Exam; more information about the Chemistry Placement Requirements to ensure enrollment in CHE 002A can be found at https://chemistry.ucdavis.edu/undergraduate/general-chemistry-series/chemistry-placement-requirements.

title: General Chemistry
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Continuation of CHE 002A. Thermodynamics, atomic and molecular structure and chemical bonding, condensed phases and intermolecular forces, solubility. Laboratory experiments in thermochemistry, equilibria, and quantitative analysis using volumetric methods.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 002A C- or better.

title: Honors General Chemistry
description: Course Description: Limited enrollment course with a more rigorous treatment of material covered in CHE 002B. Students completing CHE 002BH can continue with CHE 002CH or CHE 002C.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 002A or CHE 002AH C or better; MAT 021B (can be concurrent); or consent of instructor; CHE 002A with consent of instructor.

title: General Chemistry
description: Course Description: Continuation of CHE 002B. Kinetics, electrochemistry, spectroscopy, structure and bonding in transition metal compounds, application of principles to chemical reactions. Laboratory experiments in selected analytical methods and syntheses.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 002B C- or better or CHE 002BH C- or better or CHE 004B C- or better.

title: Honors General Chemistry
description: Course Description: Limited enrollment course with a more rigorous treatment of material covered in CHE 002C.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 002B or CHE 002BH C or better; MAT 021C (can be concurrent); or consent of instructor; CHE 002B with consent of instructor.

title: Chemistry for Life Sciences: Determining Structure & Predicting Properties
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Integrated General and Organic Chemistry intended for majors in the life sciences. Core concepts of chemical composition, structure and properties. Includes phase changes, separation methods, composition, spectroscopy, atomic & molecular structure, periodicity, bonding, charge distribution, intermolecular forces, and physical properties.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): High school chemistry and physics strongly recommended; satisfactory score on the Chemistry and Mathematics Placement Examinations or satisfactory completion of the ALEKS Summer Chemistry Prep Course; a satisfactory grade in WKL 041C ('P' or 'C' or better) will suffice in lieu of a satisfactory Chemistry Placement Examination score.

title: Chemistry for Life Sciences: Predicting & Characterizing Chemical Change
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Continuation of CHE 003A covering core concepts of characterization of chemical processes and predicting chemical changes. Includes modeling chemical reactions, understanding proportions/stoichiometry, tracking energy, activation energy, reaction kinetics, thermodynamics, and equilibrium.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 003A C- or better; note: C- or better in CHE 002A or 002AH does not satisfy the prerequisite requirement.

title: Chemistry for Life Sciences: Controlling Processes & Synthetic Pathways
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Continuation of CHE 003B covering core concepts of harnessing energy, controlling reaction extent, and organic chemistry synthetic pathways. Includes acids and bases, thermodynamics, chemical equilibria, organic chemistry terminology and mechanisms.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 003B C- or better; note: C- or better in CHE 002B or 002BH does not satisfy the prerequisite requirement.

title: General Chemistry for the Physical Sciences & Engineering
description: Course Description: General chemistry course with a more rigorous treatment of material covered in CHE 002A, intended for students majoring in the physical sciences and engineering.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): MAT 021A (can be concurrent); must earn a qualifying score of 28 or higher on the Chemistry Placement Exam or complete CHE 001 or CHE 001V with a C- or higher; high school chemistry and physics strongly recommended; more information about the Chemistry Placement Requirements can be found at https://chemistry.ucdavis.edu/undergraduate/general-chemistry-series/chemistry-placement-requirements/0000.

title: General Chemistry for the Physical Sciences & Engineering
description: Course Description: General chemistry course with a more rigorous treatment of material covered in CHE 002B, intended for students majoring in the physical sciences and engineering.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): MAT 021B (can be concurrent); (CHE 004A C- or better or CHE 002AH C- or better); or consent of instructor.

title: General Chemistry for the Physical Sciences & Engineering
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: General chemistry course with a more rigorous treatment of material covered in CHE 002C, intended for students majoring in the physical sciences and engineering.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): MAT 021C (can be concurrent); (CHE 004B C- or better or CHE 002B B or better or CHE 002BH B or better); or consent of instructor.

title: Organic Chemistry: Brief Course
description: Course Description: With CHE 008B, an introduction to the nomenclature, structure, chemistry, and reaction mechanisms of organic compounds. Intended for students majoring in areas other than organic chemistry.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 002B C- or better or CHE 002BH C- or better or CHE 004B C- or better.

title: Organic Chemistry: Brief Course
description: Course Description: Laboratory concerned primarily with organic laboratory techniques and the chemistry of the common classes of organic compounds. Lecture portion a continuation of CHE 008A.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 008A or CHE 118A or CHE 128A.

title: Concept of Chemistry
description: Course Description: Survey of basic concepts and contemporary applications of chemistry. Designed for non-science majors and not as preparation for CHE 002A.

title: Directed Group Study
description: Course Description: Primarily for lower division students.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Special Study for Undergraduates
description: Course Description: Special study for undergraduates.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Environmental Water Chemistry
description: Course Description: Practical aspects of water chemistry in the environment, including thermodynamic relations, coordination chemistry, solubility calculations, redox reactions and rate laws. Computer modeling of the evolution in water chemistry from contact with minerals and gases.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 002C or CHE 002CH or CHE 004C.

title: Chemistry for Life Sciences: Determining Organic Structures & Properties
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Continuation of PHE 003C. Core concepts of organic structure, nomenclature, functional groups, organic acids and bases, resonance and delocalization, aromaticity, intermolecular forces, three-dimensional structure and conformational analysis, spectroscopy.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 002C C- or better or CHE 002CH C- or better; (CHE 008A or CHE 118A or CHE 128A).

title: Chemistry for Life Sciences: Predicting & Controlling Organic Pathways
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Continuation of CHE 103A. Core concepts of functional group transformations, synthesis, mechanisms, sustainable chemistry, structure and function of biomolecules, organic reactions in biological systems, molecular design, detection, separation, and identification of organic molecules.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 103A C- or better.

title: Forensic Applications of Analytical Chemistry
description: Course Description: Theory and application of standard methods of chemical analysis to evidentiary samples. Use and evaluation of results from screening tests, FTIR, GC and GCMS to various sample types encountered in forensics.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 002C or CHE 002CH or CHE 004C.

title: Physical Chemistry for the Life Sciences
description: Course Description: Physical chemistry intended for majors in the life science area. Introductory development of classical and statistical thermodynamics including equilibrium processes and solutions of both non-electrolytes and electrolytes. The thermodynamic basis of electrochemistry and membrane potentials.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (CHE 002C or CHE 002CH or CHE 004C); (MAT 016C or MAT 017C or MAT 021C); (PHY 007C or PHY 009C or PHY 009HC).

title: Physical Chemistry for the Life Sciences
description: Course Description: Continuation of CHE 107A. Kinetic theory of gases and transport processes in liquids. Chemical kinetics, enzyme kinetics and theories of reaction rates. Introduction to quantum theory, atomic and molecular structure, and spectroscopy. Application to problems in the biological sciences.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 107A.

title: Molecular Biochemistry
description: Course Description: Chemical principles and experimental methods applied to the biological sciences to understand the molecular structure and function of proteins, nucleic acids, carbohydrates, and membrane lipids.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 128C or CHE 118C.

title: Physical Chemistry: Introduction to Quantum Mechanics
description: Course Description: Introduction to the postulates and general principles of quantum mechanics. Approximations based on variational method and time independent perturbation theory. Application to harmonic oscillator, rigid rotor, one-electron and many-electron atoms, and homo-and hetero-nuclear diatomic molecules.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PHY 007C or PHY 009C or PHY 009HC); (CHE 002C or CHE 002CH or CHE 004C); (MAT 016C or MAT 017C or MAT 021C); completion of MAT 021D, MAT 022A, MAT 022AL; PHY 009C or PHY 009HC, strongly recommended.

title: Physical Chemistry: Properties of Atoms & Molecules
description: Course Description: Group theory. Application of quantum mechanics to polyatomic molecules and molecular spectroscopy. Intermolecular forces and the gas, liquid and solid states. Distributions, ensembles and partition functions. Transport properties.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 110A.

title: Physical Chemistry: Thermodynamics, Equilibria & Kinetics
description: Course Description: Development and application of the general principles of thermodynamics and statistical thermodynamics. Chemical kinetics, rate laws for chemical reactions and reaction mechanisms.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 110B.

title: Instrumental Analysis
description: Course Description: Intermediate theory and laboratory techniques in analytical and physical chemistry. Advanced data analysis methods and goodness-of-fit criteria. Fourier-transform spectroscopic methods and instrumentation. Mass spectrometry. Electrochemistry. Liquid chromatography.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 105; (CHE 110B (can be concurrent) or (CHE 107A, CHE 107B)).

title: Organic Chemistry for Health & Life Sciences
description: Course Description: The 118A-C series is for students planning professional school studies in health and life sciences. Rigorous, in-depth presentation of basic principles with emphasis on stereochemistry and spectroscopy and preparations and reactions of nonaromatic hydrocarbons, haloalkanes, alcohols and ethers. May be taught abroad.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 002C C- or better or CHE 002CH C- or better or CHE 004C C- or better.

title: Organic Chemistry for Health & Life Sciences
description: Course Description: Continuation of CHE 118A, with emphasis on spectroscopy and the preparation and reactions of aromatic hydrocarbons, organometallic compounds, aldehydes and ketones.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 118A or CHE 128A.

title: Organic Chemistry for Health & Life Sciences
description: Course Description: Continuation of CHE 118B, with emphasis on the preparation, reactions and identification of carboxylic acids and their derivatives, alkyl and acyl amines, ß-dicarbonyl compounds, and various classes of naturally occurring, biologically important compounds.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 118B or (CHE 128B, CHE 129A).

title: Introduction to Molecular Structure & Spectra
description: Course Description: Modern theoretical and experimental methods used to study problems of molecular structure and bonding; emphasis on spectroscopic techniques.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 110B.

title: Chemistry of Nanoparticles
description: Course Description: Chemical and physical aspects of inorganic nanoparticles. Topics include synthesis, structure, colloidal behavior, catalytic activity, size and shape dependency of physical properties, analytical methods and applications.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 110C (can be concurrent) or CHE 107B (can be concurrent).

title: Inorganic Chemistry: Fundamentals
description: Course Description: Symmetry, molecular geometry and structure, molecular orbital theory of bonding (polyatomic molecules and transition metals), solid state chemistry, energetics and spectroscopy of inorganic compounds.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 002C or CHE 002CH or CHE 004C.

title: Inorganic Chemistry: Main Group Elements
description: Course Description: Synthesis, structure and reactivity of inorganic and heteroorganic molecules containing the main group elements.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 124A.

title: Inorganic Chemistry: D & F Block Elements
description: Course Description: Synthesis, structure and reactivity of transition metal complexes, organometallic and bioinorganic chemistry, the lanthanides and actinides.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 124A.

title: Laboratory Methods in Inorganic Chemistry
description: Course Description: The preparation, purification and characterization of main group and transition metal inorganic and organometallic compounds.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 124B or CHE 124C (can be concurrent).

title: Advanced Methods in Physical Chemistry
description: Course Description: Advanced theory and laboratory techniques in analytical and physical chemistry. Advanced spectroscopic methods. Thermodynamics. Kinetics. Chemical literature. Digital electronics and computer interfacing. Laboratory measurements and vacuum techniques.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 110C (can be concurrent); CHE 115.

title: Organic Chemistry
description: Course Description: Introduction to the basic concepts of organic chemistry with emphasis on stereochemistry and the chemistry of hydrocarbons. Designed primarily for majors in chemistry. Chemistry majors should enroll in CHE 129A concurrently.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 002C C or better or CHE 002CH C or better or CHE 004C C- or better.

title: Organic Chemistry
description: Course Description: Continuation of CHE 128A with emphasis on the chemistry of alcohols, ethers, their sulfur analogs, and carbonyl compounds. Introduction to the application of spectroscopic methods to organic chemistry. Introduction to synthesis of moderately complex organic molecules.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 128A; or consent of instructor.

title: Organic Chemistry
description: Course Description: Continuation of CHE 128B with emphasis on enolate condensations and the chemistry of amines, phenols, and sugars; selected biologically important compounds.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 128B.

title: Organic Chemistry Laboratory
description: Course Description: Introduction to laboratory techniques of organic chemistry. Emphasis on methods used for separation and purification of organic compounds.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (CHE 002C C or better or CHE 002CH C or better or CHE 004C C- or better); CHE 128A (can be concurrent).

title: Organic Chemistry Laboratory
description: Course Description: Continuation of CHE 129A. Emphasis on methods used for synthesis and isolation of organic compounds.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 129A; CHE 128B (can be concurrent).

title: Organic Chemistry Laboratory
description: Course Description: continuation of PHE 129B.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 128C (can be concurrent); CHE 129B.

title: Principles of Medicinal Chemistry
description: Course Description: Examination of the design principles and experimental methods used in pharmaceutical and medicinal chemistry.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 118C or CHE 128C.

title: Computational Drug Design
description: Course Description: Continuation of CHE 130A with emphasis on case studies of various drugs and the use of computational methods in drug design.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 130A (can be concurrent).

title: Case Studies in Medicinal Chemistry
description: Course Description: Seminar. Exploration of medicinal and pharmaceutical chemistry topics through seminars presented by professional chemists (and allied professionals). Designed to highlight career opportunities for students with a degree in pharmaceutical chemistry.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 130A (can be concurrent); CHE 130B (can be concurrent).

title: Modern Methods of Organic Synthesis
description: Course Description: Introduction to modern synthetic methodology in organic chemistry with emphasis on retrosynthetic analysis, reaction mechanisms, and application to multistep syntheses of pharmaceuticals and natural products.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 128C or CHE 118C.

title: Drug Development Laboratory
description: Course Description: Separation, purification, identification and biological evaluation of organic compounds using modern methods of synthesis, computational chemistry and instrumentation. Emphasis on pharmaceutical and medicinal substances.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 130B (can be concurrent).

title: Good Quality Practices
description: Course Description: Preparation for work in GQP laboratories in both research and industry. Context within GQP-Good Quality Practices (GMP Good Manufacturing Practice, GCP Good Clinical Practices). Lab practice in GQP skills.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 118B or CHE 129B.

title: Chemistry of Natural Products
description: Course Description: Chemistry of terpenes, steroids, acetogenins, and alkaloids: isolation, structure determination, biosynthesis, chemical transformations, and total synthesis.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 118C or CHE 128C.

title: Scientific Programming for Chemistry
description: Course Description: Chemical applications of computer programming with Python. Numpy, Scipy, Matplotlib libraries. Multidimensional arrays, data visualization, linear algebra routines. Force fields and molecular dynamics simulations. Numerical integration of differential equations with applications to chemical kinetics. Least squares fitting of experimental data.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECS 032A recommended.

title: Chemical & Engineering Principles in Whisky & Fuel Alcohol Production
description: Course Description: Chemical & engineering principles underlying the manufacture of whisky & fuel alcohol. Biochemistry of malting. Assessment of grain modification & diastatic power, and of the phenol content of peated malt. Lautering as a problem of fluidized bed compaction. Fermentation and its assessment. Fractional distillation and the Rayleigh equation. The fate of congeners in pot and column distillation. Chemical reactions affecting flavor from kilning to maturation.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 128A; CHE 128B (can be concurrent); or consent of instructor.

title: Internship in Chemistry
description: Course Description: Supervised internship in chemistry; requires a final written report.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Upper division standing; project approval by faculty sponsor prior to enrollment.

title: Undergraduate Honors Research
description: Course Description: Original research under the guidance of a faculty advisor, culminating in the writing of an extensive report.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Open only to chemistry majors who have completed 135 units and who qualify for the honors program.

title: Undergraduate Honors Research
description: Course Description: Original research under the guidance of a faculty advisor, culminating in the writing of an extensive report.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Open only to chemistry majors who have completed 135 units and who qualify for the honors program.

title: Undergraduate Honors Research
description: Course Description: Original research under the guidance of a faculty advisor, culminating in the writing of an extensive report.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Open only to chemistry majors who have completed 135 units and who qualify for the honors program.

title: Careers in Chemistry
description: Course Description: Designed to give Chemistry undergraduate students an in-depth appreciation of career opportunities with a bachelors degree in chemistry. Professional chemists (and allied professionals) describe research and provide career insights.

title: Projects in Chemical Education
description: Course Description: Participation may include development of laboratory experiments, lecture demonstrations, autotutorial modules or assistance with laboratory sessions.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Directed Group Study
description: Course Description: Directed group study. May be taught abroad.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor based upon adequate preparation in chemistry, mathematics and physics.

title: Special Study for Advanced Undergraduates
description: Course Description: Special study for advanced undergraduates. May be taught abroad.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor based upon adequate preparation in chemistry, mathematics, and physics.

title: Chemical Uses of Symmetry & Group Theory
description: Course Description: Symmetry elements and operations, point groups, representations of groups. Applications to molecular orbital theory, ligand field theory, molecular vibrations, and angular momentum. Crystallographic symmetry.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 124A; CHE 110B; or consent of instructor.

title: Mathematical Methods in Chemistry
description: Course Description: Introduction to mathematical and numerical methods in chemistry. Real and complex functions. Methods of integration. Differential equations. Linear algebra and matrices. Special functions. Integral transforms. Statistics.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 110C.

title: Symmetry, Spectroscopy, & Structure
description: Course Description: Vibrational and rotational spectra; electronic spectra and photoelectron spectroscopy; magnetism; electron spin and nuclear quadrapole resonance spectroscopy; nuclear magnetic resonance spectroscopy; other spectroscopic methods.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 201; or equivalent.

title: Special Topics in Physical Chemistry
description: Course Description: Advanced topics in physical chemistry, biophysical chemistry or chemical physics chosen from areas of current research interest.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 210A; CHE 211A.

title: Quantum Chemistry: Introduction & Stationary-State Properties
description: Course Description: Stationary-state quantum chemistry: postulates of quantum mechanics, simple solutions, central field problems and angular momenta, hydrogen atom, perturbation theory, variational theory, atoms and molecules.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 110B; CHE 110C; or consent of instructor.

title: Quantum Chemistry: Time-Dependent Systems
description: Course Description: Matrix mechanics and time-dependent quantum chemistry: matrix formulation of quantum mechanics, Heisenberg representation, time-dependent perturbation theory, selection rules, density matrices, and miscellaneous molecular properties.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 210A.

title: Quantum Chemistry: Molecular Spectroscopy
description: Course Description: Molecular spectroscopy: Born-Oppenheimer approximation, rotational, vibrational and electronic spectroscopy, spin systems, and molecular photophysics.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 210B.

title: Advanced Physical Chemistry: Statistical Thermodynamics
description: Course Description: Principles and applications of statistical mechanics; ensemble theory; statistical thermodynamics of gases, solids, liquids, electrolyte solutions and polymers; chemical equilibrium.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Statistical Mechanics
description: Course Description: Statistical mechanics of nonequilibrium systems, including the rigorous kinetic theory of gases, continuum mechanics transport in dense fluids, stochastic processes, brownian motion and linear response theory.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 211A.

title: Theoretical & Computational Chemistry
description: Course Description: Mathematics of wide utility in chemistry, computational methods for guidance or alternative to experiment, and modern formulations of chemical theory. Emphasis will vary in successive years.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 211A; CHE 210B; or consent of instructor.

title: Magnetic Resonance Spectroscopy
description: Course Description: Quantum mechanics of spin and orbital angular momentum, nuclear magnetic resonance, theory of chemical shift and multiplet structures, electron spin resonance, theory of gtensor in organic and transition ions, spin Hamiltonians, nuclear quadrupolar resonance, spin relaxation processes.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 210A; CHE 210B (can be concurrent).

title: Macromolecules: Physical Principles
description: Course Description: Relationship of higher order macromolecular structure to subunit composition; equilibrium properties and macromolecular dynamics; physical chemical determination of macromolecular structure.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 110A; CHE 110B; CHE 110C; or equivalent.

title: Spectroscopy of Organic Compounds
description: Course Description: Identification of organic compounds and investigation of stereochemical and reaction mechanism phenomena using spectroscopic methods–principally NMR, IR and MS.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 128C; or equivalent.

title: Laboratory in Spectroscopy of Organic Compounds
description: Course Description: Practical application of NMR, IR and MS techniques for organic molecules.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 219 (can be concurrent).

title: Special Topics in Organic Chemistry
description: Course Description: Selected topics of current interest in organic chemistry. Topics will vary each time the course is offered, and in general will emphasize the research interests of the staff member giving the course.

title: Special Topics in Organic Chemistry
description: Course Description: Selected topics of current interest in organic chemistry. Topics will vary each time the course is offered, and in general will emphasize the research interests of the staff member giving the course.

title: Special Topics in Organic Chemistry
description: Course Description: Selected topics of current interest in organic chemistry. Topics will vary each time the course is offered, and in general will emphasize the research interests of the staff member giving the course.

title: Special Topics in Organic Chemistry
description: Course Description: Selected topics of current interest in organic chemistry. Topics will vary each time the course is offered, and in general will emphasize the research interests of the staff member giving the course.

title: Special Topics in Organic Chemistry
description: Course Description: Selected topics of current interest in organic chemistry. Topics will vary each time the course is offered, and in general will emphasize the research interests of the staff member giving the course.

title: Special Topics in Organic Chemistry
description: Course Description: Selected topics of current interest in organic chemistry. Topics will vary each time the course is offered, and in general will emphasize the research interests of the staff member giving the course.

title: Special Topics in Organic Chemistry
description: Course Description: Selected topics of current interest in organic chemistry. Topics will vary each time the course is offered, and in general will emphasize the research interests of the staff member giving the course.

title: Special Topics in Organic Chemistry
description: Course Description: Selected topics of current interest in organic chemistry. Topics will vary each time the course is offered, and in general will emphasize the research interests of the staff member giving the course.

title: Chemistry of Nanoparticles
description: Course Description: Chemical and physical aspects of inorganic nanoparticles, including synthesis, purification, reactivity, characterization, and applications for technology. Emphasis is on problems from the current literature.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 110C; or equivalent.

title: Principles of Transition Metal Chemistry
description: Course Description: Electronic structures, bonding, and reactivity of transition metal compounds.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 124A; or equivalent.

title: Bio-inorganic Chemistry
description: Course Description: Defines role of inorganic chemistry in the functioning of biological systems by identifying the functions of metal ions and main group compounds in biological systems and discussing the chemistry of model and isolated biological compounds. Offered every third year.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 226; or consent of instructor.

title: Main Group Chemistry
description: Course Description: Synthesis, physical properties, reactions and bonding of main group compounds. Discussions of concepts of electron deficiency, hypervalency, and non-classical bonding. Chemistry of the main group elements will be treated systematically. Offered every third year.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 226; or consent of instructor.

title: Solid-State Chemistry
description: Course Description: Design and synthesis, structure and bonding of solid-state compounds; physical properties and characterization of solids; topics of current interest such as low-dimensional materials, inorganic polymers, materials for catalysis. Offered every third year.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 124A; CHE 110B; CHE 226; or equivalent.

title: Homogeneous Catalysis
description: Course Description: Overview of homogeneous catalysis and related methods, with emphasis on kinetics, mechanisms, and applications for organic synthesis. The related methods may include cluster, colloid, phase transfer, enzymatic, heterogeneous and polymer-supported catalysis. Offered every third year.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 226.

title: Magnetochemistry
description: Course Description: Covers the basic principles and concepts of magnetism, methods used for characterization of magnetic properties, as well as specific state-of-the-art magnetic materials and topics from the recent chemistry literature.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 124A or CHE 201; or an equivalent class from either Physics or Chemical Engineering and Materials Science.

title: Organic Synthesis: Methods & Strategies
description: Course Description: Current strategies and methods in synthetic organic chemistry. Focus on construction of carbon frameworks, control of relative and absolute stereochemistry and retrosynthetic strategies. Use of databases and molecular modeling software in multistep strategies.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 128C; or equivalent.

title: Advanced Organic Synthesis
description: Course Description: Current strategies and methods in synthetic organic chemistry. Continuation of CHE 231A. Organic synthesis of complex target molecules. Stereochemical considerations and asymmetric synthesis. Organometallics for selective transformations. Carbocyclic and heterocyclic ring formation.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 231A.

title: Physical-Organic Chemistry
description: Course Description: Introduction to elementary concepts in physicalorganic chemistry including the application of simple numerical techniques in characterizing and modeling organic reactions.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 128A; CHE 128B; CHE 128C; CHE 110A; CHE 110B; CHE 110C; or equivalent.

title: Chemical & Molecular Neuroscience
description: Course Description: Basic principles in chemistry and molecular biology applied to questions in neuroscience. Chemical and molecular tools driving innovative work in chemical and molecular biology.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 128C; BIS 102; or equivalent courses.

title: Organometallic Chemistry in Organic Synthesis
description: Course Description: Current trends in use of organometallics for organic synthesis; preparations, properties, applications, and limitations of organometallic reagents derived from transition and/or main group metals.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 128C.

title: Chemistry of Natural Products
description: Course Description: Advanced treatment of chemistry of naturally occurring compounds isolated from a variety of sources. Topics will include isolation, structure determination, chemical transformations, total synthesis, biological activity, and biosynthesis. Biosynthetic origin will be used as a unifying theme.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 128C; or equivalent.

title: Bio-Organic Chemistry
description: Course Description: Structure and function of biomolecules; molecular recognition; enzyme reaction mechanisms; design of suicide substrates for enzymes; enzyme engineering; design of artificial enzymes and application of enzymes in organic synthesis.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing in Chemistry or consent of instructor.

title: Introduction to Chemical Biology
description: Course Description: Synthesis of complex molecules in nature. Use of biosynthetic pathways in synthesis of new chemical entities. Applications of small molecules in chemical genetics and structural biology. Solving biological problems using synthetic biomolecules.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 118C or CHE 128C; or equivalent; CHE 130A, CHE 130B and BIS 102, BIS 103, and BIS 104, or the equivalents recommended.

title: Advanced Analytical Chemistry
description: Course Description: Numerical treatment of experimental data; thermodynamics of electrolyte and non-electrolyte solutions; complex equilibria in aqueous and non-aqueous solutions; potentiometry and specific ion electrodes; mass transfer in liquid solutions; fundamentals of separation science, including column, gas and liquid chromatography.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 110A; CHE 115; or equivalent.

title: Surface Analytical Chemistry
description: Course Description: Concepts of surfaces and interfaces: physical properties, unique chemistry and electronic effects. Focus on gas-solid interfaces, with some discussion of liquid-solid interfaces.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 110C; or equivalent.

title: Laser & X-ray Spectroscopy
description: Course Description: Concepts and mechanisms of light-matter interactions. Chemical applications of modern spectroscopic methods, including multiphoton spectroscopy, time-resolved laser and x-ray photolysis, and phase-contrast x-ray imaging.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 110B; or equivalent.

title: Mass Spectrometry
description: Course Description: Mass spectrometry and related methods with emphasis on ionization methods, mass analyzers, and detectors. Related methods may include ion-molecule reactions, unimolecular dissociation of organic and bio-organic compounds, and applications in biological and environmental analysis.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 110C; CHE 115; or equivalent.

title: Electroanalytical Chemistry
description: Course Description: Electroanalytical chemistry with consideration of mass transfer and electrode kinetics for polarizable electrodes. Current-potential curves for a variety of conditions, including both potentiostatic and galvanostatic control, and their application in chemical analysis.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 110C; CHE 115; or equivalent.

title: Microscopy & Imaging Techniques
description: Course Description: Introduction to modern microscopy and imaging techniques: scanning tunneling, atomic force, far-field optical, fluorescence, scanning near-field optical, and scanning electron microscopy. Application to nanoscience and analytical and bioanalytical chemistry. Some laboratory demonstrations.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 110C; CHE 115; or equivalent.

title: Mechanistic Enzymology
description: Course Description: Advanced topics in chemical kinetics relevant to enzymes, enzyme kinetics, theory of enzyme catalysis, and the analysis of a selection of organic enzyme reaction mechanisms by the tools introduced in the first part of the course.

title: Current Topics in Chemical Research
description: Course Description: Designed to help chemistry graduate students develop and maintain familiarity with the current and past literature in their immediate field of research and related areas.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing in Chemistry or consent of instructor.

title: Introduction to Chemistry Research
description: Course Description: Group and individual discussion of research activities in the Department and research topic selection. Designed for incoming graduate students preparing for higher degrees in chemistry.

title: Introduction to Chemical Research Methodology
description: Course Description: Introduction to identification, formulation, and solution of meaningful scientific problems including experimental design and/or theoretical analyses of new and prevailing techniques, theories and hypotheses. May be repeated for credit when topic differs.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 293; and consent of instructor; graduate student standing in Chemistry.
