prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Selected Topics in Civil Engineering: Water Resources Engineering
description: Course Description: Directed group study in Water Resources Engineering.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Selected Topics in Civil Engineering: Water Resources Planning
description: Course Description: Directed group study in Water Resources Planning.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Research Group Conferences in Civil & Environmental Engineering
description: Course Description: Research group conferences.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor. Upper division standing in Civil and Environmental Engineering.

title: Advocating for Equity in Engineering
description: Course Description: Diversity, equity, inclusion, and justice in engineering academia and professional culture. Challenges specific to engineering and tools to empower students to take action.

title: Construction Engineering & Project Management Seminar
description: Course Description: Aspects of project management and delivery such as estimating, scheduling, scoping, contract law, and construction of all types of civil and mechanical engineering infrastructure. Contractor, supplier, consultant, and owner point of view, including consideration of construction in design. Engagement with guest speakers featuring industry and government leaders followed by discussions.

title: Civil & Environmental Engineering Career Pathways
description: Course Description: Demonstration of different civil and environmental engineering career options; engagement with CEE alumni through panels.

title: Internship in Engineering
description: Course Description: Supervised work experience in civil engineering.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Upper division standing; approval of project prior to the period of the internship.

title: Civil & Environmental Engineering Senior Design
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Culminating design experience for civil engineering and environmental engineering majors. Student teams work closely with faculty, city officials or consulting clients to propose, implement and validate a unique solution to a real-world problem.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 114 C- or better; (ECI 115 C- or better or ECI 153 C- or better or MAT 118A C- or better); (ECI 140CN C- or better or (ECI 171 C- or better, ECI 171L C- or better) or (ECI 132 C- or better, ECI 134 C- or better) or (ECI 132 C- or better, ECI 135 C- or better) or (ECI 134 C- or better, ECI 135 C- or better) or (ECI 161 C- or better, ECI 162 C- or better) or (ECI 162 C- or better, ECI 163 C- or better) or (ECI 163 C- or better, ECI 161 C- or better) or (ECI 141 C- or better, ECI 141L C- or better)), (UWP 101 or UWP 102E or UWP 102G or UWP 104A or UWP 104E or UWP 104T).

title: Civil & Environmental Engineering Senior Design
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Culminating design experience for civil engineering and environmental engineering majors. Student teams work closely with faculty, city officials or consulting clients to propose, implement and validate a unique solution to a real-world problem.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 193A.

title: Directed Group Study
description: Course Description: Directed group study. May be taught abroad.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Special Study for Advanced Undergraduates
description: Course Description: Special study. May be taught abroad.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Introduction to Theory of Elasticity
description: Course Description: Fundamental equations of elasticity in three dimensions; plane stress and plane strain; flexture and torsion of bars of various shapes. Introduction to variational and approximate methods.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENG 104 or ENG 104V.

title: Inelastic Behavior of Solids
description: Course Description: Fundamentals of theories of plasticity, viscoelasticity and viscoplasticity for solids. Macroscopic constitutive modelling for engineering materials, e.g., metals, polymers, soils, etc., and microscopic motivation.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 201.

title: Continuum Mechanics
description: Course Description: Tensor formulation of the field equations for continuum mechanics, including large deformation effects. Invariance and symmetry requirements. Introduction to nonlinear thermoelasticity and thermodynamics. Solution of three-dimensional problems. Selected topics.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 201.

title: Advanced Matrix Structural Analysis
description: Course Description: Matrix structural analysis for automated analysis of structures using computers. Application of matrix analysis techniques to complex problems in structural engineering arising from soil-structure interactions, three-dimensional effects, and nonlinear material response.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 130; or equivalent or consent of instructor.

title: Nonlinear Structural Analysis
description: Course Description: Geometric and material nonlinear response of framed structures, and methods for computational simulation of such response. Applications of these concepts and techniques to structural design within modern performance assessment frameworks and software platforms.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 211A; or equivalent or consent of instructor.

title: Finite Element Procedures in Applied Mechanics
description: Course Description: Weighted-residual and Rayleigh-Ritz methods. Weak/variational formulation and development of discrete equations using finite element approximations. Application to one- and two-dimensional problems (heat conduction).
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): EAD 115 or (MAT 128A, MAT 128B (can be concurrent)).

title: Finite Elements: Application to Linear & Non-Linear Structural Mechanics Problems
description: Course Description: Application to linear and nonlinear structural mechanics problems. Linear elasticity, weak form, and finite element approximation. Incompressible media problems. Non-linear problems with material nonlinearity.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 212A.

title: Structural Dynamics
description: Course Description: Analysis of structures subjected to dynamic loading; single and multi-degree of freedom systems; response spectrum analysis; numerical methods for analysis of linear systems.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 138; Or equivalent or consent of instructor.

title: Probabilistic Seismic Hazard Analysis & Design Ground Motions
description: Course Description: Probabilistic seismic hazard analysis for use in developing design spectra and for seismic risk analyses, including the development of earthquake ground motion time series for use in dynamic analyses of structures.

title: Structural Reliability Analysis
description: Course Description: Review of probability theory (probability distributions, conditional probability, functions of random variables); component reliability analysis (exact solutions, FORM, SORM); system reliability analysis (series systems, parallel systems, general systems); simulation methods (Monte Carlo simulation, variance reduction techniques); probabilistic codified design and reliability-based design; applications to earthquake and wind engineering.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Highly recommended, but not required, that students have taken a graduate/undergraduate level course in Matrix and/or Indeterminate Structural Analysis and an undergraduate course in Probability Theory.

title: Random Vibrations
description: Course Description: Probability theory; stochastic processes/random fields (time/frequency domain description, stationarity, mean square calculus, multivariate processes); response of linear systems under stochastic excitations (single-/multi-DOF systems, time/frequency domain, state space approach); reliability-based structural design (first-excursion and fatigue failures); applications to earthquake and wind engineering.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate level course in (deterministic) Structural Dynamics such as ECI 213 required (can be concurrent).

title: Advanced Topics in Concrete Structures
description: Course Description: Ductility of reinforced concrete; strength of two-way slabs; modified compression field theory.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 130; ECI 135; ECI 138; graduate standing.

title: Advanced Design of Steel Structures
description: Course Description: Review of Load and Resistance Factor Design (LRFD); design of indeterminate systems; moment frames and bracing systems; connection design; seismic design of steel structures; direct analysis method for stability design; and performance assessment of steel structures.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (ECI 130 or ECI 131); ECI 132.

title: Prestressed Concrete
description: Course Description: Survey of methods and applications; prestressing materials and systems; prestress losses; flexural design; design for shear and torsion; deflection computation and control; continuous beams and indeterminate structures; floor systems; partial prestressing; design of compression members; strut-and-tie models.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 135; (ECI 130 or ECI 131).

title: Cement Composites
description: Course Description: Applications of cement composites; materials selection and proportioning; component and composite properties; hydration reactions and microstructure development; mechanisms of failure; nondestructive test methods; fiber reinforcement; concrete durability; novel reinforcing materials; ferrocement; repair and retrofit technologies; applications to structural design.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENG 104 or ENG 104V.

title: Design of Fiber Reinforced Polymer Composite Structures
description: Course Description: Basics of mechanics and design of polymer matrix composites: composite classification, manufacturing process, micromechanical property determination, classical lamination theory, strength theories, first-ply-failure, test methods, design practice, strengthening and retrofitting of existing reinforced concrete structures.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 135.

title: Bridge Design
description: Course Description: Bridge types, behavior and construction characteristics; design philosophy, details according to Caltrans and American Association of State Highway and Transportation Officials codes, principles; seismic design and retrofit of concrete bridges; modern bridges using advanced fiber reinforced polymer composites; fieldtrip required.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 130; ECI 135; ECI 234 recommended.

title: Performance-Based Seismic Engineering
description: Course Description: Modern seismic design; performance-based seismic design; seismic hazard; seismic demands: linear and nonlinear procedures; performance assessment: deterministic and probabilistic procedure; review of FEMA-350, FEMA-356, ATC-40 and other performance-based guidelines.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 138; ECI 213.

title: Design of Materials & Systems for Sustainability
description: Course Description: Effects on environmental impacts from materials selection and design in the built environment. Material mechanics, design constraints, and life cycle assessment in the development of “greener” materials and systems. Design of engineered materials through selecting constituents & processing techniques and the influence on mechanical properties, microstructure, and environmental impacts. Role of materials in the environmental impacts of buildings, infrastructure, and other systems.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENG 104 C or ENG 104V C or better; or consent of instructor.

title: Water Quality
description: Course Description: Quality requirements for beneficial uses of water. Hydrologic cycle of quality. Hydromechanics in relation to quality of surface and groundwaters; transport and fate of waterborne pollutants. Heat budget for surface waters; predictive methods; introduction to water quality modeling.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 141; ECI 142.

title: Environmental Reactive Chemical Transport Modeling
description: Course Description: Modeling of reactive chemical transport in air and water including kinetic reactions, equilibrium reactions, and phase partitioning. Emphasis on numerical solution schemes and programming techniques to provide deeper insight into model performance and limitations.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (CHE 002A or CHE 002B or ECI 149N); or equivalent.

title: Air Quality
description: Course Description: Factors determining air quality. Effects of air pollutants. Physical and chemical fundamentals of atmospheric transport and reaction. Introduction to dispersion modeling.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (ENG 105; ECI 141N; ECI 149); or equivalents.

title: Water & Waste Treatment
description: Course Description: Characteristics of water and airborne wastes; treatment processes and process kinetics; treatment system design.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 148A; or the equivalent.

title: Biological Treatment of Wastewater
description: Course Description: Regulations; microbiology and kinetics of engineered biological processes; aeration; and activated sludge.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 243A.

title: Biological Treatment of Wastewater
description: Course Description: Biological treatment of wastewater using fixed film, hybrid, digestion, and biological nutrient removal processes.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 243A; ECI 243B.

title: Pilot Plant Laboratory
description: Course Description: Laboratory investigation of physical, chemical, and biological processes for water and wastewater treatment.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 243A; ECI 243B (can be concurrent); or consent of instructor. Graduate standing.

title: Life Cycle Assessment for Sustainable Engineering
description: Course Description: Life cycle assessment methodology. Emphasis on applications to infrastructure and energy systems. Life cycle design, life cycle cost methods, other tools from industrial ecology, and links to policy.

title: Advanced Methods in Industrial Ecology
description: Course Description: Implementation, interpretation, and methodological issues and advances in life cycle assessment and other complementary methods from the field of Industrial Ecology with a focus on use in research.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 244A; or consent of instructor.

title: Applied Environmental Chemistry: Inorganic
description: Course Description: Chemistry of natural and polluted waters. Topics include chemical, kinetic and equilibrium principles, redox reactions, gas solution and solid-solution equilibria, thermodynamics, carbonate systems, coordination chemistry, interfacial phenomena.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENG 105; ECI 140; CHE 002B; or the equivalent of CHE 002B; CHE 002C or CHE 107A recommended.

title: Applied Environmental Chemistry: Organic
description: Course Description: Transport and transformation of organic chemicals in the environment. Topics include application of thermodynamics to predict solubility and activity coefficients; distribution of organic chemicals between the aqueous phase and air, solvent, or solid phases; chemical, photochemical and biological transformation reactions.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 128A; CHE 128B; CHE 128C; or the equivalent; CHE 002C or CHE 107A recommended.

title: Aerosols
description: Course Description: Behavior of airborne particles including particle formation, modification, and removal processes.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENG 103; ENG 105; ECI 141; ECI 149N.

title: Aerosols Laboratory
description: Course Description: Methods of generation and characterization of aerosols. Detailed topics may include flow rate measurement, aerosol generation, aerosol collection, ions measurement, metals measurement, and carbon measurement.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 247.

title: Environmental Health Engineering
description: Course Description: Principles and practices of environmental health engineering. Identification, evaluation, and management of environmental factors that impact public health with an emphasis on viruses and waterborne pathogens. Wastewater-based epidemiology, quantitative microbial and chemical risk assessment, and the removal and inactivation of pathogens in water and wastewater treatment.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 243A; or consent of instructor.

title: Probabilistic Design & Optimization
description: Course Description: Design by optimization for probabilistic systems, decision theory, the value of information, probabilistic linear programming, probabilistic dynamic programming, nonlinear probabilistic optimization. Applications in civil engineering design, project evaluation, and risk management.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 114; ECI 153; ENG 106; or equivalents.

title: Transportation Demand Analysis
description: Course Description: Procedures used in urban travel demand forecasting. Principles and assumptions of model components (trip generation, trip distribution, model split). New methods of estimating travel demand. Computer exercises using empirical data to calibrate models and forecast travel demand.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 114; or the equivalent.

title: Sustainable Transportation Systems
description: Course Description: Freight transportation systems, with optimization model applications beyond freight including mobility, autonomous vehicles, electrification, and planning. Evaluation of freight improvement strategies including operations, infrastructure, management, and vehicle technologies. Analysis of private and system impacts and environmental and social issues of freight activity such as traffic, behaviors, logistics, and e-commerce.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 251; or consent of instructor.

title: Dynamic Programming & Multistage Decision Processes
description: Course Description: Operations research. Optimization techniques with a focus on dynamic programming in treating deterministic, stochastic, and adaptive multistage decision processes. Brief review of linear programming and non-linear programming. Applications in transportation networks and other civil infrastructure systems.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): MAT 021C; MAT 022A; programming course; EAD 115 recommended.

title: Exploring Data from Built Environment Using R
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Introduction to modern data science, specifically data acquisition, exploratory data analysis, visualization, and beginning data analysis using R. Emphasizes computational reasoning and working with tabular and non-standard data. Focus will be on data generated in the built environment.

title: Transit System Planning & Design
description: Course Description: Transit agency and transit service operations and planning, including scheduling. Street design for transit priority. Bus and rail transit capacity and modal differences. Data used to measure transit performance.

title: Urban Traffic Management & Control
description: Course Description: Basic concepts, models, and methods related to the branch of traffic science that deals with the movement of vehicles on a road network, including travel speed, travel time, congestion concepts, car-following and hydrodynamic traffic models.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 114.

title: Flow in Transportation Networks
description: Course Description: Elements of graph theory, a survey of pertinent optimization techniques, extremal principles in network flow problems, deterministic equilibrium assignment, stochastic equilibrium assignment, extensions of equilibrium assignments and dynamic transportation network assignment.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 153; ECI 161 or ECI 256 recommended.

title: Asphalt & Asphalt Mixes
description: Course Description: Asphalts and asphalt mix types and their use in civil engineering structures, with primary emphasis on pavements. Asphalt, aggregate properties and effects on mix properties. Design, construction, recycling. Recent developments and research.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 179; or consent of instructor.

title: Sediment Transport
description: Course Description: Sediment transport in hydrologic systems. Process-oriented course which will emphasize how sediment moves and the physical processes that affect sediment transport. Field trip.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 141; or equivalent.

title: Colloids in Soil & Water
description: Course Description: Colloid occurrence, properties, behavior in different environments, and transport mechanisms in water and soils. Emphasis on their role in water contamination.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 002B; (ENG 103 or ECI 100); upper division or graduate standing.

title: Turbulent Land-Water-Atmosphere Interactions in the Atmospheric Boundary Layer
description: Course Description: Atmospheric boundary layer and the associated thermodynamics, surface energy balance partitioning, equations of motion for the atmospheric boundary layer, turbulent scalar (e.g., heat, water vapor, pollution, etc.) transport equations for stratified flow, atmospheric stability, land-water-atmosphere interactions, similarity relations, modeling parameterizations, and field data analysis.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): MAT 022B; ECI 100; ECI 141; or consent of instructor.

title: Evolutionary Algorithms
description: Course Description: Engineering systems design via approximate optimization of simulation models. Local vs. global search. Genetic algorithms, evolutionary strategies, genetic programming. Multi-objective methods. Parameter sensitivity and convergence assessment. Surrogate modeling.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 153 C or better; or consent of instructor. Computer programming experience required.

title: Transport, Mixing & Water Quality in River & Lakes
description: Course Description: Principal causes of mixing and transport in rivers, lakes and reservoirs, and their impacts on water quality. Case studies of specific lakes and rivers.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 141; ECI 240.

title: Transport, Mixing & Water Quality in Estuaries & Wetlands
description: Course Description: Principal causes of mixing and transport in estuaries and wetlands, and their impacts on water quality. Topics include advection/diffusion; tides; transverse mixing; longitudinal dispersion; sediment transport; nutrient cycling; computer modeling of estuaries. Case studies of specific systems.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 141; ECI 240.

title: Stochastic Hydrology & Hydraulics
description: Course Description: Physics-based stochastic methods in modeling hydrologic and hydraulic processes; theory for modeling hydrologic-hydraulic governing equations as stochastic partial differential equations applied to various hydrologic-hydraulic processes under uncertainty, including transport, open channel flow, overland flow, soil water flow, and groundwater.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 266; or consent of instructor.

title: Applied Stochastic Methods in Engineering
description: Course Description: Stochastic processes classification; Gaussian random fields; stochastic calculus in mean square; Ito and Stratonovich stochastic differential equations; Fokker-Planck equation; stochastic differential equations with random coefficients.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 114 or MAT 131 or STA 130A or STA 131A or MAT 118A (can be concurrent).

title: Water Resource Management
description: Course Description: Engineering, institutional, economic, and social basis for managing local and regional water resources. Examples in the context of California's water development and management. Uses of computer modeling to improve water management.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 114; ECI 142; or consent of instructor.

title: Infrastructure Economics
description: Course Description: Economics applied to infrastructure engineering planning, operations, maintenance, and management problems; microeconomic and macroeconomic theories; benefit-cost analysis; effect of uncertainty; optimization economics; non-classical economics; public finance.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (ECN 001A or ECN 001AY or ECN 001AV); ENG 106; or the equivalent.

title: Transportation-Air Quality: Theory & Practice
description: Course Description: Health and regulatory aspects of airborne pollutants. Principles of modeling vehicle emissions. Conformity issues and the regulatory framework. Regional and micro-scale modeling.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 149; or the equivalent.

title: Advanced Water Resources Management
description: Course Description: Discussion of technical papers related to planning theory, system maintenance, regionalization, multi-objective methods, risk analysis, institutional issues, pricing model application, economic development, forecasting, operations, and other topics.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 153; ECI 267; or the equivalent.

title: Contaminant Fate & Transport in Porous Media
description: Course Description: Solute and colloid mass transport processes in porous media. Characterizing and quantifying physical processes of advection, diffusion/dispersion, sorption, as well as basic biogeochemical reactions. Colloid-facilitated transport in porous media. Analytical and numerical solutions to the reactive advection-dispersion equation in Eulerian and Lagrangian forms with an introduction to advanced random walk models.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 144; MAT 021A; MAT 021B; MAT 021C; MAT 022A; MAT 022B; Ability to program.

title: Water Resources Systems Engineering
description: Course Description: Planning and management of water resource systems. Deterministic and stochastic simulation and optimization techniques. Capacity design and operation of reservoir systems for water supply, hydropower, flood control, and environmental objectives.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 114; ECI 153; or the equivalent.

title: Introduction to Turbulence
description: Course Description: Fundamental theory, statistics, analysis tools and models for turbulence and turbulent flows. Practical skills related to the analysis and study of turbulence.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): MAT 022B; ECI 100; ECI 141; or consent of instructor.

title: Hydrologic Time-Series Analysis
description: Course Description: Application of statistical methods for analysis and modeling of hydrologic series. Statistical simulation and prediction of hydrologic sequences using time series methodology.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 114; ECI 142.

title: Watershed Hydrology
description: Course Description: Analysis and mathematical modeling of hydrologic processes taking place in a watershed. Precipitation analysis and modeling. Theory of overland flow and its kinematic wave approximation.Analysis and modeling of saturated and unsaturated subsurface flow processes taking place on a hill slope.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 142; or the equivalent.

title: Computational River Mechanics I
description: Course Description: Unsteady open channel flows, computation of water surface profiles, shallow water equations, St. Venant equations, method of characteristics, finite difference methods, stability and accuracy of explicit and implicit schemes, flood routing in simple and compound channels, advection of plumes.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): EAD 115 (can be concurrent); ECI 141 (can be concurrent).

title: Computational River Mechanics II
description: Course Description: Open channel flows, physical aspects of river mechanics, formulation of depth-averaged equations, boundary conditions, coordinates transformation and grid generation, finite-difference solution techniques, applications to two-dimensional momentum and pollutant transport in rivers.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 277A.

title: Hydrodynamics
description: Course Description: Perturbation methods. Basic water waves. Governing equations for fluid motion on a rotating earth. Rotation effects, vorticity dynamics, Ekman layer. Stratification effects, internal waves and turbulent mixing. Combined effects.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 141.

title: Advanced Mechanics of Fluids
description: Course Description: Rotational flows. Navier-Stokes equations and solutions for laminar flow; boundary layer equations and solution techniques. Nature of turbulence. Reynolds equations. Introduction to turbulence modeling.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 141.

title: Nonlinear Finite Elements for Elastic-Plastic Problems
description: Course Description: State of the art finite element methods and tools for elasticplastic problems, including computational techniques based on the finite element method and the theory of elastoplasticity.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Nonlinear Dynamic Finite Elements
description: Course Description: State of the art computational methods and tools for analyzing linear and nonlinear dynamics problems.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Advanced Soil Mechanics
description: Course Description: Consolidation and secondary compression. Preloading and wick drains. Seepage and seepage pressures. Filtration, drainage, and dewatering. Shear strength: friction, cohesion, dilatancy and critical states.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 171.

title: Engineering Geology
description: Course Description: Principles of geology in engineering design, construction, and natural hazards. Study of geologic setting, geologic processes, properties of geologic materials and influence on safety, constructability, economic feasibility, environmental impact, and performance.

title: Integrated Site Characterization
description: Course Description: Integrated site characterization using field and laboratory techniques. Applied within context of slope stability analysis as a representative design problem.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 281A; or consent of instructor.

title: Pavement Design & Rehabilitation
description: Course Description: Advanced pavement design and structural/functional condition evaluation for concrete and asphalt pavements. Highways, airfields, port facilities; new facilities, rehabilitation, reconstruction. Mechanistic-empirical procedures, materials, climate and traffic characterization. Use of current design methods; recent developments and research.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 179; or consent of instructor.

title: Physico-Chemical Aspects of Soil Behavior
description: Course Description: Study of the geotechnical behavior of soils considering formation, transport, mineralogy, soil-fluid-electrolyte systems, surface tension, particle mechanics, shape, fabric, and structure. Laboratories demonstrate effects of fundamental interparticle forces (contact, Van Der Waals, capillarity and chemical).
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 171.

title: Theoretical Geomechanics
description: Course Description: Elasticity, plasticity, micromechanics, coupled behavior and large deformations for geomaterials. Prediction of stress-strain-volume change behavior of geomaterials. Monotonic and cyclic loading, anisotropy, bifurcation of deformation.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 171.

title: Advanced Topics in Concrete Pavement Engineering & Materials
description: Course Description: Advances in concrete pavements engineering and materials, current and future needs. Strategies to reduce global warming potential through optimizing structural design and enhancing the durability of concrete pavements. Topics include structural design optimization strategies, mixture design optimization, implementation of performance-based specifications for concrete pavements, use of supplementary cementitious materials, fibers, nanomaterials, admixtures.

title: Advanced Foundation Design
description: Course Description: Design and analysis of pile and pier foundations, including seismic effects; deep excavation systems; tie-back, nailing, and anchor systems; coffer dams; loads on buried conduits; ground modification techniques; and other related topics.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 173.

title: Geotechnical Earthquake Engineering
description: Course Description: Characteristics and estimation of earthquake ground motions; wave propagation and local site response; liquefaction potential and remediation; residual strength and stability considerations; ground deformations; dynamic soil-structure interaction.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 138; ECI 281A.

title: Earth & Rockfill Dams
description: Course Description: Site selection; design considerations; layout; seismic effects including considerations of fault movements; construction; environmental considerations, instrumentation; maintenance remediation and retrofit of existing dams.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 281A; ECI 281B (can be concurrent).

title: Selected Topics in Civil Engineering: Environmental Engineering
description: Course Description: Directed group study in Environmental Engineering.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Selected Topics in Civil Engineering: Hydraulics & Hydrologic Engineering
description: Course Description: Directed group study in Hydraulics & Hydrologic Engineering.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Selected Topics in Civil Engineering: Engineering Planning
description: Course Description: Directed group study in Engineering Planning.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Selected Topics in Civil Engineering: Geotechnical Engineering
description: Course Description: Directed group study in Geotechnical Engineering.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Selected Topics in Civil Engineering: Structural Engineering
description: Course Description: Directed group study in Structural Engineering.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Selected Topics in Civil Engineering: Structural Mechanics
description: Course Description: Directed group study in Structural Mechanics.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Selected Topics in Civil Engineering: Transportation Engineering
description: Course Description: Directed group study in Transportation Engineering.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Selected Topics in Civil Engineering: Transportation Planning
description: Course Description: Directed group study in Transportation Planning.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Selected Topics in Civil Engineering: Water Resources Engineering
description: Course Description: Directed group study in Water Resources Engineering.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Seminar
description: Course Description: Discussion of current graduate research, and guest lectures on recent advances. Oral presentation of individual study. Required of graduate degree candidates.

title: Graduate Research Group Conference
description: Course Description: Research problems, progress, and techniques in civil engineering.

title: Projects for Environmental & Water Resources
description: Course Description: Capstone independent literature-based research project. Project development, reading and mining the literature, strategies and best-practices for writing and oral communication.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Geotechnical Professional Practice 1
description: Course Description: Geotechnical practice issues that affect the delivery of technical solutions for geotechnical projects, including business and management practices, contract documents, legal constraints, public policy and societal concerns, ethics, and professional development. Part 1 of three part series that includes a capstone project.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Geotechnical Professional Practice 2
description: Course Description: Geotechnical practice issues that affect the delivery of technical solutions for geotechnical projects, including business and management practices, contract documents, legal constraints, public policy and societal concerns, ethics, and professional development. Part 2 of three part series that includes a capstone project.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 292A; or consent of instructor.

title: Geotechnical Professional Practice 3
description: Course Description: Geotechnical practice issues that affect the delivery of technical solutions for geotechnical projects, including business and management practices, contract documents, legal constraints, public policy and societal concerns, ethics, and professional development. Part 3 of three part series that includes a capstone project.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECI 292B; or consent of instructor.

title: Engineering Education I
description: Course Description: Overview of the discipline of engineering education. One course in a three-course series.

title: Engineering Education II - DEI
description: Course Description: Diversity, equity, inclusion and justice in engineering education. One course in a three-course series.

title: Engineering Education III
description: Course Description: Designing engineering lessons and modules for an academic or professional audience. One course in a three-course series.

title: Topics in Water & Environmental Engineering
description: Course Description: Seminars presented by visiting lecturers, UC Davis faculty, and graduate students.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing; or consent of instructor.

title: Group Study
description: Course Description: Group study.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Research
description: Course Description: Research.

title: The Teaching of Civil Engineering
description: Course Description: Participation as teaching assistant or associate-in in a designated engineering course. Methods of leading discussion groups or laboratory sections, writing and grading quizzes, use of laboratory equipment, and grading laboratory reports.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Meet qualifications for teaching assistant and/or associate-in in Civil Engineering.

title: Principles & Applications of Ecology
description: Course Description: Covers principles of community structure and functioning, species diversity patterns, ecosystem ecology and biogeochemistry, landscape ecology, biogeography and phylogenetics.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): MAT 016A; MAT 016B; STA 102; or consent of instructor; first course in Ecology (e.g., ESP 100).

title: Principles & Applications of Ecology
description: Course Description: Provides a broad background in the principles and applications of ecology, and serves as a foundation for advanced ecology courses. Topics include ecophysiology, behavioral ecology, population ecology, genetics and evolution. Emphasis on historical developments, current understanding, and real world applications.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): STA 102; MAT 016A; MAT 016B; or consent of instructor; first course in Ecology (e.g., ESP 100).

title: Community Ecology
description: Course Description: Introduction to literature and contemporary research into processes structuring ecological communities.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): An upper division course in Ecology (ECL).

title: Concepts & Methods in Plant Community Ecology
description: Course Description: Principles and techniques of vegetation analysis, including structure, composition, and dynamics. Emphasis given to sampling procedures, association analysis, ordination, processes and mechanisms of succession, and classification. Most techniques are demonstrated or conducted during field trips and laboratories.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor; introductory courses in statistics and plant ecology.

title: Plant Population Biology
description: Course Description: Introduction to theoretical and empirical research in plant population biology. Emphasis placed on linking ecological ecological and genetic approaches to plant population biology.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Advanced undergraduate ecology course (e.g., ESP 100, EVE 101, ENT 104 or PLB 117), and advanced undergraduate course in genetics and/or evolution (e.g., BIS 101 or EVE 100).

title: Issues in Conservation Biology
description: Course Description: Graduate-level introduction to current research in conservation biology. Emphasizes reading and discussing primary literature. Specific topics will reflect the research interests of UC Davis conservation biology faculty.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Introductory biology (e.g. BIS 002B) and an upper division organismal biology class.

title: Environmental Policy Process
description: Course Description: Introduction to selected topics of the policy process and applications to the field of environmental policy. Develops critical reading skills, understanding of policy theory, and an ability to apply multiple theories to the same phenomena.

title: Environmental Policy Evaluation
description: Course Description: Method and practice, philosophical basis, and political role of policy analysis. Reviews basic concepts from economic theory; how and why environmental problems emerge in a market economy; and tools necessary for solving environmental problems.

title: Marine Ecology: Concepts & Practice
description: Course Description: Critical review and analysis of concepts and practices in modern marine ecology at the interface of several fields of study including oceanography, evolution, behavior, and physiology. Emphasis on critical thinking, problem solving, and hands-on study. Two field trips required.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor. Graduate standing or one course in ecology, one course in evolution or genetics; survey course in marine ecology recommended.

title: Social Ecological Systems
description: Course Description: Overview of social-ecological systems that links environmental policy and decision-making to ecological processes. Delves deeper into different social science topics related to this broader idea. Applying of course readings to case studies chosen by students and a final paper.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Completion of core courses for specific graduate programs, for example ECL 200A, ECL 200B.

title: Ecology & Agriculture
description: Course Description: Ecological principles as relevant to agriculture. Integration of ecological approaches into agricultural research to increase ecosystem functions and services. Topics include crop autoecology, biotic interactions among crops and pests, ecosystem and landscape ecology.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): EVE 011; or consent of instructor.

title: Ecosystem Biogeochemistry
description: Course Description: Multi-disciplinary analysis of energy and nutrient transfers within terrestrial ecosystems. Examination of processes and inter- and intra-system interactions between the atmosphere, biosphere, lithosphere, and hydrosphere. Laboratory section uses biogeochemical simulation models to examine case studies.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Introductory courses in ecology/biology and soils are recommended; undergraduates accepted with consent of instructor.

title: Data Management & Visualization in R
description: Course Description: Introduction to programming and data analysis in R. Workflow (version control, markdown, reading and writing data), object-oriented programming, statistical analysis, and visualization.

title: Terrestrial Field Ecology
description: Course Description: Field course conducted over spring break and four weekends at Bodega Bay, emphasizing student projects. Ecological hypothesis testing, data gathering, analysis, and written and oral presentation of results are stressed.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Introductory ecology and introductory statistics or consent of instructor.

title: Mathematical Methods in Population Biology
description: Course Description: Mathematical methods used in population biology. Linear and nonlinear difference equation and differential equation models are studied, using stability analysis and qualitative methods. Partial differential equation models are introduced. Applications to population biology models are stressed.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): MAT 016C or MAT 021C; or the equivalent.

title: Theoretical Ecology
description: Course Description: Examination of major conceptual and methodological issues in theoretical ecology. Model formulation and development will be emphasized. Topics differ from year to year.

title: Computational Methods in Population Biology
description: Course Description: Numerical methods for simulating population dynamics using the computational software package R. Emphasis placed on model formulation and development, theoretical concepts and philosophical principles to guide simulation efforts, model parameterization, and implementing simulations with R.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): A course in theoretical ecology (e.g., ECL 231 or an equivalent to ESP 121 from your undergraduate institution) or consent of instructor; no programming experience required.

title: Bayesian Models: A Statistical Primer
description: Course Description: Practical model-building skills and intuition in statistical modeling. Construction of accurate mathematical expressions to link observation to specific hypotheses. For researchers in the natural and social sciences.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): EVE 231 or PLS 205 or PLS 120 or EVE 225; or consent of instructor; experience with basic linear modeling and R coding strongly recommended.

title: Ecological Genomics
description: Course Description: Genomics concepts, technologies, and analyses for ecology research. Mixture of lecture, discussion of recent literature, hands-on training in data analysis and experimental design, and research proposal preparation and evaluation. One all-day field trip is required.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECL 242; or equivalent training in ecology and genetics according to the discretion of the instructors.

title: Climate Change Science & Impacts
description: Course Description: Overview of climate change science with a focus on climate change communication. Impacts of climate change on water, agriculture, energy, health, infrastructure, ecosystem services, tribal and indigenous communities. Climate justice, political, societal, and economic dimensions of these issues.

title: Advanced Population Dynamics
description: Course Description: Logical basis for population models, evaluation of simple ecological models, current population models with age, size, and stage structure, theoretical basis for management and exemplary case histories. Emphasis on development and use of realistic population models in ecological research.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing; advanced course in ecology (e.g., EVE 101), population dynamics (e.g., WFC 122), and one year of calculus; familiarity with matrix algebra and partial differential equations recommended.

title: Research Conference in Ecology
description: Course Description: Critical presentation and evaluation of current literature and ongoing research in ecology. Requirements include active participation in weekly discussions and the presentation of a paper or chapter once per quarter.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Current Anthropology Journal Editorial Workshop
description: Course Description: Reading and offering workshop critiques of manuscripts submitted for publication, and reading and discussion of other relevant work in anthropology and human ecology. Track and edit published comments and authors’ replies that accompany major features. Participation in the development of new sections for the electronic edition of the journal, including a "news and views" section and a debate section.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Seminar in Ecology
description: Course Description: Topics in ecology. Students are expected to present an oral seminar on a particular aspect of the general topic under consideration.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Topics in Ecology & Evolution
description: Course Description: Seminars presented by visiting lecturers, UC Davis faculty, and graduate students.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing.

title: Tutoring in Ecology
description: Course Description: Teaching ecology including conducting discussion groups for regular departmental courses under direct guidance of staff.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor; graduate standing in ecology.

description: Course Description: Optimal decisions under uncertainty, expected utility theory, economics of insurance, asymmetric information, signalling in the job market, incentives and Principal-Agent theory, optimal search strategies and the reservation price principle.

description: Course Description: Design of Experiments. Experimental evidence on behavioral biases observed in intertemporal decisions, risky decisions, and strategic decisions. Economic models of boundedly rational behavior.

prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (ECN 001A or ECN 001AY or ECN 001AV); (ECN 001B or ECN 100BV).

prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (ECN 001A or ECN 001AY or ECN 001AV); (ECN 001B or ECN 001BV).

prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (ECN 001A or OR ECN 001AY or ECN 001AV); (ECN 001B or ECN 001 BV); or consent of instructor.

prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (ECN 001A or ECN 001AY or ECN 001AV); (ECN 001B or ECN 001BV); or consent of instructor.

title: Economic Development
description: Course Description: Major issues encountered in emerging from international poverty, including problems of growth and structural change, human welfare, population growth and health, labor markets and internal migration. Important issues of policy concerning international trade and industrialization.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (ECN 001A or ECN 001AY or ECN 001AV); (ECN 001B or ECN 001BV).

title: Economic Development
description: Course Description: Major macroeconomic issues of developing countries. Issues include problems in generating capital, conduct of monetary and fiscal policies, foreign aid and investment. Important issues of policy concerning international borrowing and external debt of developing countries. May be taught abroad.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (ECN 001A or ECN 001AY or ECN 001AV); (ECN 001B or ECN 001BV).

title: Economic Development
description: Course Description: Macroeconomic issues of developing countries. Problems in generating capital, conduct of monetary and fiscal policies, foreign aid and investment. Issues of policy concerning international borrowing and external debt of developing countries.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (ECN 001A or ECN 001AY or ECN 001AV); (ECN 001B or ECN 001BV).

title: Comparative Economic Systems
description: Course Description: Economics analysis of the relative virtues of capitalism and socialism, including welfare economics. Marxian exploitation theory, the socialist calculation debate (Hayek and Lange), alternative capitalist systems (Japan, Germany, U.S.) and contemporary models of market socialism.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (ECN 100 or ECN 100A or ECN 100AV or ARE 100A); (ECN 100B or ARE 100B).

title: Economics of International Immigration
description: Course Description: Introduction to research on the economics of immigration. Immigrant demographics and reasons for migrating. Immigrant success and effects on native populations. Influence of immigrants on those left behind. Discussion of migration policy in the US and other countries.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (ECN 001A or ECN 001AY or ECN 001AV); (ECN 001B or ECN 001BV).

title: Industrial Organization
description: Course Description: Appraisal of the role of competition and monopoly in the American economy; market structure, conduct, and economic performance of a variety of industries.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (ECN 100 or ECN 100A or ECN 100AV or ARE 100A); (ECN 100B or ARE 100B).

title: Industrial Organization
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Study of antitrust and economic regulation. Emphasis on applying theoretical models to U.S. industries and case studies, including telecommunications, software, and electricity markets. Topics include natural monopoly, optimal and actual regulatory mechanisms, deregulation, mergers, predatory pricing, and monopolization.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (ECN 100 or ECN 100A or ECN 100AV or ARE 100A); (ECN 100B or ARE 100B); ECN 102.

title: Theory of Games & Strategic Behavior
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Introduction to game theory. Explanation of the behavior of rational individuals with interacting and often conflicting interests. Non-cooperative and cooperative theory. Applications to economics, political science and other fields.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (MAT 016A, MAT 016B) or (MAT 021A, MAT 021B) or (MAT 017A, MAT 017B); or consent of instructor.

description: Course Description: Application of theoretical and empirical models to examine efficiency in energy production and use. Energy and environmental policy, market structure and power, global climate change, optimal regulation, and real-world applications; e.g., California electricity crisis.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECN 100 or ECN 100A or ECN 100AV or ARE 100A; consent of instructor.

title: Public Microeconomics
description: Course Description: Public expenditures; theory and applications. Efficiency and equity of competitive markets; externalities, public goods, and market failures; positive and normative aspects of public policy for expenditure, including benefit-cost analysis. Topics include consumer protection, pollution, education, poverty and crime.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (ECN 100A or ARE 100A); (ECN 100B or ARE 100B)

prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (ECN 100A or ECN 100AV or ARE 100A); (ECN 100B or ARE 100B).

description: Course Description: The health care market, emphasizing the role and use of economics. Individual demand, provision of services by doctors and hospitals, health insurance, managed care and competition, the role of government access to health care.

description: Course Description: Banks and the banking system. Uncertainty and asymmetric information in the lending process; efficiency of competitive equilibrium in lending markets. Regulation and the conduct of monetary policy.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (ECN 101 or ECN 101Y or ECN 101V); (STA 013 or STA 013Y or STA 032).

Possible topics include, business cycle theories, growth theory, monetary economics, political economics and theories of unemployment and inflation.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECN 101.

prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (ECN 100 or ECN 100A or ECN 100AV or ARE 100A); (ECN 100B or ARE 100B); ECN 101; (STA 013 or STA 013Y).

description: Course Description: Social problems and government responses in developed and developing countries; health, unemployment, informal insurance, poverty, corruption, and public service delivery; emphasis on quantitative and data analysis skills; applications to real world policy problems.

description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Problems of observation, estimation and hypotheses testing in economics through the study of the theory and application of linear regression models. Critical evaluation of selected examples of empirical research and exercises in applied economics.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (ECN 100 or ECN 100A or ECN 100AV or ARE 100A); (ECN 102 or STA 108); or consent of instructor.

description: Course Description: Introduction to economics and financial forecasting tools. Econometrics modeling methods and real-world data applications using the computing language R. Time-series techniques including auto-regression, random walk, and volatility forecasting. Financial econometrics topics including yield curve modeling and asset pricing.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (ECN 100 or ECN 100A or ECN 100AV); ECN 101; (ECN 102 or STA 108); or consent of instructor.

description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Introduction to economics and business data analytics. Statistical learning methods to analyze big data in economics and business data analysis. Statistical learning, linear regression, classification, resampling methods, and linear model selection and regularization.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (ECN 100 or ECN 100AV or ECN 100A); (ECN 102 B or better or ECN 140 B or better).

title: Economics & Business Data Analytics
description: Course Description: Introduction to economics and business data analytics. Statistical learning methods to analyze big data in economics and business data analysis. description: Course Description: Examination of fundamental problems of planning and financing transportation "infrastructure" (roads, ports, airports). Economics of the automobile industry, as well as the impact of government regulation and deregulation in the airlines and trucking industries. Intended for advanced Economics undergraduates.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (ECN 100 or ECN 100A or ECN 100AV or ARE 100A); (ECN 102 or STA 108); or consent of instructor.

title: Economics of the Labor Market
description: Course Description: Theory of labor supply and demand; determination of wages and employment in the labor market. Policy issues: labor force participation by married women; minimum wages and youth unemployment; effect of unions on wages.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECN 100 or ECN 100A or ECN 100AV or ARE 100A.

title: Economics of Human Resources
description: Course Description: Human resource analysis; introduction to human capital theory and economics of education; the basic theory of wage differentials, including theories of labor market discrimination; income distribution; poverty. Policy issues; negative income tax; manpower training programs; incomes policy.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECN 100 or ECN 100A or ECN 100AV or ARE 100A.

title: Economics of Education
description: Course Description: Application of theoretical and empirical tools of economics to the education sector. Demand for Education; Education Production and Market Structures in Education. Policy applications: class size reduction, school finance equalization, accountability, and school choice.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (ECN 100 C- or better or ECN 100A or ECN 100AV or ARE 100A C- or better); (ECN 100B or ARE 100B C- or better); ECN 102 C- or better; (STA 013 C- or better or STA 013Y C- or better or STA 032 C- or better); or consent of instructor.

Pricing of currencies, equities and bonds as well as intermediation and regulation of asset markets. Solving theoretical models, analyzing financial data, and presenting real-life case studies.

Conflict between the state and other subnational actors for legitimate political control. Field experiments and other methodology to understand the success of economic and policy tools in weak states.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (ECN 100A or ECN 100AV); ECN 100B.
