prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENG 035 C- or better; PHY 009B C- or better; (MAT 022B C- or better or MAT 027B C- or better).

title: Mechanics of Materials
description: Course Description: Uniaxial loading and deformation. General concepts of stress-strain-temperature relations and yield criteria. Torsion of shafts. Bending of beams. Deflections due to bending. Introduction to stability and buckling.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENG 035 C- or better; (MAT 022B C- or better or MAT 027B C- or better).

title: Mechanics of Materials
description: Course Description: Uniaxial loading and deformation. General concepts of stress-strain-temperature relations and yield criteria. Torsion of shafts. Bending of beams. Deflections due to bending. Introduction to stability and buckling.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENG 035 C- or better; (MAT 022B C- or better or MAT 027B C- or better).

title: Thermodynamics
description: Course Description: Fundamentals of thermodynamics: heat energy and work, properties of pure substances, First and Second Law for closed and open systems, reversibility, entropy, thermodynamic temperature scales. Applications of thermodynamics to engineering systems. May be taught abroad.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): PHY 009B C- or better; (MAT 022B C- or better or MAT 027B C- or better).

title: Engineering Economics
description: Course Description: Analysis of problems in engineering economy; the selection of alternatives; replacement decisions. Compounding, tax, origins and cost of capital, economic life, and risk and uncertainty are applied to methods of selecting most economic alternatives.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Upper division standing in Engineering.

title: Launching a Company
description: Course Description: Technological innovation and product development. Working as a team to turn ideas into companies through customer development.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENG 008 or consent of instructor.

title: Electric Machinery Fundamentals
description: Course Description: Principles of AC and DC electric motors and generators, their control systems and power sources. Selection of electric power equipment components based on their construction features and performance characteristics.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENG 017 C- or better or ENG 017V C- or better.

title: Fluid Power Actuators & Systems
description: Course Description: Hydraulic and pneumatic systems with emphasis on analysis and control of actuators. Design of hydraulic and pneumatic systems, specification and sizing of components, and selection of electro-hydraulics/electro-pneumatics, servo valves, and closed loop systems to solve basic control problems.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENG 100 C- or better; ENG 102 C- or better.

title: Introduction to Mechanical Vibrations
description: Course Description: Free and forced vibrations in lumped-parameter systems with and without damping; vibrations in coupled systems; electromechanical analogs; use of energy conservation principles.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENG 102 C- or better; (ENG 006 C- or better or ENG 005 C- or better or ECS 030 C- or better); ability to program in MATLAB.

title: Environmental Physics & Society
description: Course Description: Impact of humankind on the environment are discussed from the point of view of the physical sciences. Calculations based on physical principles will be made, and the resulting policy implications are considered.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PHY 009D or PHY 010 or PHY 001B); MAT 016A.

title: Engineering Analysis
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Solutions of systems of linear and nonlinear algebraic equations; approximation methods; solutions of ordinary differential equations; initial and boundary value problems; solutions of partial differential equations of Elliptic, parabolic, and hyperbolic types; Eigen value problems.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (ENG 006 C- or better or EME 005 C- or better or ECS 030 C- or better or ECS 032A C- or better or ECS 032AV C- or better or ECS 036A C- or better); ((MAT 021D C- or better, (MAT 022B C- or better or MAT 027B C- or better)).

title: Science & Technology of Sustainable Power Generation
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Focus on scientific understanding and development of power generation that is the basis of modern society. Concentration on power generation methods that are sustainable, in particular, discussion of the most recent innovations.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): PHY 007C or PHY 009C; upper division standing.

title: Professional Responsibilities of Engineers
description: Course Description: Organization of the engineering profession; introduction to contracts, specifications, business law, patents, and liability; discussion of professional, ethical, societal, and political issues related to engineering.

title: Directed Group Study
description: Course Description: Directed group study. May be taught abroad.

title: Technology Management
description: Course Description: Management of the engineering and technology activity. Functions of design, planning, production, marketing, sales, and maintenance. Technological product life cycle. Research and development activity. Project planning and organization. Manufacturing issues. Case studies.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Special Topics in Engineering
description: Course Description: Special topics in Engineering. Topics vary by instructor/quarter.

title: Introduction to Environmental Plants
description: Course Description: Classification, nomenclature and variation of environmental plants. The use of floral and vegetative characteristics and terminology to key unknown plants. Characteristics of plant groups and basics of climate, soils and plant selection. Identification of 150 common landscape plants.

title: Urban Forests are Nature-Based Solutions
description: Course Description: Principles and practices of planning and managing urban vegetation, forests, green infrastructures and nature-based solutions. Basics of tree/site monitoring, natural resource inventory, and development of long term urban forest management plans at both local and landscape scales.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): PLS 002 or BIS 002B or LDA 001.

title: Taxonomy & Ecology of Environmental Plant Families
description: Course Description: Classification and identification of introduced and native species used in urban forests, with emphasis on floral and vegetative characteristics of the prominent families of angiosperms and gymnosperms, adaptations to environmental variations in western landscapes, and horticultural classification.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENH 006; or consent of instructor.

title: Management of Container Media
description: Course Description: Principles of soil science and practices related to management of container media are taught, emphasizing appropriate use of soils and amendments, irrigation, and fertilizers.Physical and chemical properties are tested and effects of management on crops are evaluated in the laboratory.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): SSC 010 or SSC 100.

title: Greenhouse & Nursery Crop Production
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Principles and techniques for the production of ornamental greenhouse and nursery crops. Hands-on experience producing greenhouse crops. Optional weekend field trip.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): PLS 002.

title: Woody Plants in the Landscape: Growth, Ecology & Management
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Principles and practices of managing trees and shrubs in the urban landscape and other managed environments. Topics include woody plant form; growth response and adaptation; tree management in relation to soil, moisture, climate; plant problems.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): PLS 002 or BIS 002C.

title: Restoration Ecology
description: Course Description: Broad, interdisciplinary approach to effective restoration. Design and implementation of restoration projects based on principles of physiology, population, community, ecosystem and landscape ecology.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): SSC 112 C or better or ESP 100 C or better or ESM 144 C or better or PLS 162 C or better or PLS 163 C or better or PLS 130 C or better or PLS 144 C or better or PLS 147 C or better or PLS 160 C or better or ESP 121 C or better or ESP 127 C or better or ESP 155 C or better or EVE 101 C or better or EVE 104 C or better or EVE 117 C or better or EVE 119 C or better or EVE 181 C or better or PLB 117 C or better or ECL 200AN C or better or ECL 200BN C or better; or consent of instructor; or equivalent course in ecology/plant ecology.

title: Restoration Ecology Laboratory
description: Course Description: Companion field course to ENH 160. Design, implementation, and analysis of ecological monitoring of a site, followed by design of a restoration project for the site through work in ENH 160. Weekly field trips and classroom training on monitoring design and analysis.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENH 160 (can be concurrent); consent of instructor.

title: Introduction to Literature
description: Course Description: Introductory study of several genres of English literature, emphasizing both analysis of particular works and the range of forms and styles in English prose and poetry. Frequent writing assignments.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Completion of Entry Level Writing Requirement (ELWR); or concurrent enrollment in ENL 003A.

title: Writers' Workshop
description: Course Description: Focuses on the development of writing and revision strategies, exploring ways to understand a writing task; to develop appropriate content for a writing task; to revise content to reflect competence as a communicator.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Has not yet completed the Entry Level Writing Requirement and concurrent enrollment in ENL 003 or ENL 003V

title: Introduction to Literature
description: Course Description: Introductory study of several genres of English literature, emphasizing both analysis of particular works and the range of forms and styles in English prose and poetry. Frequent writing assignments will be made.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Completion of Entry Level Writing Requirement (ELWR); or concurrent enrollment in ENL 003A or ENL 003AV.

title: Introduction to Creative Writing: Fiction
description: Course Description: Elementary principles of writing fiction. Write both in prescribed forms and in experimental forms of their own choosing. No final examination.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Completion of Entry Level Writing Requirement (ELWR).

title: Introduction to Creative Writing: Non-Fiction
description: Course Description: Elementary principles of writing creative non-fiction. Work in prescribed literary forms (such as essay, meditation, biography, memoir, book review, documentary, or experimental non-fiction forms) and forms of students' choosing. No final examination.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Completion of Entry Level Writing Requirement (ELWR).

title: Introduction to Creative Writing: Poetry
description: Course Description: Elementary principles of writing poetry. Write both in prescribed forms and in experimental forms of their own choosing. No final examination.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Completion of Entry Level Writing Requirement (ELWR).

title: Literatures in English I: To 1700
description: Course Description: Historical introduction to English language and literature from 800-1700. Linguistic borrowing, innovation, and change. Emergence of key literary genres. Colonial America as a new site of English literary production and consumption.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y; or equivalent.

title: Literatures in English II: 1700-1900
description: Course Description: Historical introduction to English language and literature from 1700-1900. Linguistic borrowing, innovation, colonization, and change. Emergence and development of key literary genres. America, Britain, Ireland, Scotland, and India as important sites of English literary production and consumption.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y; or equivalent.

title: Literatures in English III: 1900 to Present
description: Course Description: Historical introduction to English language and literature from 1900-present. Linguistic borrowing, innovation, and change. Emergence and development of key literary genres. Formal experimentation. Modernism as transnational phenomenon.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y; or equivalent.

title: Introductory Topics in Literature
description: Course Description: Study of a special topic. Literature written in English in any period or place or genre. Thematic, formal, or temporal focus.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y; or equivalent.

title: Introductory Topics in Literature & Media
description: Course Description: Study of a topic centered on the relationships between literature and moving-image media.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (ENL 003 or ENL 003V) or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y); or equivalent.

title: Approaches to Reading
description: Course Description: Close reading and interpretation of literature from a variety of traditional and contemporary approaches. Topics include traditional textual and historical approaches; new criticism; formalism; psychological criticism; feminism and gender; reader-response; materialist approaches. Frequent written assignments.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (ENL 003 or ENL 003V) or (UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y); or equivalent.

title: Introductory Topics in Drama
description: Course Description: Close reading of, and topics relating to selected works of British and American drama from a range of historical periods.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y; or equivalent.

title: Introductory Topics in Fiction
description: Course Description: Close reading of, and topics relating to, British and American Fiction: short stories, novellas, novels.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (ENL 003 or ENL 003V) or (UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y); or equivalent.

title: Introductory Topics in Poetry
description: Course Description: Topical study and close reading of selections from English and American poetry.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (ENL 003 or ENL 003V) or (UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y); or equivalent.

title: Topics in Creative Writing
description: Course Description: Focus on topics in writing fiction, nonfiction/memoir, and poetry at an introductory level. Emphasis on guided practice in techniques of literary creative writing alongside examination of key themes and concerns for writers. Involves a mix of creative and literary-critical writing to develop competencies in these related areas.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y.

title: Careers in English
description: Course Description: Career options for English Majors. Career exploration and decision making using the design thinking process. Learn career tools and skills to help them articulate the application of their major into job skills.

title: Hot Bars, Supreme Lyrics, & Rhymes for Days: Hip Hop as Poetry
description: Course Description: Literary approaches to hip hop as poetry. Formal examination of rap lyrics in relation to technology, visual expression, dance, and knowledge production. Historical and cultural consideration of race, ethnicity, gender, urban culture, and politics.

title: Pop Culture Shakespeare
description: Course Description: Critical approaches to the study of Shakespeare’s afterlife in contemporary American media. Focus on visual, audio, and kinesthetic modes of analysis and presentation. Relation of Shakespeare to contemporary society, politics, media, and economics.

title: Youth in Revolt
description: Course Description: American literary and cultural representations of adolescence as a time of rebellious refusals and wild behaviors in relation to questions of gender and sexuality, psychological states, economic pressures, racial inequality, and methods of discipline and punishment.

title: Literature, Health, & Medicine
description: Course Description: Literary and cultural representations of health and medicine in relation to issues of race, gender, sexuality, and disability. Narratives and reading practices that provide complex accounts of the social determinants of health, contested understandings of illness, and critical perspectives on biomedicine.

title: Literary Animals
description: Course Description: Literary exploration of animals as spectacles, symbols, scientific objects, human companions, and speaking subjects through animal fables and allegories, tales for children, poems, realist narratives, essays, and experimental fictions.

title: Speculative Fictions
description: Course Description: Historical or thematic exploration of speculative genres such as science fiction, fantasy, utopia/dystopia, and/or horror. May include worldbuilding, intersections with scientific and other disciplinary knowledges, relation to social and political conditions.

title: Literatures of Climate Change
description: Course Description: Readings in English-language climate change literature from around the world. Narratives of climate migration, adaptation, and geopolitical upheaval. Use of innovative literary forms and genres for representing the scale and impacts of global warming. Comparison of scientific and fictional scenarios.

title: Racial Imaginaries
description: Course Description: Critical vocabularies and frameworks for studying the cultural construction of race as a signifier of human difference. Theories of racial formation in poetry, fiction, and other imaginative genres. Rhetorical and media strategies that present and/or obscure the material sources and institutional histories of race and racism.

title: Introduction to Games
description: Course Description: Introduction to the history, theory, and practice of play. Survey of both analog and digital games. Overview of gaming cultures, aesthetics, industries, and technologies.

title: Internship in English
description: Course Description: Internships in fields where students can practice their skills.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y; and consent of instructor.

title: Directed Group Study
description: Course Description: Directed group study.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y; and consent of instructor.

title: Student Facilitated Course
description: Course Description: Student facilitated course intended primarily for lower division students.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (ENL 003 or ENL 003V) or (UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y); consent of instructor.

title: Special Study for Undergraduates
description: Course Description: Special study for undergraduates.

title: Creative Writing: Fiction
description: Course Description: Writing of fiction. No final examination.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 005F or ENL 005P; ENL 005NF; and consent of instructor.

title: Creative Writing Advanced Fiction
description: Course Description: Development and evaluation of students' work in prose, primarily in the workshop format. Some reading and discussion of published novels and short stories. Conferences with individual students once per quarter.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 100F.

title: Creative Writing Advanced Non-Fiction
description: Course Description: Development and evaluation of students’ work in non-fiction, primarily in the workshop format. Reading and discussion of published works of non-fiction. Conferences with individual students once per quarter.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 100NF or ENL 100P or ENL 100F; consent of instructor.

title: Creative Writing: Non-Fiction
description: Course Description: Writing of non-fiction. No final examination.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 005F or ENL 005P or ENL 005NF; and consent of instructor.

title: Creative Writing: Poetry
description: Course Description: Writing of poetry.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 005F or ENL 005P or ENL 005NF; and consent of instructor.

title: Creative Writing Advanced Poetry
description: Course Description: Development and evaluation of students' work in poetry, primarily in the workshop format. Some reading and discussion of published works of poetry. Conferences with individual students once per quarter.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 100P.

title: History of the English Language
description: Course Description: History of the English language. Examination of the language as recorded from Old English to present-day English. Relationship of English to other languages; development of vocabulary, phonology, and grammatical patterns.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (ENL 003 or ENL 003V) or (UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y); or the equivalent.

title: English Grammar
description: Course Description: Survey of present-day English grammar as informed by contemporary linguistic theories. The major syntactic structures of English; their variation across dialects, styles, and registers; their development; and their usefulness in describing the conventions of English.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or LIN 001 or LIN 001Y or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y; or consent of instructor.

title: English Grammar Practicum
description: Course Description: Practice in teaching the principles of grammar to the kinds of audiences teachers encounter in California. Discussions with teachers who teach in these areas. Examination of pedagogical research on teaching grammar.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 106; LIN 106 (can be concurrent).

title: Freedom of Expression
description: Course Description: Historical development of fundamental issues and contemporary controversies about freedom of expression, with emphasis on literary and artistic censorship.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or (UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y); or the equivalent.

title: Introduction to Literary Theory
description: Course Description: Key theoretical terms, concepts, and thinkers from the Greeks to the modern era.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y.

title: Introduction to Modern Literary & Critical Theory
description: Course Description: History of literary criticism in the modern era, with emphasis on the ties with the past and the special problems presented by modern literary theory.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y.

title: Topics in Medieval Literature
description: Course Description: Historically or thematically focused intensive examination of selected topics in Medieval British literature.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y; or the equivalent.

title: Chaucer: Troilus & the "Minor" Poems
description: Course Description: Development of the poet's artistry and ideas from his first work to his masterpiece, "Troilus and Criseyde"
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y; or the equivalent.

title: Chaucer: The Canterbury Tales
description: Course Description: Literary analysis of the complete "Canterbury Tales." Courtly love, literary forms, medieval science and astrology, theology and dogma as they inform the reading of Chaucer's work.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y; or the equivalent.

title: Topics in 16th- & 17th-Century Literature
description: Course Description: Historically or thematically focused study of works of the Renaissance.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y.

title: Shakespeare
description: Course Description: Historically, generically, or thematically focused study of Shakespeare's works.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y; or equivalent.

title: Law & Literature
description: Course Description: Historically, thematically, or generically focused study of the relationship between law and literature.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y; or the equivalent.

title: Milton
description: Course Description: Selected major works, including Paradise Lost.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y.

title: Topics in Irish Literature
description: Course Description: Intensive study or treatment of special topics relating to the emergence, invention, and re-invention of Irish literature.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y; or equivalent.

title: Food & Literature
description: Course Description: English-language literatures of food from around the world. Literary cultures of food as they relate to broader cultural practices, ethnic and racial identities, place and diaspora. Literary genres and forms of food writing.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y or COM 001 or COM 002 or COM 003 or COM 004 or NAS 005 or NAS 005V.

title: British Romantic Literature
description: Course Description: Historically or thematically focused study of works of Romantic English literature.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y; or the equivalent.

title: 19th-Century British Literature
description: Course Description: Historically or thematically focused study of works of 19th-century English literature.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y; or the equivalent.

title: British Literature, 1900-1945
description: Course Description: Historically or thematically focused study of works of British literature (drama, poetry, prose fiction) from the period between 1900 and the end of World War II.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y; or the equivalent.

title: British Literature, 1945 to Present
description: Course Description: Historically or thematically focused study of works of British literature (drama, poetry, prose fiction) from the period between 1945 and the present.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y; or the equivalent.

title: Topics in Global Literatures & Cultures
description: Course Description: Historically or regionally focused study of world literatures in English (other than the national literatures of British Isles and the United States), particularly from post-colonial regions in Africa, the Caribbean, and Asia, and immigrant cultures in the English-speaking world.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y.

title: Topics in Postcolonial Literatures & Cultures
description: Course Description: Study of postcolonial literature of Anglophone colonies. Specific emphases may include literature from and about Anglophone India, the Caribbean, the Middle East, South Asia, Africa, and/or South America.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y; or the equivalent.

title: Topics in Diasporic Literatures & Migration
description: Course Description: Study of literatures, histories, and cultures of one or more diasporic groups.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y; or the equivalent.

title: Early American Literature
description: Course Description: Historically or thematically focused study of American literature of the 17th and 18th centuries.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y; or the equivalent.

title: 19th-Century American Literature to the Civil War
description: Course Description: Historically or thematically focused study of works of 19th-century American literature.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y; or the equivalent.

title: Post-Civil War American Literature
description: Course Description: Historically or thematically focused study of works of post-Civil War American literature.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y; or the equivalent.

title: Reading Race, Class, & Gender
description: Course Description: Readings in literature and culture that present race, class, gender, sexuality, ability, and citizenship as inter-articulated signifiers of difference. Literature is contextualized alongside works of sociology, history, philosophy, and law. Inter-discursive nature of racial thought and policy, and on historical and rhetorical practices through which race acquires meaning.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or COM 001 or COM 002 or COM 003 or COM 004 or NAS 005 or NAS 005V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y.

title: American Literature 1900-1945
description: Course Description: Historically or thematically focused study of American literature (drama, poetry, prose fiction) from the period between 1900 and the end of World War II.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y; or the equivalent.

title: American Literature, 1945 to the Present
description: Course Description: Historically or thematically focused study of American literature (drama, poetry, prose fiction) from the period between 1945 and the present.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y; or equivalent.

title: Topics in Literature
description: Course Description: Intensive examination of literature considered in topical terms, not necessarily historically.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y.

title: British Drama to 1800
description: Course Description: Historically or thematically focused study of works of English drama prior to 1800.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y; or the equivalent.

title: Drama from 1800 to the Present
description: Course Description: Historically or thematically focused study of works of British drama from 1800 to the present.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y; or the equivalent.

title: Topics in Drama
description: Course Description: Historical or thematic study of drama.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y.

title: The Graphic Novel
description: Course Description: Thematically, historically, and formally focused study of the graphic novel genre. Contents may include any regional, national, or transnational group of graphic novels.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y; or equivalent courses.

title: 18th-Century British Novel
description: Course Description: Historically or thematically organized examination of the 18th-century British novel, with particular emphasis on its evolution, including the epistolary novel, the picaresque novel, and the Gothic novel: Richardson, Fielding, Sterne, Austen.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y; or the equivalent.

title: 19th-Century British Novel
description: Course Description: Historically or thematically organized examination of 19th-century British novelists, with emphasis on the historical novel, the social novel, and novels by women: Scott, Dickens, the Brontes, Eliot, Hardy.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y; or the equivalent.

title: 20th-Century British Novel
description: Course Description: Historically or thematically organized examination of the 20th-century British novel, with emphasis on impressionism; the revolt against naturalism; the experimental novel; the anti-modernist reaction: Conrad, Joyce, Woolf, Lawrence, Drabble, Rhys.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y; or the equivalent.

title: The Short Story
description: Course Description: The short story as a genre; its historical development, techniques, and formal character as a literary form. European as well as American writers.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y.

title: Detective Fiction
description: Course Description: Historically, formally, and thematically focused study of novels and short stories in the detective fiction genre.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y.

title: The American Novel to 1900
description: Course Description: Historically or thematically organized examination of the rise and development of the American novel from its beginnings; Hawthorne, Melville, Twain, James, and others.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y; or the equivalent.

title: The American Novel from 1900 to the Present
description: Course Description: Historically or thematically organized examination of important American novelists from 1900 to the present: authors may include Willa Cather, Nathanael West, William Faulkner, Ralph Ellison, Zora Neale Hurston, Thomas Pynchon, Ishmael Reed, Maria Helena Viramontes, Rachel Kushner, and others.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y; or the equivalent.

title: Topics in the Novel
description: Course Description: Examination of major novels arranged thematically. Topics might include Bildungsroman, stream-of-consciousness novel, Gothic novel, historical novel.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y.

title: Film as Narrative
description: Course Description: Study of modern film (1930 to present) as a storytelling medium.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y.

title: Film Theory & Criticism
description: Course Description: Film theory and criticism, with a study of ten major works of international film art.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y.

title: Literary Study in the British Isles
description: Course Description: Literary Study in the British Isles: On-site study of the literature, film, and/or performance of the British Isles.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y.

title: Writing Science
description: Course Description: Texts and writing practices in the production of scientific knowledge. Surveys the literary structure of scientific arguments; history of scientific genres; rhetoric and semiotics in scientific culture; graphical systems in the experimental laboratory; narratives of science, including science fiction.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (ENL 003 or ENL 003V); STS 001; or equivalent.

title: Topics in Poetry
description: Course Description: Intensive examination of various topics expressed in poetry from all periods of English and American literature.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y); ENL 045.

title: Love & Desire in Contemporary American Poetry
description: Course Description: Close reading of contemporary American poems on the theme of love and desire by poets of diverse ethnicities and of gay, lesbian, and heterosexual orientations.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y.

title: 20th-Century African American Poetry
description: Course Description: African American poetry in the 20th-century, including oral and literary traditions. Authors covered may include Gwendolyn Brooks, Countee Cullen, Robert Hayden, and Langston Hughes.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V; UWP 001Y; or the equivalent.

title: 20th-Century American Poetry
description: Course Description: Historical Study of American poetry since 1900, with thematic and formal focus at the instructor's discretion.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y; or the equivalent.

title: Memoir & Life-Writing
description: Course Description: English-language memoir and life-writing across various periods. Literary cultures of memoir as they relate to questions of identity, social situation, and broader ideologies of self and culture.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y.

title: Narrative Game Design
description: Course Description: Narrative game design. Character, dialogue, environmental storytelling, procedurally-generated text, play as narrative. Workshop, critique, and publish narrative and text-based games.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 072 or CDM 072 or ENL 172 or CDM 172 or STS 172; or consent of instructor.

title: Game Studies Seminar
description: Course Description: Theory and methods for researching games, play, media, and culture. Reading, writing, and discussion about playable media.

title: The Bible as Literature: The Old Testament
description: Course Description: Selected readings from the Old Testament illustrating various literary forms. Emphasis on the Pentateuch, the Historical Books, and the Wisdom Books. May be taken independently of ENL 171B.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y.

title: The Bible as Literature: Prophets & New Testament
description: Course Description: Selected readings from the Old Testament prophets and the New Testament. May be taken independently of ENL 171A.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y.

title: Science Fiction
description: Course Description: Literary modes and methods of science fiction. Representative texts, authors, and themes of the genre, e.g., time travel, alternative universes, and utopias. Relations of science fiction to science, philosophy, and culture.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or STS 001; or equivalent.

title: The Bible as Literature
description: Course Description: Selected biblical readings considered as literary works; readings of later anglophone literature influenced by the Bible.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y.

title: Study of an Individual Author
description: Course Description: In-depth study of an author's works; historical context; relation to predecessors and contemporaries; critical reception; influence.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 110A or ENL 110B.

title: Topics in Nations, Regions, & Other Cultural Geographies
description: Course Description: Study of a local, regional, national, transnational, or other geographical literary formation (e.g., Global South; Literatures of the U.S./Mexico border; Literatures of the Pacific; Indigenous North American literature) centering underrepresented racial and/or ethnic perspectives.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y or COM 001 or COM 002 or COM 003 or COM 004 or NAS 005 or NAS 005V.

title: Topics in Comparative Racial & Ethnic Literary Studies
description: Course Description: Writings by Black, Indigenous, and/or People-of-Color authors responding to specific American contexts of racialization such as slavery, imperial warfare, international and internal migration, naturalization law, religion, mass incarceration, and environmental injustice.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y or COM 001 or COM 002 or COM 003 or COM 004 or NAS 005 or NAS 005V; Or standing above freshman level.

title: Children's Literature
description: Course Description: Historical backgrounds and development of types of children's literature, folklore and oral tradition, levels of interest, criticism and evaluation, illustration and bibliography.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y.

title: African American Literature to 1900
description: Course Description: African American literature from the colonial period to 1900. Particular attention to the rapid development of the African American literary culture from a primarily oral tradition to various literary genres, including the slave narrative.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y.

title: African American Literature 1900-Present
description: Course Description: Major African American writers in the context of cultural history from 1900 to the present. Writers may include Richard Wright, Ann Petry, James Baldwin, Ralph Ellison, Paule Marshall, Toni Morrison, Alice Walker, Clarence Major.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y.

title: Literature of California
description: Course Description: Focus is on the diverse contributions to the rise of California literature from the 19th century to today. Reading of poetry, fiction, and essays. Includes Native American, Latino/a, and other Californian writers.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y.

title: Young Adult Literature
description: Course Description: Theoretical, critical, and literary issues informing the study and teaching of American young adult literature.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y; or equivalent.

title: Literature & the Environment
description: Course Description: Historical and/or thematic survey of topics in writing about the environment.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y.

title: Literature by Women Before 1800
description: Course Description: Women's Writing in English before 1800; organized by period, place, genre, or theme.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y or COM 001 or COM 002 or COM 003 or COM 004 or NAS 005 or NAS 005V.

title: Literature by Women from 1800-1900
description: Course Description: Women's Writing in English from 1800 to 1900; organized by period, place, genre, or theme.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y or COM 001 or COM 002 or COM 003 or COM 004 or NAS 005.

title: Literature by Women after 1900
description: Course Description: Women's Writing in English after 1900; organized by period, place, genre, or theme.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y or COM 001 or COM 002 or COM 003 or COM 004 or NAS 005 or NAS 005V.

title: Literature, Sexuality, & Gender
description: Course Description: Historically or thematically focused intensive examinations of gender and sexuality in British and American literature.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y; or the equivalent.

title: Topics in Literature & Media
description: Course Description: Group study of a topic centered on the relationships between literature and film or other moving-image media.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 110A or ENL 110B; and consent of instructor.

title: Topics in Literary & Critical Theory
description: Course Description: Intensive examination of theories addressing a particular problem, topic, or question.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 110A or ENL 110B; and consent of instructor.

title: Seminar in Literary Studies
description: Course Description: Intensive, focused study of literature at an advanced level. May be organized by topic, author, period, movement, or genre. High participation.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 110A or ENL 110B.

title: Internship in English
description: Course Description: Internships in fields where students can practice their skills. A maximum of 4 units is allowed toward the major in English.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y.

title: Seminar for Honors Students
description: Course Description: Preparation for writing an honors thesis in ENL 195H. High level of participation expected.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 110A or ENL 110B; One advanced study course; admission to English Department Senior Honors Program in Literature, Criticism, and Theory.

title: Honors Thesis
description: Course Description: Preparation of a thesis, under the supervision of an instructor. Students satisfying requirements for the general major or the teaching emphasis write on a scholarly or critical subject; creative writing students submit a volume of poems or fiction.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 194H.

title: Tutoring in English
description: Course Description: Leading of small voluntary discussion groups designed to develop reading and writing skills and affiliated with one of the university's regular courses.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Upper division standing and consent of Chairperson.

title: Community Tutoring in English
description: Course Description: Field experience, with individuals or in classroom in instruction of English language, literature, and composition. Does not fulfill requirement for major.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Upper division standing and a major in English; consent of Chairperson.

title: Directed Group Study
description: Course Description: Directed group study.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or ENL 005F or ENL 005P or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y.

title: Student Facilitated Course
description: Course Description: Student facilitated course intended primarily for upper division students.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y; consent of instructor.

title: Directed Group Study
description: Course Description: Group study closely tied to the texts and periods studied in ENL 163S. Investigations of historical sites, museums, galleries, and performances.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 163S (can be concurrent); and consent of instructor; ENL 163S required concurrently.

title: Special Study for Advanced Undergraduates
description: Course Description: Special study for advanced undergraduates.

title: Student Facilitated Course Development
description: Course Description: Under the supervision of a faculty member, an undergraduate student plans and develops the course they will offer under 098F/198F.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y; consent of instructor.

title: Student Facilitated Teaching
description: Course Description: STU FAC. Under the supervision of a faculty member, an undergraduate student teaches a course under 098F/198F.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 003 or ENL 003V or UWP 001 or UWP 001V or UWP 001Y; consent of instructor.

title: Introduction to Graduate Studies in English
description: Course Description: Introduction to literary scholarship with special attention to the elements of professionalism and to different modes of literary investigation.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing.

title: Anglo-Saxon Language & Culture
description: Course Description: The language and culture of Anglo-Saxon England; readings in Old English prose and poetry.

title: Beowulf
description: Course Description: A study of the poem and the Heroic Age of Germanic literature.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 205; or the equivalent.

title: Middle English
description: Course Description: Study of the phonology, morphology, syntax, and lexicon between 1100 and 1500 with investigation of the regional dialects; pertinent facts on both the internal and external linguistic history; intensive reading of texts.

title: Readings in English & American Literature
description: Course Description: Content varies according to specialty of instructor.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Upper division course in area studied.

title: Topics in Irish Literature
description: Course Description: Varied topics, including the 19th-century novel, contemporary Irish poetry, rise of the drama, or a study of a major author.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing.

title: Study of a Major Writer
description: Course Description: Artistic development of one major writer and his intellectual and literary milieu.

title: Problems in English Literature
description: Course Description: Selected issues in the current study and critical assessment of a limited period or topic in English literature.

title: Problems in American Literature
description: Course Description: Selected topics for intensive investigation.

title: Dramatic Literature
description: Course Description: Historical introduction to dramatic theory; the genres of tragedy, comedy, and tragicomedy.

title: Theory of Fiction
description: Course Description: Theories of fiction as they relate to the professional writer's practice of the craft. For students in the Creative Writing Program.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing.

title: Seminar for Writers
description: Course Description: Varied topics in the study of literature and literary culture craft and poetics from the perspective of the writer/practitioner.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing.

title: Special Topics in Literary Theory
description: Course Description: Advanced topics in literary theory and criticism. Preparation and evaluation of research paper.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENL 237; or the equivalent.

title: Medieval Literature
description: Course Description: Studies of Medieval literature. Course materials to be selected by the instructor. Preparation and evaluation of research papers.

title: 16th-Century Literature
description: Course Description: Studies in 16th-century literature. Course materials to be selected by the instructor. Preparation and evaluation of research papers.

title: Shakespeare
description: Course Description: Studies in Shakespeare. Course materials to be selected by the instructor. Preparation and evaluation of research papers.

title: 17th-Century Literature
description: Course Description: Studies in 17th-century literature. Course materials to be selected by the instructor. Preparation and evaluation of research papers.

title: 18th-Century Literature
description: Course Description: Studies in 18th-century literature. Course materials to be selected by the instructor. Preparation and evaluation of research papers.

title: Romantic Literature
description: Course Description: Studies in Romantic literature. Course materials to be selected by the instructor. Preparation and evaluation of research papers.

title: Victorian Literature
description: Course Description: Studies in Victorian literature. Course materials to be selected by the instructor. Preparation and evaluation of research papers.

title: 20th-Century British Literature
description: Course Description: Studies in 20th-century British literature. Course materials to be selected by the instructor. Preparation and evaluation of research papers.

title: Early American Literature
description: Course Description: Studies in Early American literature. Course materials to be selected by the instructor. Preparation and evaluation of research papers.

title: American Literature: 1800 to the Civil War
description: Course Description: Studies in American literature from 1800 to Civil War. Course materials to be selected by the instructor. Preparation and evaluation of research papers.

title: American Literature: Civil War to 1914
description: Course Description: Studies in American literature from the Civil War to 1914. Course materials to be selected by the instructor. Preparation and evaluation of research papers.

title: American Literature after 1914
description: Course Description: Studies in American literature after 1914. Course materials to be selected by the instructor. Preparation and evaluation of research papers.

title: Studies in Modern British & American Literature
description: Course Description: Studies in modern British and American literature. Course materials to be selected by the instructor. Preparation and evaluation of research papers.

title: Studies in Contemporary World Literature
description: Course Description: Emerging global, international or transnational techniques, theories, and individual works of contemporary world prose or poetry. Discussion, seminar reports, research papers.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing, consent of instructor, with preference given to those enrolled in the masters program in Creative Writing.

title: Proseminar in Research Practices
description: Course Description: Study of various practical and technical skills needed to perform research in literary studies. Materials to be selected by the instructor. Evaluation based on student projects that involve hands-on application of skills taught in the proseminar.

title: Seminar in Research Practices
description: Course Description: Study of various practical and technical skills needed to perform research in literary studies. Course materials to be selected by the instructor. Evaluation based on student projects that involve hands-on application of skills taught in the seminar.

title: Literature by Women
description: Course Description: Studies in literature by women and the theoretical approaches to literature by women. Course materials to be selected by the instructor. Preparation and evaluation of research papers.

title: Topics in Literature & Media
description: Course Description: Study of a topic centered on film or other moving-image media. Course materials to be selected by the instructor. Preparation and evaluation of research papers.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing.

title: Prospectus Workshop
description: Course Description: Training in writing the dissertation prospectus. Participation in group discussions of preparatory assignments and final proposal.

title: Article Writing Workshop
description: Course Description: Training in preparing an article for publication. Participation in group discussions of article drafts.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Creative Writing: Special Topic
description: Course Description: Writing that falls outside the generic confines of traditional genres (fiction, poetry, and nonfiction) or traditional workshop formats. Evaluation of written materials and individual student conferences.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Creative Writing: Fiction
description: Course Description: Writing of prose fiction. Evaluation of written materials and individual student conferences.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor. Graduate standing, with preference given to those enrolled in master's program in Creative Writing.

title: Creative Writing: Non-Fiction
description: Course Description: Writing of literary non-fiction, with emphasis on autobiography, biography, memoir, the occasional or nature essay, or other non-fiction prose narratives.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor. Graduate standing; with preference given to those enrolled in the master's program in Creative Writing.

title: Creative Writing: Poetry
description: Course Description: Writing of poetry. Evaluation of written materials and individual student conferences.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor. Graduate standing, with preference given to those enrolled in master's program in Creative Writing.

title: Directed Group Study
description: Course Description: Directed group study.

title: Individual Study
description: Course Description: Individual study.

title: Special Study for the Doctoral Dissertation
description: Course Description: Special study for the doctoral dissertation.

title: Teaching Creative Writing
description: Course Description: Designed for new instructors of ENL 005F or ENL 005P; discussion of ways to facilitate creative writing workshops and to respond to student manuscripts.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing; appointment as Teaching Assistant in the English Department.

title: Teaching Literature & Composition
description: Course Description: Designed for new instructors of ENL 003 or the equivalent courses; discussion of problems related to teaching literature and composition to lower division students.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing; appointment as Teaching Assistant in the English Department.

title: Teaching Assistant Training Practicum
description: Course Description: Teaching assistant training.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing.

title: Internship in Endocrinology
description: Course Description: Supervised work experience in endocrinology.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Upper division standing; approval of project by preceptor prior to internship.

title: Research
description: Course Description: Endocrinology research.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Endocrinology Clinical Clerkship
description: Course Description: Participation with members of subspecialty service in the initial evaluation, work-up, management and follow-up of patients with endocrinologic disorders.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): IMD 430; and/or consent of instructor.

title: Clinical Nutrition Clerkship
description: Course Description: In-depth experience in assessment and monitoring of nutritional support of patients whose illnesses are complicated by malnutrition and of patients with problems in under-nutrition due to various illnesses.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): IMD 430; consent of instructor of record.

title: Insights in Endocrinology
description: Course Description: First- or second-year students observe in morning Endocrine and Diabetes clinics; attend bi-weekly noon and afternoon endocrine conferences. Present brief endocrine physiology oral presentation to the endocrine group.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor; student in good academic standing.

title: Research
description: Course Description: Research.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Art, Science & the World of Insects
description: Course Description: Fusion of entomology and art to create an appreciation of insect biology, ecology, interactions with humans and importance in human culture. Multidisciplinary approaches in education and career paths in entomology and art will be highlighted.

title: Biodiversity
description: Course Description: Introduction to nature, scope and geographical distribution of biodiversity (diversity of life, with emphasis on plants and animals, especially insects). Humans and biodiversity; domestication, aesthetics, ethics and valuation. Species richness and "success." Biodiversity through time; monitoring, evaluation and conservation. Biomes-global, continental and Californian.

title: Natural History of Insects
description: Course Description: Introduction to the insects detailing their great variety, structures and functions, habits, and their significance in relation to plants and animals including man. Designed for students not specializing in entomology.

title: Special Topics in Entomology
description: Course Description: Freshman seminar course for indepth examination of a special topic within the subject area.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Internship
description: Course Description: Work-learn experience on and off campus in all subject areas offered by the department, supervised by a member of the faculty.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Special Study for Undergraduates
description: Course Description: Special study for undergraduates.

title: General Entomology
description: Course Description: Biology, anatomy, physiology, development, classification, ecology and relation of insects to human welfare.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): BIS 002B; or consent of instructor.

title: General Entomology Laboratory
description: Course Description: Anatomy, development, population ecology, methods of collecting, classification and identification of insects of all orders and of major families.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENT 100 (can be concurrent).

title: Functional Insect Morphology
description: Course Description: Study of the basic external and internal structures, organs and tissues of insects, with emphasis on functional systems. Functional anatomy, histology and fine structures of important organs and tissues will be discussed.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENT 100.

title: Insect Physiology
description: Course Description: Processes by which insects maintain themselves, reproduce, and adapt to environment. Insects as models for basic/applied research through detailed analysis of metabolic, physiological, and behavioral processes. Emphasis on analysis of methodology, fact, and theory.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENT 100; or course in physiology or invertebrate zoology.

title: Insects Systematics
description: Course Description: Principles and methods of systematics, with particular reference to insects. Emphasis on different theories of classification, and analysis of phylogenetic relationships.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Introductory course in zoology or entomology.

title: Behavioral Ecology of Insects
description: Course Description: Basic principles and mechanisms of insect behavior and ecology. An evolutionary approach to understanding behavioral ecology of insects.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Introductory biology or zoology.

title: Insect Ecology
description: Course Description: Introduction to insect ecology combining fundamental concepts and questions in ecology with ideas, hypotheses and insights from insects. Integrates aspects of individual, population, community and ecosystem ecology.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): BIS 002B (can be concurrent); or consent of instructor.

title: California Insect Diversity
description: Course Description: Survey of the diversity of insects from selected ecological zones in California with emphasis on collection, identification, and natural history.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): An introductory course in entomology.

title: Evolution & Ecology of Arachnids
description: Course Description: Spider external morphology, functional anatomy, metabolism, neurobiology, development, predatory & reproductive behavior, ecology, phylogeny, systematics, zoogeography, and faunistics.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): BIS 002C or ENT 100

title: Evolution & Ecology of Arachnids Laboratory
description: Course Description: Laboratory on spider natural history, identification, external morphology, and taxonomy, morphology and identification. Emphasis on the spiders of the California Floristic Province.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (BIS 002C or ENT 100 or ENT 100L); ENT 108 (can be concurrent); ENT 108 required concurrently or consent of instructor.

title: Field Taxonomy & Ecology
description: Course Description: Study of insects in their natural habitats; their identification and ecology.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): An introductory course in entomology or consent of instructor.

title: Arthropod Pest Management
description: Course Description: Sustainable management & ecology of arthropod pests in agricultural systems. Techniques for effective, management of arthropod pests via in-depth knowledge about pest biology, life strategies, spatial ecology, and food web functions. Lab & field trip exercises in applied research in pest management, ecology, and evolution using digital technologies (apps, remote sensing, cloud-based decision support).
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (STA 013 or STA 013Y or STA 100 C- or better); BIS 002B C- or better; or consent of instructor.

title: Chemical Ecology
description: Course Description: Fundamental concepts in chemical ecology, classic and recent examples of chemical-mediated species interactions; introduction to the methods and techniques used in the field of chemical ecology.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): BIS 002B C- or better; CHE 002A C- or better; CHE 002B C- or better; or consent of instructor.

title: Longevity
description: Course Description: Nature, origin, determinants, and limits of longevity with particular reference to humans; emphasis on implications of findings from non-human model systems including natural history, ecology and evolution of life span; description of basic demographic techniques including life table methods.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Upper division standing or consent of instructor.

title: Apiculture
description: Course Description: Biology and behavior of honeybees; communication, orientation, social organization, foraging activities, honey production, pollination activities.

title: Pollination Biology
description: Course Description: Natural history, ecology, evolution and applications of pollination. Conceptual underpinnings and basic methodologies of pollination. Primary focus on animal-mediated pollination.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): BIS 002B C- or better; or consent of instructor. EVE 100 & EVE 101 recommended.

title: Plant-Virus-Vector Interaction
description: Course Description: Analysis of interactions necessary for viruses to infect plants.Interactions among insect vectors and host plants involved in the plant-virus life cycle. Evolutionary aspects of the molecular components in viral infection and modern approaches to the interdiction of viral movement.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): BIS 002A; (BIS 101 or BIS 101V); PLB 105, PLP 120, and ENT 100 recommended.

title: Introduction to Biological Control
description: Course Description: Introduction to biological control.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENT 100 or ENT 110.

title: Medical Entomology
description: Course Description: Basic biology and classification of medically important arthropods with special emphasis on the ecology of arthropodborne diseases and principles of their control. Relationships of arthropods to human health.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): BIS 002A; BIS 002B; or consent of instructor; upper division standing in one of the biological sciences.

title: Lab Course in Medical Entomology
description: Course Description: Medical entomology and vector control; physiology and identification of disease vectors; methods for trapping and sampling mosquitoes and ticks; techniques for testing insecticide resistance; utilization of online resources for epidemiological data analysis; inclusion of field trips for mosquito and tick collections and visits to vector control districts for practical insights.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENT 153 C or better; or consent of instructor.

title: Biology of Parasitism
description: Course Description: Lectures on the biological and ecological aspects affecting host-parasite relationships using selected examples from protozoan and metazoan fauna.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): BIS 002A; or consent of instructor.

title: Biology of Parasitism Laboratory
description: Course Description: Laboratory demonstrations using selected examples of protozoan and metazoan organisms along with various techniques used in parasitology to exemplify concepts presented in the lecture course.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENT 156 (can be concurrent); ENT 156 required concurrently or consent of instructor.

title: Forensic Entomology
description: Course Description: Arthropods, their general biology, succession, developmental cycles and population biology in matters of criminal prosecution and civil litigation. Emphasis on basic arthropod biology, ecological and developmental concepts and methods, development of reasoning abilities, implication, development of opinions and evidence.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENT 100; or consent of instructor; upper division standing.

title: Experimental Ecology & Evolution in the Field
description: Course Description: Experimental design in field ecology. Examination of primary literature, experimental design, independent and collaborative research, analysis of data, development of original research paper based on field experiments.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): EVE 100 (can be concurrent); (ENT 105 (can be concurrent) or ESP 100 (can be concurrent) or EVE 101 (can be concurrent)); consent of instructor.

title: Experimental Ecology & Evolution in the Field
description: Course Description: Experimental design in field ecology. Examination of primary literature, experimental design, independent and collaborative research, analysis of data, development of original research paper based on field experiments.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): EVE 180A or ENT 180A.

title: Internship
description: Course Description: Laboratory experience or fieldwork off and on campus in all subject areas offered in the Department of Entomology. Internships supervised by a member of the faculty.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor; completion of 84 units.

title: Tutoring in Entomology
description: Course Description: Leading small discussion groups. Preview assignments and prepare guidelines for discussion.

title: Directed Group Study
description: Course Description: Directed group study.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Special Study for Advanced Undergraduates
description: Course Description: Special study for advanced undergraduates.

title: Molecular Biology of Insects & Insect Viruses
description: Course Description: Molecular biological analysis of insect systematics, physiology, and defense mechanisms. Molecular biology of insect viruses. Baculovirus expresion vectors and post-translation modification of expressed polypeptides. Biological control of using neuropeptides and toxin genes in insect viruses.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Terrestrial Field Ecology
description: Course Description: Field course conducted over spring break and four weekends at Bodega Bay, emphasizing student projects. Ecological hypothesis testing, data gathering, analysis, and written and oral presentation of results are stressed.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Introductory ecology and introductory statistics or consent of instructor.

title: Advanced Biological Control
description: Course Description: Principles and current issues in biological control of arthropod pests and weeds; laboratory devoted to identification and life history of the major groups of parasitic and predaceous arthropods.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate or upper division standing in biological science or consent of instructor.

title: Advanced Medical Entolomogy
description: Course Description: An analysis of several anthropod-borne human diseases with emphasis on the relationships of the biology of the vector to the ecology of the disease. Discussion includes demonstration of vectors and techniques.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): One upper division ENT course (other than ENT 153) and one course in Microbiology; ENT 153 strongly recommended.

title: Exploratory Topics in Entomology
description: Course Description: Interdisciplinary topics in entomology, including innovative applications of entomological concepts to other fields of research and human endeavor (e.g. medicine, technology, art, criminology).

title: Current Topics in Medical & Veterinary Entomology
description: Course Description: Discussions of parasitology, ecology and epidemiology related to vectors of pathogens causing disease in humans and animals.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENT 153.

title: Current Topics in Insect Physiology & Behavior
description: Course Description: Analysis of contemporary advances in insect physiology, biochemistry and/or behavior. Interpretation and description of physiological and behavioral mechanisms and functions. Application of general principles to solution of problems in the laboratory and field.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENT 102 if topic is physiology, a course in behavior if topic is behavior, or either if topic bridges both.

title: Current Topics in Insect Biotechnology & Genomics
description: Course Description: Discussion of advances in insect biotechnology, including genetic engineering and genomics.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENT 212.

title: Current Topics in Insect Ecology, Evolution, & Systematics
description: Course Description: Discussions of advanced topics in ecology, evolution and systematics with emphasis on analysis of factors influencing the distribution, abundance, adaptations and evolutionary relationships of insects. Includes consideration of applications of basic theory; e.g. biological control.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENT 103; general course in ecology or evolution.

title: Current Topics in Agricultural Entomology & Bee Biology
description: Course Description: Discussion of advanced topics about the biology, ecology, behavior, and management of pest and beneficial insects.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENT 110 if topic relates to pests and beneficial predators; ENT 119 if topic is bee biology; either if topic bridges both.

title: Seminar in Entomology
description: Course Description: Weekly Entomology seminar.

title: Group Study
description: Course Description: Group study.

title: Research
description: Course Description: Research.

title: Climate Change Impacts, Mitigation, & Natural Resources Management
description: Course Description: Major topics in environmental sciences, with emphasis on climate change impacts on natural and human managed systems.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing.

title: Environmental Policy Evaluation
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Method and practice, philosophical basis, and political role of policy analysis. Reviews basic concepts from economic theory; how and why environmental problems emerge in a market economy; and tools necessary for solving environmental problems.

title: Environmental Governance
description: Course Description: Theories of environmental governance and their practical applications in solving modern environmental problems. Application of governance theory to choice of real-world case studies of environmental problems.

title: Environmental Law
description: Course Description: Roles of legislatures, agencies, and courts in creating and interpreting law; legal strategies for addressing environmental problems; major environmental statutes; and the relationship between federal and state/local legal authority.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing; consent of instructor.

title: Policy Development & Implementation
description: Course Description: Environmental policy development and implementation processes: developing and enacting legislation, regulation development and review, and implementation of new laws. California state processes. Local, federal, and international policy. Business’ role in climate policy. Theory, applied study, discussion with guest experts, and practical skills such as how to write for a policy audience.

title: Environmental Policy Clinic
description: Course Description: Teams analyze environmental policy problems and natural resources issues from scientific, legal, and economic perspectives. Hands-on learning partnering with a client organization.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Environmental Policy Clinic
description: Course Description: Teams analyze environmental policy problems and natural resources issues from scientific, legal, and economic perspectives. Hands-on learning partnering with a client organization. Continuation of ENV 203A.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ENV 203A B- or better; or consent of instructor.

title: Policy Clinic Prep Seminar
description: Course Description: Introductions to project management, conflict resolution and team building. Review and selection of project proposals and draft scope of work.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Data Management & Visualization in R
description: Course Description: Introduction to programming and data analysis in R. Workflow (version control, markdown, reading and writing data), object-oriented programming, statistical analysis, and visualization.

title: Seminar
description: Course Description: Varied topics in environmental policy and management. May feature guest speakers.

title: Graduate Internship
description: Course Description: Individually designed supervised internship, off campus, in community or institutional setting. Developed with advice of faculty mentor.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Environmental Policy & Management Practicum
description: Course Description: Practicum experience integrating coursework into an applied professional setting.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Professional Development Seminar
description: Course Description: Weekly seminar focused on the development of professional skills needed for environmental careers. May feature guest speakers working in environmental or policy careers to discuss pathways and day-to-day job tasks and skills required.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing.

title: Group Study
description: Course Description: Group Study.

title: Research
description: Course Description: Individual study under the direction of a faculty member.

title: Quantitative Epidemiology I: Probability
description: Course Description: Foundations in probability for epidemiologists. Emphasis on properties of and relationships between distributions and application of probability concepts to epidemiology. Includes a mathematical skills laboratory to assist in solution of epidemiologic problems.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ((MAT 016A, MAT 016B) or (MAT 017A, MAT 017B) or (MAT 021A, MAT 021B)); STA 100; STA 108 (can be concurrent); or equivalent of any listed course with consent of instructor; concurrent or previous enrollment in a basic epidemiology course (e.g., EPI 205).

title: Quantitative Epidemiology II: Statistical Inference
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Provides the mathematical statistics foundation for statistical models, methods, and data analysis.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): STA 108; (EPI 202 or STA 130A or STA 131A); EPI 205.

title: Quantitative Epidemiology III: Statistical Models
description: Course Description: Introduces statistical models, methods, and data analysis in the areas of generalized linear model and survival analysis methodology.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): STA 108; (EPI 203 or STA 130B or STA 131B); EPI 205.

title: Principles of Epidemiology
description: Course Description: Basic epidemiologic concepts and approaches to epidemiologic research, with examples from veterinary and human medicine, including outbreak investigation, infectious disease epidemiology, properties of tests, and an introduction to epidemiologic study design and surveillance.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): MPM 202; or consent of instructor; an introductory statistics course.

title: Epidemiologic Study Design
description: Course Description: Builds on concepts presented in EPI 205. Concepts of epidemiologic study design (clinical trials, observational cohort studies, case control studies) introduced in EPI 205A are covered in more depth, using a problem-based format.
