Discussion of published epidemiologic studies.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): EPI 205; or consent of instructor.

title: Analysis & Interpretation of Epidemiologic Data
description: Course Description: Application of theory and concepts of statistics and epidemiology to analysis and interpretation of data typically found in veterinary and human epidemiologic research.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): EPI 204 (can be concurrent); EPI 207; (STA 144 or PHR 202); and entry level skill in standard statistical software (eg. SPSS, BMDP, SAS, Stata, MinTab, S-Plus).

title: History of Epidemiology in Public Health
description: Course Description: Introduction to the history of epidemiology in solving major public health problems. Original historical articles will be read/discussed. Topics may include: infectious disease, accidents/adverse events, nutritional deficiencies, community vaccination trials, occupational exposures, cancer, birth defects, cardiovascular disease, and smoking.

title: Implementation Research in Health
description: Course Description: Introduction to concepts and methods in implementation science, including theories, models and frameworks, qualitative and mixed methods, adaptations, and common study designs in implementation research.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): EPI 205 and consent of instructor.

title: Infectious Disease Epidemiology
description: Course Description: Basic and advanced level of conceptual and methodological foundations in infectious disease epidemiology necessary for veterinarians to develop and evaluate programs for detection, prevention, and control of infectious diseases in animal populations.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Problems in Epidemiologic Study Design
description: Course Description: Design and development of research protocols and funding applications for peer review. Application of research methods data collection and management and statistical analysis in research proposals. Methods of evaluating research proposals, mechanisms of funding, specifying human subjects considerations.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): MPM 405; STA 102; STA 106; or the equivalent; MPM 406 or the equivalent; PHR 207 required concurrently.

title: Spatial Epidemiology
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Geographic Information Systems (GIS) and spatial statistics. Students are expected to complete a term project based on their graduate research.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): EPI 205 or MPM 205.

title: Health & Ecological Risk Analysis
description: Course Description: Methodological approach to risk analysis for human and animal-related health and ecological issues. Basic principles of risk analysis, including perception, communication, assessment and management. Emphasis on the assessment of risk.

title: Advanced Topics in Epidemiology Methods
description: Course Description: In-depth study of topics in epidemiology theory and methods, selected from: causal inference, confounding, study design, or other related areas, with year to year variation. Readings are assigned and students are expected to lead discussions on the readings.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): EPI 205; EPI 206; EPI 207; or equivalents, with consent of instructor.

title: Methods for Longitudinal & Repeated Measurement Data
description: Course Description: Mixed models for longitudinal data (LD)/repeated measurements; Mean and covariance models; General linear LD models; Random coefficients models; Linear mixed effects models for continuous outcome; Generalized linear mixed effects model for discrete outcome including binary, ordinal and count data.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): EPI 204; or consent of instructor.

title: Meta Analysis
description: Course Description: Systematic review, standard and advanced statistical methods for meta-analysis and syntheses of knowledge and evidence. Quantitative analysis of published data, primarily in aggregate form. Students demonstrate skills in study design, protocol, analysis, and results reporting through presentation of drafted first author paper.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): A course in basic statistics or consent of instructor.

title: Geographic Information Systems for Health Professionals
description: Course Description: Emphasis on basic geographic and data management principles. Focus on software proficiency in application to analyzing/solving health-related problems. For graduate and professional students in epidemiology, public health, preventive veterinary medicine, health informatics with interest in spatial techniques in research.

title: Introduction to Molecular Epidemiology
description: Course Description: Overview of the modern field of molecular epidemiology. Integrates molecular biology into traditional epidemiologic research by identifying pathways, molecules and genes that influence the risk of developing disease.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): EPI 205.

title: Infectious Disease Epidemiology
description: Course Description: Infectious disease epidemiology and prevention, with emphasis on human and veterinary diseases of global health importance. Major global health epidemics and challenges of infectious diseases, by mode of transmission.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Introductory epidemiology course; e.g., EPI 205.

title: Advanced Data Analysis with SAS
description: Course Description: Provide an overview of common advanced statistical methods as well as a treatment of how to use SAS to implement them. Learn the ideas of reproducible research and reporting of statistical analyses.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): EPI 202; EPI 203; EPI 204; or the equivalent, or consent of instructor.

title: Principles of Injury Epidemiology
description: Course Description: Overview of the epidemiology of human injury, including general principles, surveillance methods, behavioral factors, environmental factors, treatment issues and engineering and legal interventions related to vehicular injuries, drownings, falls, fires and burns, poisonings, firearm injuries, and other intentional injuries.

title: Critical Thinking in Epidemiology
description: Course Description: Critical thinking in Epidemiology.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): EPI 205 B or better; EPI 206 B or better; EPI 207 (can be concurrent).

title: Environmental Epidemiology
description: Course Description: Examination of the human health effects and the risk of disease from community, occupational, and personal exposure to toxic substances.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): MPM 405 (can be concurrent); upper division undergraduates who have completed EST 126; or the equivalent.

title: Epidemiology of Chronic Diseases & Aging
description: Course Description: Overview of the epidemiology of chronic disease in old age. Topics include biology of aging, epidemiology of cardiovascular disease, neoplasms, osteoporosis and fractures, psychosocial factors and health in old age, dementias, functional status and prevention of disease.

title: Science, Technology, & Policy
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Analysis of policymaking for science and the use of scientific expertise for making decisions about technology. Topics include funding of basic research, relationship of science to technological development, science and military policy, technological risks, technology assessment and scientists and politics.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor; POL 001 recommended.

title: Racial Politics
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Race, racial attitudes and racial policies in the United States with a specific emphasis on African Americans.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): POL 001 recommended.

title: Special Studies in Comparative Politics
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Intensive examination of one or more special problems appropriate to comparative politics. Coverage is given to formal and informal political institutions, economically developing and developed countries, and non-democratic, democratic, and democratizing countries.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor; POL 002 recommended.

title: Bureaucracy in Modern Society
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Role of bureaucracy in a complex society, with emphasis upon changing relationships between government and the economy; consequences of rapid technological and social change for bureaucratic structures and processes; the problems of reconciling expertise and democracy and increasing the responsiveness of public bureaucracy.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor; POL 001 or POL 002 recommended.

title: Administrative Behavior
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Implications for American public administration of evolving concepts about behavior in organizations.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): POL 001 recommended.

title: Administrative Theory
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Historical and critical analysis of the principal theories of organization and management of public agencies in light of such concepts as decision making, bureaucracy, authority and power, communication and control; examination of role of government bureaucracies in the total society.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): POL 004 recommended.

title: International Relations
description: Course Description: Analysis and evaluation of substantive issues in contemporary international relations. Readings drawn from current academic and non-academic periodicals. May be taught abroad.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor; POL 003 recommended.

title: Internship in Public Affairs
description: Course Description: Supervised internship and study in political, governmental, or related organizations.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of Instructor; highest priority assigned to students with Political Science Public Service major; upper division standing required.

title: Internship in Public Affairs
description: Course Description: Supervised internship and study in political, governmental, or related organizations.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): POL 192A; consent of instructor; highest priority assigned to students with Political Science-Public Service major; upper division standing.

title: Research in Practical Politics
description: Course Description: Supervised preparation of an extensive paper relating internship experience to concepts, literature, and theory of political science.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): POL 192A; POL 192B; open only to Political Science-Public Service majors, for whom it is required.

title: Washington Center Research Seminar
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Core academic component of Washington Program offered every quarter. Topics coordinated with internships. Research draws on resources uniquely available in Washington, DC. Supervised preparation of extensive paper.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): WAS 192W (can be concurrent).

title: Special Study for Honors Students
description: Course Description: Directed reading, research and writing culminating in preparation of a senior honors thesis under the direction of faculty advisor.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Major in Politcal Science with upper division standing and a GPA of 3.500 in the major.

title: Special Study for Honors Students
description: Course Description: Directed reading, research and writing culminating in preparation of a senior honors thesis under the direction of faculty advisor.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Major in Political Science with upper division standing and a GPA of 3.500 in the major.

title: Special Studies in American Politics
description: Course Description: Intensive examination of one or more special problems appropriate to American politics.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor; upper division standing.

title: Seminar in American Politics
description: Course Description: Intensive reading, discussion, research, writing in American politics. Topics may include Congress, the Presidency, the Supreme Court, federalism, voting behavior, interest groups, ethnic groups or other topics with a more specialized content than normal course offerings.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Upper division Political Science majors or consent of instructor.

title: Seminar in Comparative Politics
description: Course Description: Intensive reading, discussion, research, writing in comparative politics. Topics may include one country or geographical area, political institutions or behavior across countries, political development, or other topics that are more specialized than normal course offerings.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Upper division Political Science majors or consent of instructor.

title: Seminar in International Relations
description: Course Description: Intensive reading, discussion, research, writing in international relations including study of international political institutions (UN, EU, or NATO) or interstate relations (war, trade, immigration) and other topics with more specialized content than normal course offerings.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Upper division Political Science majors or consent of instructor.

title: Seminar in Political Theory
description: Course Description: Intensive reading, discussion, research, writing in political theory. Topics may include study of a single political thinker, a group of related thinkers, development of political concepts, or other topics with more specialized content than normal course offerings.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Upper division Political Science majors or consent of instructor.

title: Seminar in Research Methods
description: Course Description: Intensive reading, discussion, research, and writing in selected topics in Research Methods such as research design, statistics, game theory, etc.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Upper division Political Science majors or consent of instructor.

title: Tutoring in Political Science
description: Course Description: Tutor responsibilities such as attending class, offering a guest lecture on a select topic, assisting in group discussions, hosting consultation or review sessions, or assisting instructor with assignments (though not grading).
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Directed Group Study
description: Course Description: Directed group study. May be taught abroad.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor; upper division standing.

title: Special Study for Advanced Undergraduates
description: Course Description: Special study for advanced undergraduates.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of Instructor; upper division standing.

title: Urban Government & Politics
description: Course Description: Survey and analysis of the literature in the field of local government and politics in the United States. Approaches to the study of political reform, local autonomy, community power, representation, expertise, service delivery, policy making and political change.

title: American State Government & Politics
description: Course Description: Survey and analysis of the literature in the field of state government, politics, and policy. Approaches to the study of the American states as political systems, including their governing institutions and processes and their role in the Federal system.

title: American Government: The Presidency
description: Course Description: Thorough overview of the current research on political executives, with particular emphasis on the American presidency. Two principal goals: the development of important and innovative student research programs; and adequate preparation for qualifying examinations.

title: American Government: Congress
description: Course Description: Thorough overview of the current research on Congress, with particular emphasis on political representation. Two principal goals: the development of important and innovative student research programs; and adequate preparation for qualifying examinations.

title: American Government: Courts
description: Course Description: Survey and analysis of the literature in the field of American government with a focus on courts. Emphasis on the development and testing of theories of behavior and processes.

title: Environmental Public Policy
description: Course Description: Analysis of the interface between the world of academic reflection about ecological and environmental problems and the world of political action. Evaluation of alternative approaches to policy analysis and recommendation. Individual research, including field research, will parallel discussion of the literature.

title: Policy Analysis
description: Course Description: Social science techniques applied to public policy formation and evaluation.

title: The American Political System
description: Course Description: Analysis of selected theoretical and empirical issues posed by contemporary research in American government and politics.

title: Research Design in Political Science
description: Course Description: Introduction to philosophy of science and research design for political science. Topics include: logic of empirical research, overview of research design approaches for political science research.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing.

title: Research Methods in Political Science
description: Course Description: Introductory seminar on the foundations of probability theory and mathematical statistics that are critical to empirical investigations in political science.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing.

title: Quantitative Analysis in Political Science I
description: Course Description: Seminar provides an introduction to the linear regression model. Will obtain a working knowledge of basic regression techniques and problems.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): POL 211.

title: Quantitative Analysis in Political Science II
description: Course Description: More advanced topics in the use of statistical methods, with emphasis on political applications. Topics include: properties of least squares estimates, problems in multiple regression, and advanced topics (probit analysis, simultaneous models, time-series analysis, etc.).
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): POL 211; POL 212.

title: Research in Political Science
description: Course Description: Research seminar sequence required of all PhD students. Design, execution, and defense of an original piece of research in political science, culminating in a paper of publishable quality.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): POL 213.

title: Research in Political Science
description: Course Description: Research seminar sequence required of all PhD students. Design, execution, and defense of an original piece of research in political science, culminating in a paper of publishable quality.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): POL 212; POL 214A.

title: Introduction to Modeling Political Behavior
description: Course Description: Introduction to formal and game theoretic analyses of politics. Students will learn basic game theory and modeling skills. We examine the benefits of modeling, and look at examples of formal analysis in a variety of political science sub-fields.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): POL 211; POL 212.

title: Qualitative Research Methods
description: Course Description: Methodology for utilizing theoretically-oriented case studies and controlled comparison of a small number of cases to develop and test theories. Examination of how the case study method compliments experimental, statistical and deductive modes of research.

title: Social Choice Theory & Spatial Modeling
description: Course Description: Introduction to social choice theory and formal spatial modeling including Arrows Theorem, the paradox of voting, cycling and agenda control. Focus on mastering modeling techniques as well as interpretation of classic works.

title: Topics in Political Theory
description: Course Description: Topics vary and may be the work of a single theorist, time period, or political concept, such as justice.

title: Political Theory Sequence
description: Course Description: Survey of the great works in ancient and medieval political theory including such writers as Plato, Aristotle, Cicero, St. Augustine, Aquinas, Alfarabi and Marsilius. Discussions of various interpretations of these authors.

title: Political Theory Sequence
description: Course Description: Survey of the great works in early modern to contemporary political theory including such writers as Machiavelli, Hobbes, Locke, Rousseau, Marx, Mill, Nietzsche, and Rawls. Discussion of various interpretations of these authors.

title: Contemporary Political Theory
description: Course Description: Survey of important works in contemporary political theory including such writers as Nietzsche, Heidegger, Arendt, Rawls, Nozick, Sandel.

title: Seminar in Political Theory
description: Course Description: Introduction to political theory and current debates over its study. Readings from and textual interpretations of political theory including the Federalist Papers and major works by thinkers such as Plato, Aristotle, Machiavelli, Hobbes, Locke, Rousseau, and Rawls. Other readings addressing issues of textual interpretation.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing.

title: International Relations
description: Course Description: International relations.

title: International Security
description: Course Description: Contemporary scholarship on issues related to international conﬂict and political violence, with particular emphasis on how current trends in globalization and governance aﬀect global security.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): POL 223; consent of instructor.

title: The International System
description: Course Description: Analysis of the international system by means of theory formulation and integration; critique of research designs; use of various techniques of data generation and analysis.

title: Seminar in International Political Economy
description: Course Description: Research in international political economy. Structure of the global economy, as well as specific dimensions of international economic relations, including trade, capital flows, global production structures, and migration.

title: American Foreign Policy
description: Course Description: American foreign policy.

title: U.S. Political Culture & Foreign Relations
description: Course Description: Relates U.S. political culture to formulation of foreign policy. Analyzes American ideological preferences in historical perspective, contemporary public opinion, decision making and implementation. Concludes by examining linkages between foreign policy behavior and democratic process.

title: Communist Political Systems
description: Course Description: Systematic analysis of selected topics dealing with the political process of communist political systems.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Seminar in Comparative Politics
description: Course Description: Systematic survey of theories and methods used in the study of comparative politics.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing or consent of instructor.

title: Comparative Institutional Change
description: Course Description: Comparison of institutional changes in countries of the former Soviet Union and Eastern Europe during the period of transition to democracy. Special attention to institutions of mass representation; electoral and party systems and national legislatures.

title: Policymaking in Third-World Societies
description: Course Description: Included in an analysis of policymaking process in Third-World countries are such topics as political resources, institutional resources, decision making, resource allocations, planning, and budgeting, implementation, and distribution of world resources.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing or consent of instructor.

title: Policy Development & Impact in U.S. Courts
description: Course Description: Thorough overview of the literature regarding courts as policymaking institutions of government, with emphasis on the formation and implementation of judicial policy. Differences and similarities across the judicial, congressional, and executive branch policy processes.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing.

title: Political Parties
description: Course Description: Survey of selected topics in American and comparative parties.

title: Political Behavior
description: Course Description: Survey of selected topics in political behavior and public opinion.

title: Political Economy
description: Course Description: Politics of economic policy as reflected in taxation, spending and regulation; impact of prices, employment, and growth on political demands; government responses to economic conditions; electoral politics and the political business cycle.

title: Political Networks: Methods & Applications
description: Course Description: Structure of political networks, socio-matrices and affiliation networks; general networks characteristics: density, centralization, polarization, interdependence, dyadic and triadic characteristics: structural and role equivalence; subsets of networks: cliques, blocks and bloc modeling; characteristics of individuals in networks: centrality and prestige.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing.

title: Bayesian Methods: for Social & Behavioral Sciences
description: Course Description: Methodology seminar introducing Bayesian quantitative methods to issues and problems in political science and other social and behavioral sciences.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): POL 212; or equivalent to course.

title: Statistical Computing Issues in Political Science
description: Course Description: Methodology seminar introducing computing issues in empirical models for political science and other social and behavioral sciences.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): POL 213; or equivalent to course.

title: Advanced Modeling of Political Behavior
description: Course Description: Applications of formal theory to political science. Review of relevant contributions in other social sciences. Consideration of advanced techniques in game theory. Rational and behavioral approaches.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): POL 215; or equivalent to course.

title: Organizational Behavior
description: Course Description: Organizational behavior as it relates to public sector decision making.

title: Advanced Network Analysis
description: Course Description: Exponential Random Graph Models (ERGMS) of networks, game theoretic models of network formation and network dynamics, diffusion processes, shocks and network collapse, percolation, cross-network spillover processes, social and political applications of advanced network models.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): POL 211; POL 212; POL 279.

title: Statistics of Causal Inference in Political Science
description: Course Description: Approaches to causal inference using the potential outcomes framework, with political science applications using observational and experimental data. Covers several prominent causal research designs used for studying politics.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): POL 211; POL 212; POL 213.

title: Research in American Government & Public Policy
description: Course Description: Special research seminar on problems and issues in the study of American government and public policy.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Research in Political Theory
description: Course Description: Special research seminar on problems and issues in the study of political theory.

title: Research in International Relations
description: Course Description: Special research seminar on select problems and issues in the study of international relations.

title: Research in Judicial Politics
description: Course Description: Contemporary research on judicial politics, judicial institutions, jurisprudence, and judicial behavior.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing in Political Science or consent of instructor.

title: Research in Political Parties, Politics, & Political Behavior
description: Course Description: Special research seminar on selected problems and issues in the study of political parties, politics, and political behavior.

title: Research in Comparative Government & Policy
description: Course Description: Special research seminar on select problems and issues in the study of comparative government and policy.

title: Research in Methodology
description: Course Description: Special research seminar on selected problems and issues in methods in political science.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): POL 212.

title: Internships in Political Science
description: Course Description: Application and evaluation of theoretical concepts through work experience or systematic observation in public and political agencies.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Open only to persons who have internships or other positions in governmental agencies, political parties, etc.

title: Group Study
description: Course Description: Group study.

title: Research
description: Course Description: Research.

title: Research
description: Course Description: Research.

title: The Teaching of Political Science
description: Course Description: Methods and problems of teaching political science at the undergraduate level.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate student standing in Political Science.

title: Teaching Assistant Training Practicum
description: Course Description: Teaching assistant training.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing.

title: Elementary Portuguese
description: Course Description: Introduction to Portuguese grammar and development of all language skills in a cultural context with special emphasis on communication.

title: Accelerated Intensive Elementary Portuguese
description: Course Description: Intended for students who wish to complete three quarters of Portuguese 1, 2, and 3.

title: Elementary Portuguese
description: Course Description: Continuation of POR 001 in the areas of grammar and development of all basic language skills in cultural context with special emphasis on communication.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): POR 001.

title: Elementary Portuguese
description: Course Description: Continuation of POR 002 in the areas of grammar and development of all basic language skills in cultural context with special emphasis on communication.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): POR 002.

title: Elementary Portuguese Conversation
description: Course Description: Designed to develop oral communication skills. Emphasis on increasing vocabulary, improving listening comprehension, pronunciation, accuracy and grammar control. Practice of everyday situations.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): POR 003.

title: Intermediate Portuguese
description: Course Description: Review and develop the grammar, vocabulary, and composition acquired in first year Portuguese through exercises and reading of modern texts.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): POR 003.

title: Intermediate Portuguese
description: Course Description: Continuation of POR 021. Focus on more difficult grammar concepts and further composition practice. Development of all language skills through exercises and reading of modern texts.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): POR 021.

title: Portuguese Composition I
description: Course Description: Development of writing skills by way of reading, discussion, and analysis of authentic materials, literary texts, and videos. Selective review of grammar. Class activities include composition, journals, letters, individual and group projects.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): POR 022.

title: Intermediate Portuguese Conversation
description: Course Description: Continuation of POR 008. Designed to develop oral communication skills at a more advanced level. Practice in more complex situations.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): POR 008.

title: Intermediate Portuguese for Spanish Speakers
description: Course Description: Development of linguistic and learning skills required for Spanish-speaking students in upper division courses in Portuguese.

title: Portuguese for Spanish-Speaking Graduate Students
description: Course Description: Intensive review of grammar and composition. Development of all language skills, but with emphasis on reading of modern texts, presentation/discussion, and writing on academic topics. Development on the following writing skills: analytical, argumentative, and creative.

title: Directed Group Study
description: Course Description: Directed group study primarily for lower division students.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor and department chairperson.

title: Principles of Luso-Brazilian Literature & Criticism
description: Course Description: Principles of literary criticism applied to the study of fiction, poetry, and essays of major literary writers of the Luso-Brazilian world.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): POR 022 or POR 023; or consent of instructor.

title: Structure of Portuguese: Sounds & Words
description: Course Description: Linguistic description of sound patterns of Portuguese and how those sounds can be used to form larger units, such as morphemes and words. Theoretical and practical comparisons with English and with other Romance languages.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Survey of Luso-Brazilian Literature: 1500-1800
description: Course Description: Overview of Luso-Brazilian literature, covering three major literary periods: Renaissance, Baroque, and Enlightenment. Attention to the concept of imitation and nativism.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): POR 100; (POR 022 or POR 023).

title: Portuguese Literature: Medieval & Renaissance
description: Course Description: Overview of the origins of the Portuguese literature, spanning from the 13th century to the 16th century. Studies of lyrical and epic poetry, drama, and travel narratives.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): POR 100; or consent of instructor.

title: Luis de Camões
description: Course Description: Overview of the greatest Renaissance Portuguese poet, Luis de Camões. Study his famous epic poem, Os Lusiadas, and a series of sonnets written by him.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): POR 100; (POR 022 or POR 031).

title: Introduction to Luso-Brazilian Culture
description: Course Description: Introduction to history, geography, and culture of Portugal and Brazil. Art, history of ideas, and everyday cultural manifestations. Introduction to critical reading and textual analysis. Taught in Portuguese.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): POR 023 or POR 022.

title: Special Topics in Luso-Brazilian Literature & Culture
description: Course Description: Special topics in the study of Luso-Brazilian literature and culture.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): POR 100; or consent of instructor.

title: Luso-Brazilian Literature & Culture
description: Course Description: Colonial Brazilian literature survey. Readings include 16th-18th centuries manuscripts and books of cultural importance in a society dominated by censorship and with no printing presses. Study of the role literary Academies played in the so called "culture of manuscripts."
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): POR 100; or consent of instructor.

title: Introduction to Brazilian Literature
description: Course Description: Narrative and poetic texts of the 19th and 20th centuries in Brazil. In-depth and comparative study of Romantic and (Neo)Naturalist movements as a forum for discussion about literary tradition and modernity in Latin America.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): POR 003; (POR 031 or POR 031G).

title: 20th C Masters in Brazilian Literature
description: Course Description: Overview of modern Brazilian literature from early 20th C to the poetry by João Cabral de Melo Neto and the Concretists (1960s), including European avant-garde movements and literary and cultural manifestos leading to a revolutionary body of literature.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): POR 100; or consent of instructor.

title: Tutoring in Portuguese
description: Course Description: Tutoring in undergraduate courses, including leadership in small voluntary discussion groups affiliated with departmental courses.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Directed Group Study
description: Course Description: Directed group study.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor and Department Chairperson.

title: Special Study for Advanced Undergraduates
description: Course Description: Independent study with professor for advanced undergraduate students, or honor thesis students.

title: General Psychology
description: Course Description: Introduction to empirical approaches. Focus on perception, cognition, personality and social psychology, and biological aspects of behavior.

title: General Psychology
description: Course Description: Introduction to empirical approaches. Focus on perception, cognition, personality and social psychology, and biological aspects of behavior.

title: General Psychology
description: Course Description: Introduction to empirical approaches. Focus on perception, cognition, personality and social psychology, and biological aspects of behavior.

title: Data Visualization in the Social Sciences
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Introduction to quantitative data across the social sciences (Communications, Political Science, Psychology, Sociology, and other disciplines). Transforming data, describing data, producing graphs, visual reasoning, and interpretations.

title: Freshman Psychology Seminar
description: Course Description: Instructor will acquaint students with his or her program of research, the development of scientific questions from the literature, and the application of research methods to examine these questions. Critical thinking will be encouraged via expository writing and brief presentations.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Freshman standing.

title: Research Methods in Psychology
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Introduction to experimental design, interviews, questionnaires, field and observational methods, reliability, and statistical inference.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V); PSC 012Y or STA 013 or STA 100 strongly recommended.

title: Research Methods in Psychology
description: Course Description: Introduction to experimental design, interviews, questionnaires, field and observational methods, reliability, and statistical inference.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V); PSC 012Y or STA 013 or STA 100 strongly recommended.

title: Mindreading: Understanding Other Minds
description: Course Description: Psychological perspectives on the process of understanding other people’s minds. Integration of social-cognitive theories with empirical evidence to explore the human ability to make sense of others' thoughts, feelings, and behaviors.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V); (SOC 001 or SOC 001V or PHI 010).

title: Lower Division Seminar
description: Course Description: Examination of a special topic in Psychology through shared readings, discussions, written assignments, or special activities such as fieldwork or laboratory work.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor; lower division standing.

title: Directed Group Study
description: Course Description: Primarily for lower division students.

title: Special Study for Lower Division Students
description: Course Description: Special study for lower division students.

title: Introduction to Cognitive Psychology
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Introduction to human information processing, mental representation and transformation, imagery, attention, memory, language processing, concept formation, problem solving, and computer simulation.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V); (PSC 041 or PSC 041V).

title: Introduction to Cognitive Psychology
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Introduction to human information processing, mental representation and transformation, imagery, attention, memory, language processing, concept formation, problem solving, and computer simulation.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V); (PSC 041 or PSC 041V).

title: Introduction to Biological Psychology
description: Course Description: Survey and integration of the relationships between behavior and biological processes, including physiology, genes, development, ecology, and evolution. May be taught abroad.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V); PSC 041.

title: Statistical Analysis of Psychological Data
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Design and statistical analysis of psychological investigations and the interpretation of quantitative data in psychology.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V); (PSC 041 or PSC 041V); (STA 013 or STA 013Y or STA 032 or STA 100 or STA 102).

title: Statistical Analysis of Psychological Data
description: Course Description: Probability theory, sampling distributions, statistical inference, and hypothesis testing using standard parametric and correlational approaches. Simple regression analysis, multiple regression analysis, non-parametric statistics, introduction to multivariate statistics, with applications in psychology.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): PSC 103A.

title: Applied Psychometrics: An Introduction to Measurement Theory
description: Course Description: Examination of the basic principles and applications of classical and modern test theory. Topics include test construction, reliability theory, validity theory, factor analysis and latent trait theory.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 041 or PSC 041V); PSC 103A; (STA 013 or STA 013Y); upper division standing in Psychology.

title: Network Analysis of Psychological Data
description: Course Description: Fundamental principles of Graph Theory and applications of Network Analysis to psychological data. Community detection, network control, and modeling of cascading failures.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V); (PSC 041 or PSC 041V); PSC 103A.

title: Questionnaire & Survey Research Methods
description: Course Description: Introduction to survey and questionnaire research methods with emphasis on how to ask questions. Social and psychological factors that influence survey response. Practical aspects of fielding survey and questionnaire research.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V; and consent of instructor; PSC 041 or an equivalent course on social or behavioral research methods or consent of instructor.

title: Developmental Psychobiology
description: Course Description: The biology of behavioral development; survey and integration of the organismic and environmental processes that regulate the development of behavior.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): PSC 101.

title: Agent-Based Modeling
description: Course Description: Introduction to agent-based computer simulation and analysis with emphasis on learning how to model animals, including humans, to achieve insight into social and group behavior.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): PSC 100 and/or PSC 101 recommended.

title: Physiological Psychology
description: Course Description: Relationship of brain structure and function to behavior, motivation, emotion, language, and learning in humans and other animals. An introduction to the methodology of physiological psychology and neuroscience.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V); PSC 041; PSC 101.

title: Advanced Animal Behavior
description: Course Description: Advanced integrative survey of biological principles of behavioral organization, emphasizing historical roots, current research directions, conceptual issues and controversies. Laboratory exercises on the description and analysis of the behavior of captive and free-living animals
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): PSC 101 or NPB 102.

title: Hormones & Behavior
description: Course Description: Endocrine physiology with an emphasis on the principles of behavior. Fundamental relationships between hormones and various behaviors engaged in by the organism during its lifetime. Role of hormones in behavioral homeostasis, social behavior, reproductive behavior, parental behavior, adaptation to stress.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): NPB 101; (PSC 101 or NPB 102).

title: Comparative Neuroanatomy
description: Course Description: Overview of the neuroanatomy in mammalian vertebrates, focusing on the cerebral cortex and experimental techniques. Examine changes or modifications to neural structures as a result of morphological or behavioral specializations.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): NPB 100 or NPB 101 or NPB 110B or PSC 121.

title: Comparative Neuroanatomy Laboratory
description: Course Description: Comparative neuroanatomy laboratory illustrating modern neuroanatomical techniques in determining neural connections within the mammalian brain. Includes experimentation and presentation of results.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): PSC 124 (can be concurrent).

title: Behavioral Epigenetics
description: Course Description: Review of basic principles in genetics and epigenetics with emphasis on behavior. Introduction to the use of modern molecular methods in understanding the complex relationships between genes, environment, and behavior.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): PSC 101.

title: Health Psychology
description: Course Description: Psychological factors influencing health and illness. Topics include stress and coping, personality and health, symptom perception and reporting, heart disease, cancer, compliance, and health maintenance and promotion.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V); (PSC 041 or PSC 041V); PSC 101 recommended.

title: Human Learning & Memory
description: Course Description: Consideration of major theories of human learning and memory with critical examination of relevant experimental data.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V); (PSC 041 or PSC 041V); (PSC 012Y or STA 013 or STA 013Y or STA 100); or consent of instructor.

title: Perception
description: Course Description: Cognitive organizations related to measurable physical energy changes mediated through sensory channels. Perception of objects, space, motion, events.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V); (PSC 041 or PSC 041V); (PSC 100 or PSC 100Y or PSC 135).

title: Language & Cognition
description: Course Description: Introduction to the cognitive processes involved in language comprehension and production. Topics include the biological foundations of language, speech perception, word recognition, syntax, reading ability, and pragmatics.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V); (PSC 041 or PSC 041V); (PSC 100 or PSC 100Y or PSC 135); or consent of instructor.

title: Neuroeconomics/Reinforcement Learning & Decision Making
description: Course Description: Theoretical and empirical approaches to neuroeconomics (neuroscience of decision making) from psychology, neuroscience, economics, and computer science. Neuroscience of judgment and decision making, behavioral economics, and reinforcement learning.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 100 or PSC 100Y or PSC 135 or ARE 100A or ECN 100A or ECN 100AV or NPB 162 or NPB 163); (STA 013 or STA 013Y or STA 100 or PSC 103A); or consent of instructor.

title: Computational Cognitive Neuroscience
description: Course Description: Explorations of how brain secretes mind, via computer simulations that are manipulated and probed to investigate how neurons produce perception, attention, memory, language, and cognitive control.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V); PSC 100 or PSC 101 or PSC 135 recommended.

title: Cognitive Neuroscience: The Biological Foundations of the Mind
description: Course Description: Neuroscientific foundations of higher mental processes including attention, memory, language, higher-level perceptual and motor processes, and consciousness. Emphasis on the neural mechanisms which form the substrates of human cognition and the relationship of mind to brain. May be taught abroad.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V); (PSC 041 or PSC 041V); or consent of instructor; PSC 101 or PSC 121 recommended.

title: Psychology of Music
description: Course Description: Introduction to the mental and neural representations of musical structures and processes involved in perceiving, remembering, and performing music. Music and emotion.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V); (PSC 041 or PSC 041V); (PSC 100 or PSC 100Y or PSC 135 or MUS 006C); or consent of instructor.

title: Neurobiology of Learning & Memory
description: Course Description: Overview of the neural basis of learning and memory focusing on modern behavioral neuroscience research with animals. Topics include consolidation, neural plasticity, cellular competition for memory storage, and the role of neurogenesis in learning.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V); (PSC 041 or PSC 041V); PSC 101.

title: Consciousness & Cognition
description: Course Description: Current theoretical and empirical evidence in the study of cognition and consciousness. Theories of consciousness, psychological and neural basis of conscious and unconscious processes such as attention, intentionality, and dreams.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V); (PSC 041 or PSC 041V); (PSC 100 or PSC 100Y or PSC 135).

title: Advanced Cognitive Neuroscience
description: Course Description: Advanced integrative survey of cognitive neuroscience, including perception, attention, memory, and navigation. Emphasis on reviewing literature in psychology, neuroscience, and statistics; understanding methods in cognition; and presentation skills.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V); (PSC 041 or PSC 041V); PSC 135; or consent of instructor.

title: Developmental Psychology
description: Course Description: An ontogenetic account of human behavior through adolescence with emphasis on motor skills, mental abilities, motivation, and social interaction.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V); (PSC 041 or PSC 041V).

title: Developmental Psychology
description: Course Description: Ontogenetic account of human behavior through adolescence with emphasis on motor skills, mental abilities, motivation, and social interaction.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V); (PSC 041 or PSC 041V).

title: Developmental Psychology
description: Course Description: Ontogenetic account of human behavior through adolescence with emphasis on motor skills, mental abilities, motivation, and social interaction.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V); (PSC 041 or PSC 041V).

title: Cognitive Development
description: Course Description: Theories, methods, evidence, and debates in the field of cognitive development, such as nature/nurture, constraints on learning, and the role of plasticity. Topics include attention, memory, concepts about the physical and social world, and language.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): PSC 140 or PSC 140Y or PSC 140V or HDE 100B or HDE 100A or HDE 100AV.

title: Social & Personality Development
description: Course Description: Social and personality development of children, infancy through adolescence. Topics include the development of personality, achievement motivation, self-understanding, sex-role identity, and antisocial behavior. Emphasis on the interface between biological and social factors.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): PSC 140 or PSC 140Y or PSC 140V or HDE 100A or HDE 100AV or HDE 100B or HDE 100BV.

title: Infant Development
description: Course Description: Psychological development in infancy. Topics include physical and motor development, sensory and nervous system development, and memory and cognitive development. Emphasis will be on evaluating theories, empirical research, and experimental methods for understanding infant development.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V); (PSC 041 or PSC 041V); (PSC 140 or PSC 140Y or PSC 140V or HDE 100A or HDE 100AV).

title: Developmental Cognitive Neuroscience
description: Course Description: Neuroscientific theories and methods (EEG, ERP, fNIRS, fMRI) that inform an understanding of behavioral and cognitive development over infancy and childhood. Neurodevelopmental correlates of perception, action, language, and social cognition; value of the neuroscientific perspective; limitations and challenges of neuroscientific research in the developmental context.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): PSC 135 or ((PSC 140 or PSC 140Y or PSC 140V or HDE 100A or HDE 100B); (PSC 101 or PSC 121 or NPB 161 or HDE 163)); PSC 141 recommended.

title: The Development of Memory
description: Course Description: Theory and research on memory development with focus on infancy and childhood.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V); (PSC 041 or PSC 041V); (PSC 140 or PSC 140Y or PSC 140V or PSC 141 or PSC 142 or PSC 100 or PSC 130).

title: Developmental Disorders
description: Course Description: Current scientific knowledge of the influences of biological, cognitive, and environmental factors on the emergence of disorders with onset in childhood. Examples include autism spectrum, ADD/ADHD, dyslexia and dyscalculia. Emphasis placed on understanding these disorders, their causes and their treatments.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V); (PSC 041 or PSC 041V); (PSC 140 or PSC 140Y or PSC 140V or PSC 141 or HDE 100A or HDE 100AV or HDE 100B or HDE 100BV).

title: Relationship Science: Lust, Love, & Evolution
description: Course Description: Evolutionary perspectives on attraction and close relationships. Integrating social psychological and evolutionary theories with empirical evidence pertaining to human mating.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V; PSC 041 recommended.

title: Relationship Science: Lust, Love, & Evolution
description: Course Description: Evolutionary perspectives on attraction and close relationships. Integrating social psychological and evolutionary theories with empirical evidence pertaining to human mating.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V; PSC 041 recommended.

title: Social Psychology
description: Course Description: Behavior of the individual in the group. Examination of basic psychological processes in social situations, surveying various problems of social interaction: group tensions, norm-development, attitudes, values, public opinion, status.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V), PSC 041 recommended.

title: Social Psychology
description: Course Description: Behavior of the individual in the group. Examination of basic psychological processes in social situations, surveying various problems of social interaction: group tensions, norm-development, attitudes, values, public opinion, status.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V); PSC 041 recommended.

title: Social Cognition
description: Course Description: Examines how social factors influence how we attend to, encode, and process information and how these mental processes affect subsequent judgments and behavior.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V); (PSC 041 or PSC 041V).

title: Psychology & Law
description: Course Description: Current theoretical and empirical issues in the study of psychology and law. Topics include eyewitness testimony, child abuse, jury decision making, juvenile delinquency and criminology, prediction of violence, insanity defense, and memory for traumatic events.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V); (PSC 041 or PSC 041V).

title: Psychology of Emotion
description: Course Description: Introduction to current theories and research on emotion and bodily feelings with special reference to self-knowledge.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V); (PSC 041 or PSC 041V).

title: Psychology of Emotion
description: Course Description: Introduction to current theories and research on emotion and bodily feelings with special reference to self-knowledge.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V); PSC 041.

title: The Psychology of Cultural Appropriation
description: Course Description: Social psychological approach to study of cultural appropriation; integration of social psychological theories with empirical evidence and examples from popular press and digital media; development of critical and integrative ways of thinking about theory and research on intergroup relations, identity, and social inequality; examines routes to cultural appreciation.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 001 or PSC 001V or PSC 001Y); (PSC 041 or PSC 041V).

title: The Psychology of Diversity in Aging
description: Course Description: Psychological perspectives on various parameters of diversity in aging and older adulthood to explore cross-cultural differences, aging assumptions, ageism, discrimination, and resilience. Optimal aging, generativity, Indigenous healthy brain aging, LGBTQIA2S+ health. Disparities in cognitive functioning, health, and longevity. Evaluation of gerontological theories, methodologies, and current empirical research.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V); (PSC 041 or PSC 041V); (PSC 140 or PSC 140Y or PSC 140V); or consent of instructor.

title: Stereotyping, Prejudice, & Stigma
description: Course Description: Social psychological underpinnings of stereotyping, prejudice, and stigma from sociocultural, motivational, and cognitive perspectives. Topics include: origins, maintenance, change, effects on person perception and memory, and the automaticity/controllability of stereotyping and prejudice.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V); (PSC 041 or PSC 041V).

title: Sexual Orientation & Prejudice
description: Course Description: Current scientific knowledge about sexual orientation and prejudice based on sexual orientation. Emphasis is placed on learning the skills necessary for a critical understanding of science and public policy issues relevant to sexuality.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V); (PSC 041 or PSC 041V).

title: Gender & Human Reproduction
description: Course Description: Psychology of reproduction. Reproductive events over the course of an individual’s life, including sexual development, mate choice, relationships, and reproduction. Biological and social psychological explanations at the levels of mechanism and evolutionary function.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V); (PSC 041 or PSC 041V).

title: Psychology of the Self
description: Course Description: Psychological theory and research on the self. Topics include: self-knowledge, self-esteem, self-regulation, self-presentation, cognitive and emotional aspects of the self, and the role of the self in shaping social interaction.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V); (PSC 041 or PSC 041V).

title: Introduction to Personality Psychology
description: Course Description: Scientific study of personality. Methods of personality research. Overview of current research and theory in the field of personality psychology.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V); (PSC 041 or PSC 041V).

title: Introduction to Clinical Psychology
description: Course Description: Major theoretical formulations in the history of clinical psychology, from classical psychoanalysis to contemporary existentialism and behavior modification. Survey based on lectures, films, and tapes, of what clinical psychologists do, including methods of appraisal, professional roles, and approaches to treatment.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V); (PSC 041 or PSC 041V).

title: Mental Health, Mental Illness, & Problems in Living
description: Course Description: Overview of psychological disorders and psychological conditions that lead to problems in daily living, with consideration of causes, classification systems, and treatment.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V); (PSC 041 or PSC 041V).

title: The Science of Multi-Cultural Psychology
description: Course Description: Effects of culture and ethnicity on psychological processes, such as social cognition, emotion, motivation, personality, self-concept, and interpersonal behavior (i.e., how culture makes who you are). Individuals' construction of multicultural identity and experiences.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V); (PSC 041 or PSC 041V).

title: Psychology of Religion
description: Course Description: Major theories, issues, data, and research methodologies of the psychology of religion. Religious experience and expression; religious development in childhood, adolescence, and adulthood; conversion; religious influences on physical and mental health; cross-cultural perspectives.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V); (PSC 041 or PSC 041V).

title: Genius, Creativity, & Leadership
description: Course Description: Phenomenon of genius is examined from a diversity of theoretical, methodological, and disciplinary perspectives, with an emphasis on outstanding creativity and leadership in art, music, literature, philosophy, science, war, and politics.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V); (PSC 041 or PSC 041V); or equivalents, or consent of instructor.

title: Research in Cognitive & Perceptual Psychology
description: Course Description: Empirical research on selected topics in general experimental psychology (general research design and analysis, perception, cognition, cognitive development, etc.). Specific content will vary from quarter to quarter.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): PSC 041 or PSC 041V; four upper division Psychology courses and consent of instructor.

title: Research in Psychobiology
description: Course Description: Empirical research on selected topics in psychobiology (animal learning, animal behavior, physiological and sensory psychology, developmental psychobiology, computer modeling of neural systems). Content varies.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): PSC 101; three additional upper division Psychology courses and consent of instructor.

title: Research in Personality & Social Psychology
description: Course Description: Empirical research on selected topics in personality and social psychology (personality, social psychology, organizational psychology, etc.). Content will vary from quarter to quarter.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): PSC 041 or PSC 041V; four upper division Psychology courses and consent of instructor.

title: Research in Developmental Psychology
description: Course Description: Empirical research on selected topics in developmental psychology (research design and analysis, development, cognitive development, social and personality development etc.).
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): PSC 041 or PSC 041V; consent of instructor; Four upper division Psychology courses.

title: Methods in Laboratory Research
description: Course Description: Training in specific lab-based research methods coupled with hands-on experience in a research lab. Students assessed on their ability report and interpret research findings. Content varies from quarter to quarter.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: History of Psychology
description: Course Description: Development of psychological thought and research in context of history of philosophy and science.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (PSC 001 or PSC 001Y or PSC 001V); (PSC 041 or PSC 041V); upper division standing or consent of instructor.

title: Seminar in Psychology
description: Course Description: Intensive treatment of a special topic or problem of psychological interest.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Junior or senior standing; major in Psychology or consent of instructor.

title: Upper Division Seminar
description: Course Description: In-depth examination at an upper division level of a special topic in Psychology. Emphasis on student participation in learning. May not be repeated for credit.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor; upper division standing.

title: Fieldwork in Psychology
description: Course Description: Supervised internship off and on campus, in community and institutional settings. Maximum of 6 units may be used towards satisfaction of upper division major requirement.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor; upper division standing in Psychology.

title: Special Study for Honors Students
description: Course Description: Directed research. Supervised reading, research and writing leading to submission of a Senior Honors thesis under the direction of faculty sponsor.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor; senior standing in Psychology and qualifications for admission into college honors program; at least one course from PSC 180A, PSC 180B, PSC 180C or PSC 199 strongly recommended.

title: Special Study for Honors Students
description: Course Description: Directed research. Supervised reading, research and writing leading to submission of a Senior Honors thesis under the direction of faculty sponsor.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): PSC 194HA; consent of instructor; senior standing in Psychology and qualifications for admission into college honors program; completion of PSC 194HA required.

title: Tutoring in Psychology
description: Course Description: Intended for advanced undergraduate students who will lead discussion sections in Psychology courses.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor; upper division standing.

title: Directed Group Study
description: Course Description: Directed group study. May be taught abroad.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Special Study for Advanced Undergraduates
description: Course Description: Special study for advanced undergraduates. May be taught abroad.

title: Proseminar in Psychology
description: Course Description: Introduces matriculating graduate students to research activities of departmental faculty.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing in Psychology or consent of instructor.

title: Research Preceptorship
description: Course Description: Research preceptorship.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Research Seminar
description: Course Description: Presentation of graduate research to program faculty and graduate students.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing in Psychology.

title: Data Cleaning & Management in the Social Sciences
description: Course Description: Theoretical and technical skills to build a data cleaning and management pipeline. Programming in R, applied data analysis, workflow, and data documentation.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Data Visualization in the Social Sciences
description: Course Description: Conceptual and technical skills to build data effective, evidence-based figures and graphics for the social sciences using the R programming language. Basic programming for data visualization; R programming language; ggplot2; cognitive approaches to data visualization; frequency distributions; visualizing associations; visualizing uncertainty; piecing plots together; building interactive visualizations.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): PSC 203A B or better; or consent of instructor.

title: Statistical Analysis of Psychological Experiments
description: Course Description: Probability theory, sampling distributions, statistical inference, and hypothesis testing using standard parametric and correlational approaches. Analysis of variance, factorial and repeated measures, and tests of trends.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): STA 102; or equivalent; graduate standing in Psychology or consent of instructor.

title: Causal Modeling of Correlational Data
description: Course Description: Examination of how to make causal inferences from correlational data in the behavioral sciences. Emphasis is on testing rival causal models using correlations among observed variables. Beginning with multiple regression analysis, discussion advances to path analysis and related techniques.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): PSC 204A; or the equivalent course and graduate standing in Psychology or consent of instructor.

title: Advanced Statistical Inference from Psychological Experiments
description: Course Description: Advanced topics in statistical inference, which may include probability theory, sampling distributions, statistical inference and hypothesis testing, nonparametric statistics, Bayesian approaches, and advanced issues in analysis of variance.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): PSC 204A; or the equivalent; graduate standing in Psychology or consent of instructor.

title: Applied Multivariate Analysis of Psychological Data
description: Course Description: Review of the major methods of multivariate data analysis for psychological data. Students will program statistical routines using a linear algebra-based computing language. Topics will include multivariate analysis of variance. discriminant analysis, canonical analysis factor analysis, and component analysis.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): PSC 204A; PSC 204B; PSC 204D; or consent of instructor.

title: Factor Analysis
description: Course Description: Theory and methods of factor analysis, including exploratory factor analysis, confirmatory factor analysis, and principal component analysis.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): PSC 204A; PSC 204B; or equivalent courses, or consent of instructor; graduate standing.

title: Structural Equation Modeling
description: Course Description: Theory and methods of structural equation modeling, including path analysis, confirmatory factor analysis, multiple-group modeling and latent growth curve modeling.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): PSC 204A; PSC 204B; or equivalent courses, or consent of instructor; graduate standing.

title: Multilevel Models
description: Course Description: Introduction to statistical techniques for the analysis of normal, hierarchically structured data, such as cross-sectional clustered data or repeated-measures data. Topics include hierarchical linear models, latent growth curve models, and how these methods handle unbalanced and/or missing data.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): PSC 204A; graduate standing or consent of instructor.

title: Applied Psychometrics & Measurement Theory
description: Course Description: Examination of the basic principles and applications of classical and modern test theory. Topics include test construction, reliability theory, validity theory, factor analysis, and latent trait theory.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): PSC 204A; graduate standing in Psychology or consent of instructor.

title: Item Response Theory
description: Course Description: Item response theory allows for the creation of precise measurement instruments in psychological testing. Review Classical Test Theory, and then cover basic IRT models through advanced applications.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): PSC 204A; or the equivalent; graduate standing in Psychology or consent of instructor.

title: Applied Longitudinal Data Analysis
description: Course Description: Modeling and understanding of intraindividual change and interindividual differences in change. Reviews conventional methods and introduces contemporary techniques for modeling intraindividual change.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): PSC 204A; graduate standing in Psychology or consent of instructor.

title: Applied Bayesian Statistics for Social Scientists
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Bayesian methodology and inference for social scientists. Basics of probability theory, Bayes' theorem, inference and model checking, discussion of common distributions, sampling approaches, Bayes factors for hypothesis testing and introduction to dedicated software.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): PSC 204B; or consent of instructor.

title: Theoretical Foundations: Research Methods in Psychology
description: Course Description: Examines the philosophy and research practices underlying experimental psychology. Topics to be covered include philosophy of science/epistemology, research design, inference and bias in research, theory development, validity, the social context of research, and critical thinking about research.

title: Research Methods in Psychology: Applications in Social-Personality Research
description: Course Description: Overview of the research designs, assessment methods, and statistical procedure used by social-personality psychologists. Focus on the practical issues that arise when using each method in specific research contexts.

title: Survey & Questionnaire Research Methods
description: Course Description: Survey and questionnaire research methods with emphasis on how to ask questions. Cognitive, motivational, and social processes that influence how respondents answer questions; sampling techniques; Internet resources; practical aspects of fielding survey and questionnaire research.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Completion of a course on social or behavioral research methods; graduate standing.

title: Physiological Psychology
description: Course Description: A conceptual analysis of the contributions of neuroanatomy, neurophysiology and neurochemistry to an understanding of animal and human behavior.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing in Psychology or consent of instructor.

title: Fundamentals of Human Electrophysiology
description: Course Description: In-depth introduction and hands-on experience with the event-related potential (ERP) method in the study of attention, executive control, memory, language and social cognitive neuroscience.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Introduction to Programming: Matlab
description: Course Description: The Matlab programming environment as a means of organizing, analyzing, and visualizing scientific data. Basic programming concepts such as variables, loops, conditional branching, and efficient programming techniques will be emphasized.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing or consent of instructor.

title: Fundamentals of Cognitive Neuroimaging
description: Course Description: Introduction to empirical foundations and methodology of neuroimaging, emphasizing pragmatics of functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI) to study cognition. Topics include MR physics, the relationship between neural activity and the BOLD response, experimental design, and analysis of fMRI data.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Basic knowledge of inferential statistics and experimental Psychology.

title: Advanced Topics in Neuroimaging
description: Course Description: Critical presentation and discussion of the most influential advanced issues in neuroimaging, emphasizing fMRI design/analysis and the integration of fMRI with EEG/MEG.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): PSC 210; or consent of instructor.

title: Developmental Psychology: Cognitive & Perceptual Development
description: Course Description: Theories and empirical findings concerning human cognitive and perceptual development. Development of perception, memory, concepts (e.g., theory of mind, concepts about number), problem solving, and language from infancy to adolescence.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing or consent of instructor; completion of an undergraduate or graduate course on developmental psychology or human development.

title: Developmental Psychology: Social, Emotional, & Personality Development
description: Course Description: Theories and empirical findings concerning human social, emotional, and personality development. Development of emotions, moral reasoning and behavior, personality, self-concept, and social cognition from infancy to adolescence (may include adulthood).
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing or consent of instructor; completion of an undergraduate or graduate course on developmental psychology or human development.

title: Developmental Cognitive Neuroscience
description: Course Description: Introduction to theories and methods, such as EEG, fNIRS, and fMRI, that inform cognitive development in infants and children. Classic and cutting-edge articles illustrate the value and challenges of using developmental cognitive neuroscience to study perception, action, language, and social cognition.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing or consent of instructor; completion of an undergraduate or graduate course on any of the following topics: developmental psychology, human development, neuroscience, cognitive neuroscience.

title: Behavioral Genetics
description: Course Description: Review basic principles in genetics and select topics in molecular genetics with emphasis on behavior. Use of modern molecular methods to outline complex relationships between genes, environment, and behavior.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing.

title: Fundamentals of Animal Behavior
description: Course Description: Survey of the phenomena and theory of animal behavior from the perspectives of multiple biological disciplines, including evolution, ecology, psychology, genetics, neurobiology, endocrinology, and animal science.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor; upper division undergraduate introduction to the biology of behavior, such as PSC 101, PSC 122, PSC 123, NPB 102, NPB 150, NPB 152, WFC 141, ENT 104, or ANS 105.

title: Fundamentals of Animal Behavior
description: Course Description: Survey of the phenomena and theory of animal behavior from the perspectives of multiple biological disciplines, including evolution, ecology, psychology, genetics, neurobiology, endocrinology, and animal science.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): PSC 209A; or consent of instructor.

title: History of Psychology
description: Course Description: A lecture-seminar on the history of psychology and on the applicability of early psychological theory and research to contemporary investigations.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing in Psychology or consent of instructor.

title: Academic Writing in Psychology
description: Course Description: Strategies for developing and honing academic writing skills and writing productivity, with a particular focus on how to write a clear and compelling empirical journal article in psychology.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Cognitive Psychology
description: Course Description: Analysis of the mental processes by which knowledge is acquired, manipulated, stored, retrieved and used.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing in Psychology or consent of instructor.

title: Sensation & Perception
