prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): VEN 123; VEN 124; VEN 128 (can be concurrent); MIC 103L.

title: Wine Technology & Winery Systems
description: Course Description: Process technologies and process systems that are used in modern commercial wineries. Lectures, demonstrations, problem solving sessions, and possible field trips. Includes grape preparation and fermentation equipment; post-fermentation processing equipment; winery utilities, cleaning systems, and waste treatment.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): PLS 021 or PLS 021V; MAT 016A; MAT 016B; ((PHY 001A, PHY 001B) or PHY 007A).

title: Distilled Beverage Technology
description: Course Description: Distillation principles and practices; production technology of brandy, whiskey, rum, vodka, gin, and other distilled beverages; characteristics of raw materials, fermentation, distillation, and aging.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 008B; FST 110A.

title: Grape & Wine Industry Regulation
description: Course Description: Regulatory and legal issues related to grape growing and winemaking. State and federal regulations for production and sale of alcoholic beverages. Land use, food and chemical safety regulations, crop insurance, wine distribution and importation, and direct-to-consumer sales.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Readings in Enology
description: Course Description: Critical evaluation of selected monographs in enology. Discussion leadership rotates among the students.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): VEN 003.

title: Winemaking Seminar
description: Course Description: Outside speakers on a specific winemaking topic chosen for the quarter. Discussion with the speaker hosted by the faculty member(s) in charge.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): VEN 003.

title: Internship
description: Course Description: Work experience related to Fermentation Science (Enology) or Plant Science (Viticulture) majors. Internships must be approved and supervised by a member of the department or major faculty, but are arranged by the student.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor; completion of 84 units.

title: Directed Group Study
description: Course Description: Directed group study. May be taught abroad.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Special Study for Advanced Undergraduates
description: Course Description: Special study for advanced undergraduates. May be taught abroad.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Introduction to Scientific Methods
description: Course Description: Processes involved in conducting scientific research. Topics covered will include conducting literature review, formulating hypotheses, and analyzing and reporting results. Students will complete an annotated bibliography and complete a written and oral research proposal.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing or consent of instructor.

title: Grape Development & Composition
description: Course Description: Anatomy, physiology and biochemistry of grape berry development, with emphasis on the development of grape composition relevant to winemaking.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (BIS 102, BIS 103) or BIS 105.

title: Flavor Chemistry of Foods & Beverages
description: Course Description: Become familiar with basic principles of flavor chemistry, analysis, and formation in fresh and processed foods. Required to read and critically evaluate flavor chemistry literature.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): CHE 008B; ((VEN 123, VEN 123L) or FST 103); or consent of instructor.

title: Sensometrics
description: Course Description: Experimental design and statistical analysis, including multivariate analysis, for both sensory and instrumental data in enology and food-related studies.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): FST 117; ((VEN 125, VEN 125L) or (FST 107A or FST 107B)); or equivalent to FTS 117.

title: Sustainable Vineyard Development
description: Course Description: Application of plant, meteorological, soil, water, GIS, and economic sciences to sustainable vineyard development. Preparation of a comprehensive study to determine the viticultural and economic feasibility of a given site for raisin, table, or wine grape production.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (VEN 101A, VEN 101B, VEN 101C); (VEN 115 or VEN 118); or consent of instructor.

title: Field & GIS Evaluation of Soils
description: Course Description: Principles and practices used to evaluate agricultural soils in the field, including soil pits, soil cores, electrical conductivity meters, ground penetrating radar, geomorphology and surface terrain analysis. Use of geographic information sciences, soil databases, digital elevation models and geostatistics.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): PLS 120; (PLS 205 or PLS 206); (SSC 100 or SSC 105 or SSC 107); VEN 101C; consent of instructor; ABT 180 is recommended.

title: Natural Products of Wine
description: Course Description: Structure, occurrence, and changes due to wine production to the natural products found in wine. Chemicals with a sensory impact will be emphasized, including flavonoids and other phenolics, terpenes and norisoprenoids, pyrazines, oak volatiles and other wine constituents.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): VEN 123; VEN 124; or natural products background, and consent of instructor.

title: Instrumental Analysis of Must & Wine
description: Course Description: Theory and practice of instrumental analysis of wines and musts. mphasis on the principles of analytical techniques (e.g., CE, GC, HPLC, Mass Spectrometry) and factors determining correct choice of instrumental method.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): VEN 123 or FST 103; ((BIS 102 and BIS 103) or BIS 105), (CHE 107B or CHE 115) recommended.

title: Advances in the Science of Winemaking
description: Course Description: Selected topics in the science and technology of winemaking. Topics will be drawn from current research of participating enology and viticulture faculty. Critical analysis of the technical content of published material will be emphasized.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): VEN 125; VEN 126; or consent of instructor; graduate standing.

title: Advanced Sensory Analysis of Wines
description: Course Description: Sensory descriptive analysis experiments will be designed and conducted using standard sensory science methods.Data will be analyzed by analyses of variance, principal component analyses and generalized Procrustes analysis to evaluate the judges performance and interpret the significance of the results.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ((VEN 124, VEN 125) or FST 107); AMR 120; or the equivalent.

title: Winery Design
description: Course Description: Design of wineries. Includes process calculations, equipment selection, process layout and building choice and siting. Project scheduling, capital costs, and ten-year cash flow analysis for the winery. One field trip required.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): VEN 124; VEN 135; or consent of instructor.

title: Critical Evaluation of Scientific Literature
description: Course Description: Contemporary research topics in biological sciences. Discussion of recent research articles in a special topic area chosen by instructor. Intended to develop skills in critical evaluation of scientific publications.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Seminar
description: Course Description: Seminar.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Advanced Research Conference
description: Course Description: Planning and results of research programs, proposals, and experiments. Discussion and critical evaluation of original research being conducted by the group. Discussion led by individual research instructors for research group.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor; graduate standing.

title: Advanced Viticulture
description: Course Description: Critical evaluation of scientific and popular literature on selected topics of current interest that relate viticulture to fruit or wine sensory attributes or quality.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): VEN 110; VEN 116; VEN 124; VEN 125; VEN 210 recommended.

title: Advanced Internship
description: Course Description: Work experience related to Fermentation Science (Enology) or Plant Science (Viticulture) majors. Internships must be approved and supervised by a graduate group faculty member or students major professor, but are arranged by the student.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): VEN 123; VEN 123L; VEN 124; VEN 124L; VEN 125; VEN 125L; VEN 126; VEN 126L; VEN 128; VEN 128L; and consent of instructor.

title: Tutoring in Viticulture & Enology
description: Course Description: Designed for graduate students who desire teaching experience, but are not teaching assistants. Student contact primarily in laboratory or discussion sections, and under direction of a faculty member.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor; graduate standing.

title: Group Study
description: Course Description: Group study.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Research
description: Course Description: Research.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Teaching Assistant Training Practicum
description: Course Description: Teaching assistant training practicum.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing.

title: Research
description: Course Description: Research.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): First-, second- and third-year standing in the School of Veterinary Medicine.

title: Professional & Clinical Skills — Y1
description: Course Description: Outline expectations and skill set required to participate successfully in the DVM curriculum as well as providing learning opportunity in communication, team-building, leadership, conflict management, stress management, critical thinking, professional behaviors, history taking, physical examination skills and medical record keeping.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): First-year standing in the School of Veterinary Medicine.

title: Basic Foundations
description: Course Description: Essential basic information regarding histology, general pathology, biochemistry, pharmacology and population health that is foundational to all blocks.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): First-year standing in the School of Veterinary Medicine.

title: Heme/Lymph/Coag
description: Course Description: The lexicon, morphology, production, structure and function of peripheral blood cells, their bone marrow precursors and the lymphoid system, the process and regulation of hemostasis, and the laboratory evaluation of all of these in health and disease.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): First-year standing in the School of Veterinary Medicine.

title: Musculoskeletal
description: Course Description: Anatomy, histology, and physiology necessary for understanding form and function of the bones, joints, muscles, and tendons in producing locomotion; and the pathologic processes that produce typical responses to injury of musculoskeletal structures in domestic animals.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): First-year standing in the School of Veterinary Medicine.

title: Neuroscience/Senses/Behavior
description: Course Description: Establish a basis for clinical neurology, ophthalmology, and behavior by providing an integrated study of normal neurobiology, neuroanatomy, neurophysiology, neuropathology, neurpharmacology, neurotoxicology, ophthalmology and behavior.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): First-year standing in the School of Veterinary Medicine.

title: Gastrointestinal/Metabolism
description: Course Description: Examine interrelatedness and functions of the organs associated with the gastrointestinal tract. Applied problems in pharmacology, physical examination, diagnostic testing, and imaging are used to reinforce a basic understanding of organ anatomy, physiology, pathophysiology, and nutrition/metabolism.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): First-year standing in the School of Veterinary Medicine.

title: Pharmacology/Nutrition/Toxicology
description: Course Description: Basic principles of nutrition and toxicology as well as pathophysiologic changes, diagnostic procedures and treatments of nutritional diseases and intoxications of domestic animals and wildlife. Incorporate case examples and directed self-learning to reinforce important concepts.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor; first-year standing in the School of Veterinary Medicine.

title: Connections — Y1
description: Course Description: Integrate basic and preclinical material working with senior student in a clinical environment.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): First-year standing in the School of Veterinary Medicine.

title: Cardiology/Respiratory
description: Course Description: Normal cardiovascular and respiratory system biology and the integrated response of those systems to injury or disease.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Second-year standing in the School of Veterinary Medicine.

title: Renal/Urinary
description: Course Description: Examine and explore the normal morphology (gross and microscopic), physiology, pathology and imaging of the upper and lower urinary tract in multiple species.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Second-year standing in the School of Veterinary Medicine.

title: Endocrinology/Reproduction
description: Course Description: Anatomy and histology of endocrine glands and reproductive systems in males and females. Understand the physiology and pathology at the molecular, cellular and organismal level.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Second-year standing in the School of Veterinary Medicine.

title: Skin
description: Course Description: Structure and function of the skin and specialized keratinized structures in veterinary species. Review mechanisms by which skin responds to perturbations and discuss wound healing and skin pathology.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Second-year standing in the School of Veterinary Medicine.

title: Oncology
description: Course Description: Basic mechanisms, etiology, causes and the approach to diagnosis and treatment of cancer with emphasis on veterinary patients.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Second-year standing in the School of Veterinary Medicine.

title: Immunology/Infectious Disease
description: Course Description: Integrate immunology, microbiology, virology, parasitology of common infectious, zoonotic and foreign animal diseases.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Second-year standing in the School of Veterinary Medicine.

title: Population Health
description: Course Description: Principles of evidence-based medicine, study design and inference, and disease transmission in populations. Topics include biostatistics, outbreak investigation and response, diagnostic tests, vaccine strategies, food safety, foreign animal diseases, and impact of environmental health on animals and humans.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Second-year standing in the School of Veterinary Medicine.

title: Clinical Foundations
description: Course Description: Essential concepts that are shared across year three blocks to include foundational material in anesthesia, surgery and emergency medicine with cases that include interpretation of clinical pathology and imaging.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Second-year standing in the School of Veterinary Medicine.

title: Connections — Y2
description: Course Description: Integrate basic and preclinical material working with a senior student in a clinical environment.Prerequisite(s): Enrollment Restrictions: Credit Limitation(s): Cross Listing: Repeat Credit: General Education: Grade Mode: S/U only.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Second-year standing in the School of Veterinary Medicine.

title: Professional & Clinical Skills — Y2
description: Course Description: Continue to build on foundations from freshman year in communication, history-taking, client interactions, team-building, conflict and stress management and professional behaviors.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Second-year standing in the School of Veterinary Medicine.

title: Core Stream
description: Course Description: Longitudinal stream to include law, ethics, regulatory medicine, business, communication and disaster medicine.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Third-year standing in the School of Veterinary Medicine.

title: Anesthesia & Surgical Stream
description: Course Description: Introduction to surgical anatomy, operative and anesthetic skills.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Third-year standing in the School of Veterinary Medicine.

title: Comparative Stream I
description: Course Description: Approach commonly encountered diseases and medical problems across multiple species. Development of problem-solving skills related to the medical problems of all species from the level of individual animals to groups/herds/populations.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Third-year standing in the School of Veterinary Medicine.

title: Comparative Stream II
description: Course Description: Approach commonly encountered diseases and medical problems across multiple species. Development of problem-solving skills related to the medical problems of all species from the level of individual animals to groups/herds/populations.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Third-year standing in the School of Veterinary Medicine.

title: Comparative Stream III
description: Course Description: Approach commonly encountered diseases and medical problems across multiple species. Development of problem-solving skills related to the medical problems of all species from the level of individual animals to groups/herds/populations.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Third-year standing in the School of Veterinary Medicine.

title: Small Animal Stream I
description: Course Description: Clinical manifestations, diagnostic methods, and the medical and surgical approaches to the infectious, musculoskeletal, neurologic and ophthalmologic diseases of small animals.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Third-year standing in the School of Veterinary Medicine.

title: Small Animal Stream II
description: Course Description: Clinical manifestations, diagnostic methods, and the medical and surgical approaches to the dermatologic, dental, and gastrointestinal diseases of small animals as well as preventative medicine and nutrition.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Third-year standing in the School of Veterinary Medicine.

title: Small Animal Stream III
description: Course Description: Clinical manifestations, diagnostic methods, and the medical and surgical approaches to the cardiorespiratory, endocrine, and urinary medicine as well as diagnosis and therapy of oncologic diseases in small animals. Introduction to companion, avian, and exotic pet medicine and surgery.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Third-year standing in the School of Veterinary Medicine.

title: Large Animal Stream I
description: Course Description: Fundamentals of entry level large animal medicine and surgery to promote development of problem-solving skills for diagnosis, treatment and control of various disease conditions.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Third- and/or fourth-year standing in the School of Veterinary Medicine.

title: Large Animal Stream II
description: Course Description: Continuation of fundamentals of entry level large animal medicine and surgery to promote development of problem-solving skills for diagnosis, treatment and control of various disease conditions.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Third- and/or fourth-year standing in the School of Veterinary Medicine.

title: Large Animal Stream—Equine Emphasis
description: Course Description: Fundamental and advanced levels of equine medicine, surgery, lameness and reproduction. Focus on management of individual equids.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Third- and/or fourth-year standing in the School of Veterinary Medicine.

title: Large Animal Stream—Livestock Emphasis
description: Course Description: Fundamental and advanced levels of livestock medicine and surgery of individual animals and population health. Focus on all livestock species including cattle, sheep, goats, pigs and poultry.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Third- and/or fourth-year standing in the School of Veterinary Medicine.

title: Large Animal Stream—Zoological Medicine Emphasis
description: Course Description: Taxon- and topic-based approach to learning zoological medicine. Anatomy, physiology, nutrition, management, anesthesia, medicine, surgery and pathology on non-domestic free-ranging and captive species.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Third- and/or fourth-year standing in the School of Veterinary Medicine.

title: Companion Small Mammals
description: Course Description: Fundamental understanding of the etiology, clinical presentation, diagnostic evaluation, treatment, prevention, and control of medical diseases of companion small exotic mammals.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Third-year standing in the School of Veterinary Medicine.

title: Reptile & Avian Health
description: Course Description: Organized by organ systems to present reptile and avian health issues. Overview of applied anatomy, physiology and clinical pathology of common species will be provided through laboratory opportunities and discussion of common diseases and treatments.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Third-year standing in the School of Veterinary Medicine.

title: Poultry
description: Course Description: Introduction to practical poultry medicine. Gross pathology, biosecurity and basic diagnostic tests. Visits to hatcheries, broiler, layer and turkey farms.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Third-year standing in the School of Veterinary Medicine.

title: Laboratory Animal
description: Course Description: Basics of laboratory animal medicine including diseases, medicine, and surgery and comparative biology of the most common species and introduction to regulations. Combination of lecture, wet labs, and projects.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor; third-year standing in the School of Veterinary Medicine.

title: Business & Communication
description: Course Description: Develop professional communication and business skills that are directly applicable in veterinary clinical practice by actively participating in a wide variety of interactive learning opportunities.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Third- and fourth year standing in the School of Veterinary Medicine.

title: Clinical Pathology
description: Course Description: Application of problem-solving and microscopy skills to laboratory data and laboratory specimens. Sections to include hematology, immunology, body fluids, exfoliative cytology, urinalysis, microbiology and clinical chemistry data and interpretation.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Third- and fourth year standing in the School of Veterinary Medicine.

title: Advanced Clinical Skills & Hospital Operations
description: Course Description: Development of advanced clinical skills using skills learned in years 1–3 of the DVM curriculum as a foundation. Detailed orientation to the VMTH and VMACS, with a focus on global hospital operations that will prepare students for case management in year 4 of the DVM curriculum and for clinical practice following graduation. Some evenings and weekends required.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor; third- and fourth-year standing in the School of Veterinary Medicine.

title: Internship
description: Course Description: Internship experience off and on campus in the subject of veterinary science. Internships are supervised by a member of the faculty.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Internship
description: Course Description: Work experience off and on campus in all subject areas offered in the Department of Molecular Biosciences. Internships supervised by a member of the faculty.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor; lower division standing.

title: Principles of Pharmacology & Toxicology
description: Course Description: Online course provides training in core concepts of pharmacological and toxicological sciences and prepares to develop higher-order problem solving and critical thinking skills. Designed for advanced undergraduate students with interests in pursuing graduate degrees in pharmacology, toxicology, physiological sciences, and for students with an interest in pursuing D.V.M., M.D., Pharmacy, Dentistry and Nursing professional degrees. Students who pursue careers in environmental sciences, public health management, and epidemiology may also benefit from the subject matter presented in this course.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor; upper division standing in a science major; chemistry through organic chemistry, general biology, or consent of instructor; good standing with the university; computing capability (use MS Word®, Excel®, PowerPoint®, menu driven software programs, Course LMS); own a computer or have ready access to a computer with broadband Internet access; NPB 101 and BIS 104 recommended.

title: Internship
description: Course Description: Work experience off and on campus in all subject areas offered by the Department of Molecular Biosciences. Internships supervised by a member of the faculty.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor; completion of 84 units.

title: Directed Group Study
description: Course Description: Directed group study.

title: Special Study for Advanced Undergraduates
description: Course Description: Special study for advanced undergraduates.

title: Oxidative Stress & Free Radical Signaling
description: Course Description: Nature of nitrogen and oxygen radicals, their role in health, disease, medicine, toxicology, pharmacology, and related disciplines. Free radicals, antioxidants, and biological pathways. Clinical cases of increased oxidative stress.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Advanced undergraduates, graduates, and professional students with a solid background in biochemistry and physiology.

title: Current Topics in Neurotoxicology
description: Course Description: General principles of neurotoxicology, the cell and molecular mechanisms and health impacts of specific neurotoxicants and the contribution of neurotoxic compounds to complex neurodevelopmental disorders and neurodegenerative diseases.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Core courses in one of the following graduate programs: Pharmacology & Toxicology (PTX), Agricultural & Environmental Chemistry (AGC), Biochemistry & Molecular Biology (BMB), Cell & Developmental Biology (CDB), Immunology (IMM), Molecular Cellular & Integrative Physiology (MCP) or Neuroscience (NSC).

title: Metabolism of Toxicants & Drugs
description: Course Description: Significance/chemical pathways of toxicants and drug metabolism, enzymology and molecular aspects of P450 and flavin monooxygenases, hydrolases and phase 2 transferases and experimental approaches for metabolism studies.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): PTX 201; PTX 202; PTX 203; general biochemistry or consent of instructor.

title: Toxicology of the Respiratory System
description: Course Description: Survey of structure and function of the respiratory system, the pathophysiology of major lung diseases, the interactions of toxicants with the lung and response of this organ to injury.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): PTX 201; PTX 202; PTX 203; or consent of instructor.

title: Pharmacokinetics & Biopharmaceuticals
description: Course Description: In-depth study of pharmacokinetics, including the fundamentals of pharmacokinetics, how to design a pharmacokinetic study and how to use both compartmental and non-compartmental analysis to interpret the data.

title: Seminar
description: Course Description: Topics in nutrition, pharmacology/toxicology, and biochemistry.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor; graduate standing.

title: Tutoring in Graduate Molecular Biosciences
description: Course Description: Assist in preparation and teaching of courses in Nutrition, Pharmacology and Toxicology, or other courses offered by the department under direct supervision of the instructor. Designed for graduate or professional students who desire teaching experience in graduate courses.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor; graduate or professional student standing.

title: Group Study
description: Course Description: Description:

title: Research
description: Course Description: Research.

title: Tutoring in Molecular Biosciences
description: Course Description: Experience in professional curriculum for graduate or professional students, not teaching assistants, under direct supervision of instructor.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor. Graduate or professional student standing.

title: Global Population, Health, & Environment
description: Course Description: Students critically examine multi-scale processes involving human, animal, and ecosystem health. Online team and independent work engage local and global topics around population pressures on environments and environmental pressures on populations.

title: Knights Landing One Health Center
description: Course Description: Internship at Knights Landing One Health Center (KLOHC) for undergraduate pre-veterinary student. Application of veterinary and One Health concepts to their work at the center.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Knights Landing One Health Center Lab
description: Course Description: Internship at Knights Landing One Health Center (KLOHC) for undergraduate pre-veterinary students. Interns may offer technical help, assist with patient registration and records, animal handling, and foreign language interpretation.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): VME 125 (can be concurrent); and consent of instructor.

title: Infectious Disease in Ecology & Conservation
description: Course Description: Introduction to the dynamics and control of infectious disease in wildlife, including zoonotic diseases and those threatening endangered species. Basic epidemiological models and application to field data. Scientists' role in developing disease control policies.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): BIS 002A; BIS 002B; BIS 002C; or equivalent. EVE 100 is recommended.

title: Directed Group Study
description: Course Description: Directed group study. May be taught abroad.

title: Special Studies for Advanced Undergraduates
description: Course Description: Special studies for advanced undergraduates. May be taught abroad.

title: Evaluation & Use of Diagnostic Tests
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Clinical and epidemiologic properties and application of diagnostic tests for disease, with emphasis on selecting tests; validating, evaluating, and interpreting new tests individually and in aggregate; determining cutoff values; and developing testing strategies.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): MPM 205 or EPI 205; consent of instructor.

title: Viral Pathogenesis Seminar/Journal Club
description: Course Description: Participatory seminar addressing the mechanisms of retroviral pathogenesis in a journal club format. Focus on the review of current scientific journal papers concerning viral pathogenesis, immunology and virology with a special focus on retroviruses.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor; graduate student status in the Comparative Pathology, Microbiology or Immunology graduate groups.

title: Infectious Disease in Ecology & Conservation
description: Course Description: Presentation, analysis and discussion of primary literature on the dynamics and control of infectious disease in wildlife, including zoonotic diseases and those threatening endangered species. Multidisciplinary approach combines perspectives of ecology and veterinary medicine.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): VME 158 must be taken concurrently.

title: Group Study
description: Course Description: Group study in selected areas of the clinical sciences.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Student in School of Veterinary Medicine or consent of instructor.

title: Research
description: Course Description: Research.

title: Cardiology Journal Club
description: Course Description: Critical evaluation of scientific articles relevant to cardiology; including evaluation of hypothesis/objectives, study design, experimental and statistical methods, results, conclusions, references and new applications.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Resident status in the Veterinary Medical Teaching Hospital.

title: Large Animal Resident Seminar
description: Course Description: Review organ-system based large animal internal medicine topics to prepare large animal residents for boards.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Resident status in the Veterinary Medical Teaching Hospital.

title: Companion Animal & Exotic Pet Journal Club
description: Course Description: Current evaluation of scientific articles in zoological companion animals, including evaluation of the hypothesis/objectives, study design, methods (experimental, statistical), results, conclusions, references, and new applications.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Resident status in the Veterinary Medical Teaching Hospital.

title: Hemodialysis Academy
description: Course Description: Delivered in collaboration with multiple entities to offer an Advanced Certification for Extracorporeal Therapies.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Resident status in the Veterinary Medical Teaching Hospital.

title: Aquatic Animal Health Journal Club
description: Course Description: Review current trends in aquatic animal health from both the veterinary and scientific literature.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Resident status in the Veterinary Medical Teaching Hospital.

title: Small Animal Medicine Physiology & Pathophysiology Review
description: Course Description: Review organ-system-based physiology and pathophysiology to prepare residents for board examinations.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Resident status in Veterinary Medical Teaching Hospital.

title: Dermatology Journal Club & Seminars in Veterinary & Comparative Dermatology
description: Course Description: Critical evaluation of refereed journal articles, textbooks, and proceedings within the disciplines of dermatology, pathology, or immunology.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Resident status in the Veterinary Medical Teaching Hospital.

title: Dermatopathology Conference & Seminar
description: Course Description: Presentation of interesting "unknown" cases by dermatopathologists for residents to describe lesions.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Resident status in the Veterinary Medical Teaching Hospital.

title: Zoological Medicine Journal Club
description: Course Description: Provide coverage of broad range of topics in zoological medicine, captive wildlife, free-ranging wildlife, terrestrial mammals, marine mammals, birds, reptiles, amphibians, and fish to assist residents in preparation for board examination and improve knowledge and proficiency in zoological practice.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Resident status in the Veterinary Medical Teaching Hospital.

title: Laboratory Animal Medicine Resident Seminar
description: Course Description: Laws and regulations, biology, animal models, journal review, different diagnostic techniques, anesthesia, pathology.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Resident status in the Veterinary Medical Teaching Hospital.

title: Small Animal Internal Medicine Journal Club
description: Course Description: Review of current internal medicine literature to include topics in endocrinology, renal medicine, gastrointestinal disorders, cardiorespiratory medicine, and infectious disease. Focus on critical review of scientific design and methodology, interpretation of results, and relevance to clinical practice.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Resident status in the Veterinary Medical Teaching Hospital.

title: Large Animal Medicine Journal Club
description: Course Description: Review current internal medicine literature to include topics in equine and livestock infectious diseases, organ-specific disorders (cadiovascular system, gastrointestinal tract, urogenital tract, central nervous system, musculoskeletal apparatus, skin, respiratory tract), endocrinology, animal welfare and diagnostic modalities.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Fish Medicine
description: Course Description: Focuses on fundamental concepts in fish medicine. Topics include preventing, diagnosis and treatment of important diseases in research, pet and cultured fish. Taught through didactics and laboratory sessions.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Special Study for Undergraduates
description: Course Description: Special study for undergraduates.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Special Study for Advanced Undergraduates
description: Course Description: Special study for advanced undergraduates.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Group Study
description: Course Description: Group study.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Research
description: Course Description: Research.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Comparative Dentistry & Oral Surgery
description: Course Description: Reviews of the current literature pertaining to comparative oral biology, surgery and medicine and related basic sciences; half of the sessions are based on topics assigned by the course leader, while the other half consist of critical reviews of recent papers chosen by the participants.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Residents in the Veterinary Medical Teaching Hospital; graduate students, veterinarians enrolled in training programs leading to board certification in veterinary dentistry, AVDC Diplomates and dentists with consent of the instructor.

title: Diagnostic Imaging Journal Discussion
description: Course Description: Review of current medical and veterinary diagnostic imaging literature. Focus on scientific methodology, content and relevance to clinical practice.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Resident status at the Veterinary Medical Teaching Hospital.

title: Known Case Conference--Imaging
description: Course Description: Film review of current and past Veterinary Medical Teaching Hospital proven cases. Intended for radiology residents and others with background in diagnostic imaging.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Resident status at the Veterinary Medical Teaching Hospital.

title: Diagnostic Imaging: Wildlife & Special Species Rounds
description: Course Description: Film review of current and past wildlife and special species cases from Veterinary Medical Teaching Hospital and other locations.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Resident status at the Veterinary Medical Teaching Hospital.

title: Small Animal Orthopedics Conference
description: Course Description: Current cases and literature pertaining to small animal orthopedics.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Resident status at the Veterinary Medical Teaching Hospital.

title: House Officers Professional Education Series
description: Course Description: Monthly one-hour lecture series includes topics such as time management, teaching skills, communication skills, suicide prevention, managing burning and compassion fatigue, and conflict resolution, among others. Year-long professional education curriculum aiming at tackling high burnout levels. May be repeated for credit up to 12.00 Time(s) Without limitation each term/semester, including summers.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of Instructor.

title: Program in Resident Ophthalmic Microsurgical Training
description: Course Description: Goal of this program is to set a firm foundation for the developing microsurgeon in terms of approach, knowledge and specific fundamental surgical skills.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Residents in the Veterinary Medical Teaching Hospital, specifically in Ophthalmology program, or consent of instructor.

title: Topics in Surgery/Oncology
description: Course Description: Discussion of topics relevant to surgery and oncology with special focus on new treatments, recommendations, and modalities.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Resident status at the Veterinary Medical Teaching Hospital.

title: Veterinary Cancer Biology: Mechanisms of Disease
description: Course Description: Survey of contemporary literature regarding the biology of cancer with particular reference to mechanisms underlying tumorigenesis in domestic animals.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Resident status at the Veterinary Medical Teaching Hospital.

title: Oncology Journal Discussion
description: Course Description: Review of current medical oncology and radiation oncology literature. Focus on scientific methodology, content and relevance to clinical practice. Covers both veterinary and human medical journals.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Resident status at the Veterinary Medical Teaching Hospital.

title: Radiation Oncology Journal Discussion
description: Course Description: Review and discussion of radiation oncology literature with focus on normal tissue effects, human radiation therapy advances, and critical review of current veterinary literature. Focused analysis of historical articles on specific radiation protocols and tumor types.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Resident status at the Veterinary Medical Teaching Hospital.

title: Combined Emergency/Critical Care Grand Rounds
description: Course Description: Residents or Faculty will present a case with an in depth discussion of the pathophysiologic topic of interest from the case on a once a month basis.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor. Resident status in the Veterinary Medical Teaching Hospital.

title: Graduate Veterinary Neurology/Neurosurgery
description: Course Description: Diagnosis and medical/surgical treatment of neurological diseases of animals to include relevant neurologic and neurosurgical topics from human medicine.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Resident status in the Veterinary Medical Teaching Hospital or consent of instructor.

title: Advanced Veterinary Neurosurgery
description: Course Description: Overview of the diagnosis and treatment of neurological disease in small animals with an emphasis on neurosurgery.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Resident status in Small Animal Surgery or Neurology/Neurosurgery in the Veterinary Medical Teaching Hospital or consent of instructor.

title: Clinical Neuromuscular/Neuropathology Conference
description: Course Description: Case discussions and review of neuropathology and neuromuscular disease.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Resident status at the Veterinary Medical Teaching Hospital or consent of instructor.

title: Small Animal Emergency Intern Rounds
description: Course Description: Review of physiology and topics pertinent to small animal emergency receiving.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor. Intern status in the Veterinary Medical Teaching Hospital.

title: Small Animal Emergency/Critical Care Journal Discussion
description: Course Description: Review of current medical and veterinary emergency and critical care literature. Focus on scientific methodology, content and relevance to clinical practice.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Resident status at the Veterinary Medical Teaching Hospital.

title: Small Animal Emergency/Critical Care Physiology Rounds
description: Course Description: Review of physiology and topics pertinent to small animal emergency and critical care.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Resident status in the Veterinary Medical Teaching Hospital.

title: Veterinary Ophthalmology Slide Review
description: Course Description: Review and critical evaluation of 35 mm projection slides involving clinical and microscopic depictions of normal and abnormal conditions seen in the field of veterinary ophthalmology. Discussion of current treatment modalities, diagnostic capabilities and other related and relevant issues.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Residents in Veterinary Medical Teaching Hospital, specifically in the Ophthalmology program, or students with consent of instructor.

title: Veterinary Ophthalmology Literature Review
description: Course Description: Survey and critical evaluation of contemporary literature in or related to the field of veterinary ophthalmology.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Residents in Veterinary Medical Teaching Hospital, specifically in the Ophthalmology program, or students with consent of instructor.

title: Advanced Topics in Comparative Ophthalmology
description: Course Description: Presentation and critical evaluation of advanced topics in or related to the field of comparative ophthalmology.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Residents in the Veterinary Medical Teaching Hospital, specifically in Ophthalmology program or consent of instructor.

title: Principles of Veterinary Radiation Oncology
description: Course Description: Principles and practice of veterinary radiation therapy. The topics will include a series of lectures on physical methods of radiation therapy, biological effects of therapeutic radiation and applications in veterinary patients.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): House Officers in the Veterinary Medical Teaching Hospital. Graduate students or veterinary students upon consent of instructor.

title: Equine Surgery Journal Discussion
description: Course Description: Current veterinary literature and other related topics in preparation for board certification in the American College of Veterinary Surgeons. Critical evaluation of journal articles for content, methodology and statistical methods.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): VSR 471R (can be concurrent); Residents in the Veterinary Medical Teaching Hospital or students with consent of instructor. VSR 471R required concurrently.

title: Equine Surgery Case Management Conference
description: Course Description: Discussion of soft tissue, orthopedic and lameness clinical disorders that focus on pathophysiology of disease, appropriate treatment options, and evaluation of prognosis. Simulation of mock oral examination for the American College of Veterinary Surgeons board examination.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): VSR 470R (can be concurrent); Residents in the Veterinary Medical Teaching Hospital or students with consent of instructor. VSR 470R required concurrently.

title: Clinical Soft Tissue Surgery Conference
description: Course Description: Review the current medical literature and discuss the presentation, diagnosis and treatment of small animal surgical conditions, review the morbidity and mortality of clinical cases and provide mock examinations in preparation for ACVS specialty boards.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Open to students with the DVM or equivalent degree who are residents in specialty training. Graduate students in a related discipline with consent of instructor.

title: Anesthesia/Critical Care Basic Science Management Conference
description: Course Description: Physiology, pharmacology and clinical practice as it relates to anesthetic management of veterinary patients.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Residents in the Veterinary Medical Teaching Hospital or consent of instructor.

title: Anesthesia/Critical Care Case Management Conference
description: Course Description: Discussion of Veterinary Medical Teaching Hospital case material to illustrate specific medical problems and their preventive and corrective management as it pertains to anesthesia and critical care.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Residents in the Veterinary Medical Teaching Hospital.

title: Anesthesia/Critical Patient Care Journal Discussion
description: Course Description: Review of current medical and veterinary anesthesia literature. Discussion will focus on scientific methodology, content and relevance to clinical practice.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Resident status at the Veterinary Medical Teaching Hospital.

title: Large Animal Ultrasound Journal Discussion
description: Course Description: Review of current medical and veterinary diagnostic imaging literature with a focus on large animal ultrasonography. Emphasis is on scientific methodology, content and relevance to clinical practice.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Intern/fellow status in Large Animal Ultrasound at the Veterinary Medical Teaching Hospital.

title: Large Animal Ultrasound Case Conference
description: Course Description: Discuss VMTH large animal ultrasound case material to illustrate specific surgical, medical and lameness problems.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Intern/fellow in Large Animal Ultrasound at the Veterinary Medical Teaching Hospital.

title: Gun Violence
description: Course Description: Gun violence, viewed from the perspectives of criminology and public health. Topics include personal and societal contributing factors and critical assessment of potential solutions.

title: Internship in the UC Davis Washington Program
description: Course Description: Internship in Washington, DC with associated, supervised research project.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): WAS 193 (can be concurrent); junior or senior standing, admission in the UC Davis Washington Program. WAS 193 required concurrently.

title: Internship in the UC Davis Washington Program - Summer
description: Course Description: Summer internship in Washington, DC with associated, supervised research project.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Washington Center Research Seminar
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Core academic component of Washington Program offered every quarter. Topics coordinated with internships. Research draws on resources uniquely available in Washington, DC. Supervised preparation of extensive paper.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): WAS 192 (can be concurrent).

title: Wildlife Ecology & Conservation
description: Course Description: Introduction to the ecology and conservation of vertebrates. Complexity and severity of world problems in conserving biological diversity.

title: Natural History of California's Wild Vertebrates
description: Course Description: Examination of the natural history of California's wild vertebrates (fish, amphibians, reptiles, birds, and mammals), including their biogeography, systematics, ecology and conservation status.

title: Introduction to Conservation Biology
description: Course Description: Introduction to conservation biology including both biological and social issues related to the loss of species and habitats. Intended for students with no background in biological sciences.

title: Data Literacy for Wildlife Biologists
description: Course Description: Working with data in wildlife research. Programming in R with a focus on manipulating, summarizing, and visualizing data. Creating reproducible reports that combine code, visualization, and interpretive text. Reading scientific literature and interpreting common types of experimental and field data in ecology and wildlife biology.

title: Internship
description: Course Description: Work experience off and on campus in all subject areas offered in the department. Internships supervised by a member of the faculty.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor; lower division standing.

title: Directed Group Study
description: Course Description: Group study on focused topics in Wildlife, Fish, & Conservation Biology. Topic varies according to instructor.

title: Special Study for Undergraduates
description: Course Description: Special study for undergraduates.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Field Methods in Wildlife, Fish, & Conservation Biology
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Introduction to field methods for monitoring and studying wild vertebrates and their habitats, with an emphasis on ecology and conservation. Required weekend field trips.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): EVE 101 (can be concurrent) or ESP 100 (can be concurrent); (BIS 002A, BIS 002B, BIS 002C); or equivalent course of EVE 101 or ESP 100 (can be taken concurrently), and consent of instructor.

title: Field Research in Wildlife Ecology
description: Course Description: Field research in ecology of wild vertebrates in terrestrial environments; formulation of testable hypotheses, study design, introduction to research methodology, oral and written presentation of results. Limited enrollment.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor. One upper division course in each of ecology, statistics, and either ornithology, mammalogy, or herpetology.

title: Field Research in Wildlife Ecology: Laboratory
description: Course Description: Field research in ecology of wild vertebrates in terrestrial environments; testing ecological hypotheses through field research, application of research methodology, supervised independent research projects. Held between Labor Day and fall quarter.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): WFC 101 (can be concurrent); and consent of instructor.

title: Field Studies in Fish Biology
description: Course Description: Emphasis on theory of quantitative fish capture methods and design of individual research projects on ecology, behavior, physiology or population biology of fishes.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor. Upper division course in each of ecology, aquatic biology, fish biology, and statistics.

title: Field Studies in Fish Biology: Laboratory
description: Course Description: Field investigations of fish biology are emphasized including quantitative capture methods and individual research projects on ecology, behavior, physiology or population biology of fishes at the field site in relation to their habitats.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): WFC 102 (can be concurrent); and consent of instructor.

title: Applied Statistics for Wildlife Research
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Introduction to basic statistical concepts and methods as tools for fish and wildlife research. Application of general guiding principles of developing research questions and projects, basic probability theory, statistical estimation (correlation, regression, ANOVA, Chi-square test) and hypothesis testing. Introduction of some specialized analytical techniques, such as population dynamics modeling and time series analysis.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (MAT 016B or MAT 017B or MAT 021B); (WFC 010 or WFC 050); or consent of instructor.

title: Biology & Conservation of Wild Mammals
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Origins, evolution, diversification, and geographical and ecological distributions of mammals. Morphological, physiological, reproductive, and behavioral adaptations of mammals to their environment.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (BIS 002A, BIS 002B, BIS 002C); (EVE 101 (can be concurrent) or ESP 100 (can be concurrent)); or equivalent course to ESP 100 or EVE 101.

title: Laboratory in Biology & Conservation of Wild Mammals
description: Course Description: Laboratory exercises in the morphology, systematics, species identification, anatomy, and adaptations of wild mammals to different habitats.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): WFC 110 (can be concurrent); and consent of instructor.

title: Biology & Conservation of Wild Birds
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Phylogeny, distribution, migration, reproduction, population dynamics, behavior and physiological ecology of wild birds. Emphasis on adaptations to environments, species interactions, management, and conservation.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): BIS 002A; BIS 002B; BIS 002C; upper division ecology course recommended.

title: Laboratory in Biology & Conservation of Wild Birds
description: Course Description: Laboratory exercises in bird species identification, anatomy, molts, age and sex, specialized adaptations, behavior, research, with emphasis on conservation of wild birds. Several weekend field trips, after class bird walks, and independent bird study are required.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): WFC 111 (can be concurrent); and consent of instructor.

title: Freshwater Ecology
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Freshwater ecosystems with an emphasis on the physical, chemical, and biological organization of riverine systems. Interactions of freshwater ecosystems with neighboring riparian and terrestrial systems, food web dynamics, salmon ecology, fluvial geomorphology, and reconciliation ecology.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): EVE 101 or ESP 100; or equivalent.

title: Biology & Conservation of Fishes
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Evolution, ecology, and conservation of marine and freshwater fishes.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): BIS 002A; BIS 002B; BIS 002C; upper division ecology course recommended.

title: Laboratory in Biology & Conservation of Fishes
description: Course Description: Morphology, taxonomy, conservation, and identification of marine and freshwater fishes with emphasis on California species.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): WFC 120 (can be concurrent); and consent of instructor.

title: Physiology of Fishes
description: Course Description: Comparative physiology, growth, reproduction, behavior, and energy relations of fishes.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Upper division courses in nutrition and physiology or consent of instructor.

title: Population Dynamics & Estimation
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Description of bird, mammal and fish population dynamics, modeling philosophy, techniques for estimation of animal abundance (e.g., mark-recapture, change-inratio, etc.), mathematical models of populations (e.g., Leslie matrix, logistic, dynamic pool, stockrecruitment); case histories.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (MAT 016A, MAT 016B); (STA 013 or STA 013Y); (BIS 002A, BIS 002B, BIS 002C); or the equivalent of STA 013; an upper division course in ecology.

title: Tropical Ecology & Conservation
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Ecology and natural history of the tropics. Challenges and opportunities associated with tropical conservation. Design and communicate course-based research project.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): EVE 101 or ESP 100.

title: Conservation in Working Landscapes
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Ecology, natural history, and conservation of working landscapes. Critical evaluation of tradeoffs (and potential synergies) that arise when simultaneously pursuing conservation, food production, and human livelihood objectives in crop fields, pastures, settlements, forestry systems, and patches of semi-natural habitat.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): BIS 002B; or consent of instructor; EVE 101 or ESP 100 recommended.

title: Physiological Ecology of Wildlife
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Principles of physiological ecology, emphasizing vertebrates. Ecological, evolutionary, and behavioral perspectives on physiological mechanisms used by animals to adapt to their environment, including consideration of climate-change and other threats to biodiversity. Tropical, temperate, and polar ecosystems are highlighted.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): EVE 101 or ESP 100; (BIS 002A, BIS 002B, BIS 002C); or equivalent course to ESP 100.

title: Herpetology
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Evolution and ecology of the world’s diverse reptiles and amphibians. Emphasis on adaptations to environments, species interactions, management, and conservation.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): BIS 002A; BIS 002B; BIS 002C; upper division ecology course recommended.

title: Herpetology Laboratory
description: Course Description: Diagnostic characteristics and functional attributes of amphibians and reptiles, emphasizing ecological, bio-geographic and phylogenetic patterns. Field experience with common species of reptiles and amphibians in the Davis area.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): WFC 134 (can be concurrent); and consent of instructor.

title: Wetland Ecology & Conservation
description: Course Description: Ecology of wetlands, including wetland formation and physical factors, types of wetland ecosystems, and restoration, law, and climate change.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): BIO 001 or BIS 002B; or consent of instructor; EVE 101 or ESP 100 recommended.

title: Ecology of Waterfowl & Game Birds
description: Course Description: Detailed examination of distribution, behavior, population dynamics, and management of waterfowl and upland game birds.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): WFC 111; or consent of instructor. WFC 111L strongly recommended.

title: Applied Fisheries Conservation
description: Course Description: Fisheries, water, and natural resource management. Emphasis on freshwater, estuarine and anadromous fisheries in California.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): WFC 120; WFC 120L; or the equivalent.

title: Behavioral Ecology
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Basic theories underlying the functional and evolutionary significance of behavior, and the role of ecological constraints. Supporting empirical evidence taken mainly from studies of wild vertebrates.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): EVE 101 or ESP 100 (can be concurrent); (BIS 002A, BIS 002B, BIS 002C); or equivalent course.

title: Marine Conservation Science
description: Course Description: Key differences between marine and terrestrial ecosystems, major stressors of marine ecosystems (e.g., fisheries, pollution, bioinvasions, climate change and habitat destruction) and their consequences. Laws and agencies responsible for addressing problems, and the policies used.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Course in introductory ecology.

title: Urban Wildlife Ecology
description: Course Description: Introduction to the behavior, ecology, and evolution of wild animals in urban environments. Effects of urbanization on disease, fitness, and dynamics of animal populations. Conservation and conflict management efforts in urban settings.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): BIS 002A; BIS 002B; BIS 002C; or the equivalent.

title: Wildlife Ecology
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Ecology of wild vertebrates, including habitat selection, spatial organization, demography, population dynamics, competition, predation, herbivory, energetics, and community dynamics, set in the context of human-caused degradation of environments in North America.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): BIS 002B; or equivalent.

title: Ecology of Human-Wildlife Conflicts
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Ecological approaches to managing wild vertebrates that come into conflict with agriculture, public health, or the conservation of biodiversity.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): BIS 002B; or equivalent.

title: Wildlife Ecotoxicology
description: Course Description: Various forms of environmental pollution in relation to fish and wildlife, the effects and mechanisms of pollutants, effects on individuals and systems, laboratory and field ecotoxicology, examples/case histories, philosophical/management considerations.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Introductory courses in organic chemistry, ecology, and physiology, or consent of instructor; ETX 101 recommended.

title: Conservation Biology
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Introduction to conservation biology and background to the biological issues and controversies surrounding loss of species and habitats.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): BIS 002B; or the equivalent.

title: Wildlife Space Use & Habitat Conservation
description: Course Description: Relationships between habitat characteristics and wildlife behavior/ecology, principles of habitat conservation and management.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): EVE 101 or ESP 100; or the equivalent of EVE 101 or ESP 100.

title: Habitat Conservation & Restoration Laboratory
description: Course Description: Analysis of the characteristics of wildlife and fish habitats, application of restoration methods, and evaluation of conservation and restoration projects in the field. Participate during the term in a restoration project.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (EVE 101 or ESP 100); WFC 155 (can be concurrent); or the equivalent of ESP 100 or EVE 101.

title: Plant Geography
description: Course Description: Survey of the geographical distribution of vegetation types and habitats, with consideration of the environmental and historical factors that determine these patterns. Conservation and management approaches. Analytical field and lab techniques introduced.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ESP 100 or EVE 101; PLB 102 or PLB 108 strongly recommended.

title: Coastal Ecosystems
description: Course Description: Overview of coastal ecosystems, physical and biological elements and processes, and coastal zone dynamics, including sandy, rocky and muddy shorelines, estuaries, dunes and coastal watersheds. Discussion of the role of historical factors and conservation, restoration, and management approaches.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): EVE 101; course work in organismal biology, physical geography, and geology recommended.

title: Infectious Disease in Ecology & Conservation
description: Course Description: Introduction to the dynamics and control of infectious disease in wildlife, including zoonotic diseases and those threatening endangered species. Basic epidemiological models and their applications. Role of scientists in developing disease control policies.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): EVE 101 or ESP 100 or VET 409; or the equivalent.

title: Animal Coloration
description: Course Description: Evolutionary and ecological significance of coloration in mammals, birds, reptiles, amphibians, fish, cephalopods, crustaceans, spiders, insects, humans as well as color in fashion, plants and the military. Topics include history, protective coloration, warning coloration, mimicry, sexual dichromatism and color change.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): BIS 002A; BIS 002B; BIS 002C.

title: Climate Change Ecology
description: This version has ended; see updated course, below.Course Description: Ecological responses to current and expected future climate change, across levels of biological organization from individuals to ecosystems.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): BIS 002B; (EVE 101 or ESP 100); or consent of instructor.

title: Departmental Research Seminar
description: Course Description: Reports and discussions of recent advances related to wildlife and fisheries biology.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Upper division standing in the Biological Sciences.

title: Museum Science
description: Course Description: Principles and methods required to preserve and present biological specimens for research, teaching collections, and museums.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor. Upper division standing.

title: Internship
description: Course Description: Work experience off and on campus in all subject areas offered in the department. Internships supervised by a member of the faculty.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor. Completion of 84 units.

title: Field & Laboratory Research
description: Course Description: Critique and practice of research methods applied to field and/or laboratory environments of wild vertebrates. Work independently or in small groups to design experimental protocol, analyze data, and report their findings.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (WFC 110L or WFC 111L or WFC 120L); (WFC 121 or WFC 130); EVE 101; or the equivalent of EVE 101, and consent of instructor.

title: Tutoring in Wildlife & Fisheries
description: Course Description: Experience in teaching under guidance of faculty member.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor. Major in Wildlife, Fish, and Conservation Biology.

title: Directed Group Study
description: Course Description: Directed group study.

title: Special Study for Advanced Undergraduates
description: Course Description: Special study for advanced undergraduates.

title: Conservation Biology & Animal Behavior
description: Course Description: Influences of concepts of animal behavior (functional, evolutionary, developmental, mechanistic, and methodological issues) on conservation biology theory and practice.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): ECL 208 or ANB 221; and consent of instructor.

title: Advanced Physiological Ecology of Wildlife
description: Course Description: Advanced principles of physiological ecology. Ecological, evolutionary and behavioral perspectives on physiological mechanisms used by animals to adapt to their environment in the context of climate change and other threats to biodiversity. Primary literature will form the basis of discussion.

title: Advanced Population Dynamics
description: Course Description: Logical basis for population models, evaluation of simple ecological models, current population models with age, size, and stage structure, theoretical basis for management and exemplary case histories. Emphasis on development and use of realistic population models in ecological research.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing; advanced course in ecology (e.g., EVE 101), population dynamics (e.g., WFC 122), and one year of calculus; familiarity with matrix algebra and partial differential equations recommended.

title: Seminar
description: Course Description: Seminar devoted to a highly specific research topic in any area of wildlife or fisheries biology. Special topic selected for a quarter will vary depending on interests of instructor and students.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Research Group Conference
description: Course Description: Weekly conference on research problems, progress and techniques in wildlife and fishery sciences.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Seminar in Aquatic Ecology
description: Course Description: Presentation and analysis of assigned topics in aquatic ecology emphasizing fish, fisheries and aquatic conservation.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing in Biology.

title: Physiology of Fishes Seminar
description: Course Description: Seminar devoted to current topics concerning the physiological functioning of fishes.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor. Graduate standing and at least two courses in physiology.

title: Seminar in Behavioral Ecology of Predators & Prey
description: Course Description: Presentation and analysis of research papers on social and foraging behavior of predatory animals, antipredator strategies of prey species, co-evolution of predators and prey, and ecology of predator prey interactions.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing.

title: Seminar in Wildlife Ecotoxicology
description: Course Description: Presentation and analysis of assigned and searched research papers on transport, exposure, and effects of environmental contaminants on wildlife-associated ecosystem components, especially at individual/population levels. Specific subjects vary each offering.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing in Biology.

title: Supervised Teaching in Wildlife & Fisheries Biology
description: Course Description: Tutoring and teaching students in undergraduate courses in Wildlife, Fish, & Conservation Biology. Weekly conferences with instructor; evaluations of teaching; preparing for and conducting demonstrations, laboratories, and discussions; preparing and grading examinations.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor. Meet qualifications for teaching assistant; graduate standing.

description: Course Description: Group study.

title: Cultural Representations of Gender
description: Course Description: Interdisciplinary investigation of how specific cultures represent gender difference. Examine a variety of cultural forms and phenomena including film, television, literature, music, popular movements, and institutions.

title: Gender & Global Cinema
description: Course Description: Role gender plays in film history/culture in various geographical contexts and in aspects of contemporary globalization. Films from nations such as China, Colombia, Cuba, Ethiopia, India, Iran, Korea, New Zealand, and the U.S.

title: Feminist Critiques of Western Thought
description: Course Description: Critical introduction to major traditions of social thinking in the West from a feminist perspective.

title: Feminist Research
description: Course Description: Introduction to feminist applications and transformations of traditional disciplinary research practices; initial training in methodologies for feminist interdisciplinary work.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Required for Women's Studies major.

title: Critical Food Studies
description: Course Description: Production and consumption of food at the intersections of gender, race, ethnicity, nation, and body. Individual and familial experiences as part of larger economic and political structures in the U.S. and globally.

title: Contemporary Debates in Western Feminist Theory
description: Course Description: Interpretations of poststructuralist, postmodern, and postcolonial thought from a critical feminist perspective; includes methods of applying theory to concrete social/cultural problems of gender, race, sexuality, class.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): WMS 060; or consent of instructor.

title: Critical Fashion Studies
description: Course Description: Feminist cultural studies of style-fashion-dress through transnational circuits, personal subjectivities. Fashion as means of gender oppression and liberation. Histories and discourses of masculinities and femininities. Clothing works on global assembly line. Use of dress in construction/regulation of identities.

title: Gender & Law
description: Course Description: Exploration of womens legal rights in historical and contemporary context, discussing a variety of legal issues and applicable feminist theories. Topics include constitutional equal protection, discrimination in employment and education, sexual orientation discrimination, and the regulation of abortion.

title: Women's Movements in Transnational Perspective
description: Course Description: Transnational perspectives on 20th- and 21st-century women’s movements in Western, colonial and post-colonial contexts, examining movement’s forms and political orientations and relationships between women’s movements and other forces for change.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): WMS 050 recommended.

title: Gender, War & Peace
description: Course Description: Applies a critical gender perspective to militarism as manifest in contexts of military rule, war, conflict, peacebuilding and security post-conflict. Addresses the changing configurations of gender and sexuality in military institutions and militarized economies and cultures from an interdisciplinary perspective.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Consent of instructor.

title: Masculinities
description: Course Description: Cultural, economic, and political forces which shape historical and contemporary masculinities. Impact of race, class, ability, nation and sexuality on experiences and cultural representations of masculinity.

title: Women, "Race" & Sexuality in Postcolonial Cinema
description: Course Description: Feminist analysis of race, sexuality and class in the representation of women in commercial and/or independent films.

title: Feminist Film Theory & Criticism
description: Course Description: Historical overview of and contemporary issues in feminist film theory, including representation, spectatorship, and cultural production. Film stars, women filmmakers, and the intersections of gender, race, sexuality, and class in films and their audiences.

title: Topics in Gender & Cinematic Representation
description: Course Description: Examination of a specific topic within the broad rubric of gender and cinema. Possible topics include Latinas in Hollywood; gender, nation, cinema; and gender and film genre. Topics vary.

title: Feminist Media Production
description: Course Description: Media production as a mode of cultural criticism, furthering feminist/ social justice activist goals. Fundamentals of camera, editing and distribution via a social engagement model. Study and hands-on response to key historic and contemporary feminist and social justice media discourses.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): (CTS 020 or CDM 020); or two WMS courses.

title: Queer Studies
description: Course Description: Study of queer sexualities, identities, theories, practices. Alternative sexualities as historical, social, and cultural constructions in intersections with race, gender, class, nationality. Interdisciplinary exploration of sexual liberation and the regulation of sexuality through history, theory and expressive cultural forms.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): WMS 070 recommended or consent of instructor.

title: Gender & Experience of Race
description: Course Description: Exploration of the co-construction of “race” and gender in comparative national historical contexts and contemporary lived experience. Study of intersections of race and gender in identities and how institutions, labor migration, social movements and consumption shape racialized gendered identities.

title: Women of Color Writing in the United States
description: Course Description: Literature, especially novels, written by contemporary women of color in the United States, understood in their socio-economic, cultural and historical contexts.

title: Globalization, Gender & Culture
description: Course Description: Critical gender analysis of globalization as a process of interconnected cultural, social and economic transformations inflected by gender, nation, class and race/ethnicity. Critical self-reflection and social observation skills.

title: Gender in the Arab World
description: Course Description: Examination of the history, culture, and social/political/economic dynamics of gender relations and gendering in the Arab world.

title: Gender & Public Policy
description: Course Description: Role of gender in the creation of social policies, especially with respect to issues brought into the policy arena by contemporary feminism.

title: Special Topics in Critical Gender Studies
description: Course Description: In-depth examination of a women's studies topic related to the research interests of the instructor.

title: Cultural Study of Masculinities
description: Course Description: Interdisciplinary approaches to understanding the social and cultural construction of masculinities; attention to the effects of biology, gender, race, class, sexual and national identities; criticism of oral, printed, visual, and mass mediated texts, and of social relations and structures.
prerequisites: Prerequisite(s): Graduate standing or consent of instructor.
